Questions whether intellectual understanding (parijñāna) alone is sufficient to achieve yoga. Pupil: "Is that yoga attained from mere complete knowledge alone?"
ucyate: [na] • ucyate = it is said (3rd sg. pres. pass.)
• na = no 		It is said (ucyate): No (na). Negates the idea that knowledge alone suffices. [Answer:] No.
yasmād āha: tatprāptau vidhiṃ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ • yasmāt = because
• āha = he says (3rd sg. perf.)
• tat-prāptau = for attaining that (loc. sg.)
• vidhim = method (acc. sg.)
• vyākhyāsyāmaḥ = we shall explain (1st pl. fut.) 		Because (yasmāt) he says (āha): "We shall explain (vyākhyāsyāmaḥ) the method (vidhim) for attaining (prāptau) that (tat)." Cites the sūtra itself as evidence: the text promises to explain a vidhi (method/practice), proving that knowledge alone is insufficient. Because [the Sūtra] says: "For attaining that [yoga], we shall explain the method (vidhi)."
SECTION 16: GRAMMATICAL AND TECHNICAL ANALYSIS OF VIDHI
atra yogasya vidhiḥ yogavidhir iti ṣaṣṭhītatpuruṣasamāsaḥ • atra = here
• yogasya = of yoga (gen. sg.)
• vidhiḥ = method (nom. sg.)
• yoga-vidhiḥ = yoga-method (nom. sg.)
• iti = thus
• ṣaṣṭhī-tatpuruṣa-samāsaḥ = genitive tatpuruṣa compound (nom. sg.) 		Here (atra), "the method (vidhiḥ) of yoga (yogasya)" (yogavidhi) is (iti) [explained as] a genitive (ṣaṣṭhī) tatpuruṣa compound (tatpuruṣasamāsaḥ). Grammatical analysis: "yogavidhi" is a ṣaṣṭhītatpuruṣa (genitive determinative compound) meaning "method belonging to yoga" or "method for [attaining] yoga." Here "the method of yoga" (yogavidhi) is [to be analyzed as] a genitive tatpuruṣa compound.
atra sūkṣmasthūlasabahyābhyantarasalakṣaṇavilakṣaṇakriyāsu vidhisaṃjñā • atra = here
• sūkṣma-sthūla-sabahya-ābhyantara-sa-lakṣaṇa-vilakṣaṇa-kriyāsu = in actions that are subtle, gross, external, internal, characterized, uncharacterized (loc. pl.)
• vidhi-saṃjñā = the designation "method" (nom. sg.) 		Here (atra), the designation (saṃjñā) "method" (vidhi) [refers to] actions (kriyāsu) that are subtle (sūkṣma), gross (sthūla), external (bahya), internal (ābhyantara), characterized (salakṣaṇa) and uncharacterized (vilakṣaṇa). Defines the scope of vidhi: it encompasses all types of practices - subtle/gross (sūkṣma/sthūla), external/internal (bahya/ābhyantara), with/without observable characteristics (salakṣaṇa/vilakṣaṇa). Here, in actions that are subtle, gross, external, internal, with characteristics and without characteristics, is the designation "method."
yajñavidhivan, na tu senāvanādivat • yajña-vidhi-vat = like the method of sacrifice
• na tu = but not
• senā-vana-ādi-vat = like army, forest, etc. 		Like (vat) the method (vidhi) of sacrifice (yajña), not (na tu) like (vat) [mere collections such as] army (senā), forest (vana), and so on (ādi). Distinguishes vidhi from mere aggregates: "vidhi" denotes an organized, purposeful system (like yajñavidhi - sacrificial procedure) not a random collection (like senā/vana - army/forest). Like the method of sacrifice (yajñavidhi), not like [mere collections such as] army, forest, and so on.
kasmāt • kasmāt = why Why (kasmāt)? Prompts explanation of the distinction. Why?
kriyāṇāṃ kṣaṇikānāṃ samudayasambhavāt • kriyāṇām = of actions (gen. pl.)
• kṣaṇikānām = momentary (gen. pl.)
• samudaya-sambhavāt = from the possibility of aggregation (abl. sg.) 		Because of (sambhavāt) the possibility of aggregating (samudaya) momentary (kṣaṇikānām) actions (kriyāṇām). Actions (kriyā) are momentary (kṣaṇika), but they can form an aggregate (samudaya) - a coherent sequence rather than random collection. Because of the possibility of aggregating momentary actions.
yady evaṃ vidhiḥ kasmāt • yadi = if
• evam = so
• vidhiḥ = method (nom. sg.)
• kasmāt = why 		If (yadi) so (evam), why (kasmāt) [is it called] "method" (vidhiḥ)? If it's just an aggregate of actions, why use the special term "vidhi"? If so, why [is it called] "method" (vidhi)?
vidhāyakatvāt vidhiḥ • vidhāyaka-tvāt = from being an establisher (abl. sg.)
• vidhiḥ = method (nom. sg.) 		[It is called] "method" (vidhiḥ) because it establishes (vidhāyakatvāt). Etymology of vidhi: from vidhāyaka (that which establishes/institutes) - it actively brings about or establishes the goal. [It is called] "method" because it establishes (vidhāyaka).
upāyopeyabhāvāc ca • upāya-upeya-bhāvāt = from the relationship of means and end (abl. sg.)
• ca = and 		And (ca) because of (bhāvāt) the relationship of means (upāya) and end (upeya). Vidhi has the upāya-upeya relationship: it is the means (upāya) to an end (upeya), establishing purposeful teleology. And because of the state of being means (upāya) and end (upeya).
vidhim iti karma • vidhim = method (acc. sg.)
• iti = thus
• karma = object [grammatical] (nom. sg.) 		"Method" (vidhim) [in the phrase] (iti) is the [grammatical] object (karma) [of the verb "we shall explain"]. Final grammatical note: in the phrase "vidhiṃ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ" (we shall explain the method), vidhim functions as the karma (direct object) of the verb. "Method" (vidhim) [in the phrase] is the object [of the verb "we shall explain"].
evaṃ sa duḥkhāntaḥ kāryaṃ karaṇaṃ yogo vidhir iti pañcaiva padārthāḥ samāsata uddiṣṭāḥ • evam = thus
• saḥ = that (nom. sg.)
• duḥkha-antaḥ = end of suffering (nom. sg.)
• kāryam = body (nom. sg.)
• karaṇam = senses (nom. sg.)
• yogaḥ = union (nom. sg.)
• vidhiḥ = method (nom. sg.)
• iti = thus
• pañca = five (num.)
• eva = indeed
• padārthāḥ = categories (nom. pl.)
• samāsataḥ = in summary (adv.)
• uddiṣṭāḥ = have been indicated (past participle, nom. pl.) 		Thus (evam) these (iti) five (pañca) (eva) categories (padārthāḥ) - that (saḥ) end (antaḥ) of suffering (duḥkha), body (kāryam), senses (karaṇam), union (yogaḥ), and method (vidhiḥ) - have been indicated (uddiṣṭāḥ) in summary (samāsataḥ). Summarizes the five fundamental categories (padārtha) enumerated in the teaching: (1) duḥkhānta - cessation of suffering/liberation, (2) kārya - the physical body, (3) karaṇa - the sense organs, (4) yoga - union with the Lord, (5) vidhi - the method/practice. These are presented briefly (samāsataḥ) before detailed explanation. Thus, these five categories have been indicated in summary: that end of suffering, effect, instrument, union, and method.
te vyākhyeyāḥ • te = those (nom. pl.)
• vyākhyeyāḥ = are to be explained (gerundive, nom. pl.) 		Those (te) are to be explained (vyākhyeyāḥ). States the necessity of detailed exposition - the five categories require full explanation (vyākhyā). These are to be explained.
vyākhyānam eteṣāṃ vistaravibhāgaviśeṣopasaṃhāranigamanāni • vyākhyānam = explanation (nom. sg.)
• eteṣām = of these (gen. pl.)
• vistara = detailed description
• vibhāga = analysis/division
• viśeṣa = differentiation/particularity
• upasaṃhāra = synthesis/summary
• nigamana = conclusion
• -āni = plural neuter ending 		The explanation (vyākhyānam) of these (eteṣām) [involves] detailed description (vistara), analysis (vibhāga), differentiation (viśeṣa), synthesis (upasaṃhāra), and conclusion (nigamanāni). Outlines the five-fold methodology of explanation (vyākhyāna): (1) vistara - extensive elaboration, (2) vibhāga - analytical division, (3) viśeṣa - specific differentiation, (4) upasaṃhāra - synthetic summation, (5) nigamana - conclusive determination. This establishes the hermeneutical framework. Their explanation (vyākhyāna) [involves] detailed description (vistara), analysis (vibhāga), differentiation (viśeṣa), synthesis (upasaṃhāra), and conclusion (nigamana).
tasmād anyad vyākhyeyam anyad vyākhyānam • tasmāt = therefore (abl.)
• anyad = one/different (nom. sg. neut.)
• vyākhyeyam = that which is to be explained (gerundive, nom. sg.)
• anyad = another/different (nom. sg. neut.)
• vyākhyānam = explanation (nom. sg.) 		Therefore (tasmāt), that which is to be explained (vyākhyeyam) is one thing (anyad), the explanation (vyākhyānam) is another (anyad). Establishes the distinction between object of explanation (vyākhyeya - the five categories) and the act of explaining (vyākhyāna - the methodology). Therefore, that which is to be explained (scil. the five categories) is one thing, the explanation (itself) another.
yasmād āha "vyākhyāsyāmaḥ" • yasmāt = because (conjunction)
• āha = he says (3rd sg. perf.)
• vyākhyāsyāmaḥ = we shall explain (1st pl. fut.) 		Because (yasmāt) he says (āha): "We shall explain (vyākhyāsyāmaḥ)." Cites the sūtra text as evidence for the promise of detailed explanation to come. Hence He says: "We shall explain" (vyākhyāsyāmaḥ).
atra vir vistare vibhāge viśeṣe ca bhavati • atra = here (adv.)
• viḥ = the prefix 'vi'
• vistare = in detailed description (loc. sg.)
• vibhāge = in analysis (loc. sg.)
• viśeṣe = in differentiation (loc. sg.)
• ca = and
• bhavati = occurs/is found (3rd sg. pres.) 		Here (atra), [the prefix] 'vi' (viḥ) occurs (bhavati) in detailed description (vistare), analysis (vibhāge), and (ca) differentiation (viśeṣe). Etymological analysis: the prefix vi- in vyākhyāsyāmaḥ (we shall explain) indicates three aspects of explanation - vistara, vibhāga, and viśeṣa - all sharing the intensifying prefix. [The preposition] 'vi' in the Sūtra (in vy-ā-khyāsyāmaḥ) points to detailed description, analysis, and differentiation.
tatra vistara iti pratyakṣānumānāptavacanaṃ iti pramāṇāny abhidhīyante • tatra = therein (adv.)
• vistaraḥ = detailed description (nom. sg.)
• iti = thus
• pratyakṣa = perception (nom. sg.)
• anumāna = inference (nom. sg.)
• āpta-vacanam = testimony/revelation (nom. sg.)
• iti = thus
• pramāṇāni = valid means of knowledge (nom. pl.)
• abhidhīyante = are designated (3rd pl. pres. pass.) 		Therein (tatra), by 'detailed description' (vistaraḥ) (iti) the valid means of knowledge (pramāṇāni) are designated (abhidhīyante), namely (iti): perception (pratyakṣa), inference (anumāna), and testimony (āptavacanam). Explains vistara (detailed description) as reference to the three pramāṇas (valid means of knowledge): pratyakṣa (direct perception), anumāna (inferential reasoning), āptavacana (authoritative testimony/scripture). By 'detailed description' here the valid ways of cognition (pramāṇa) are indicated, viz. perception, reasoning, and revelation.
tatra pratyakṣaṃ dvividham: indriyapratyakṣam ātmapratyakṣaṃ ca • tatra = therein (adv.)
• pratyakṣam = perception (nom. sg.)
• dvividham = twofold (nom. sg.)
• indriya-pratyakṣam = sensory perception (nom. sg.)
• ātma-pratyakṣam = inner/self-perception (nom. sg.)
• ca = and 		Therein (tatra), perception (pratyakṣam) is twofold (dvividham): sensory perception (indriyapratyakṣam) and (ca) inner perception (ātmapratyakṣam). Subdivides pratyakṣa into two types: (1) indriyapratyakṣa - perception through the external sense organs, (2) ātmapratyakṣa - direct inner perception/introspection of the self. Perception (pratyakṣa) here is of two kinds: sensory perception (indriya) and inner perception (ātman).
indriyapratyakṣaṃ vyākhyānatapomūtrapuriṣamāṃsalavaṇaprāṇayāmaiḥ siddham • indriya-pratyakṣam = sensory perception (nom. sg.)
• vyākhyāna = explanation (inst. sg.)
• tapas = ascetic practice (inst. sg.)
• mūtra-puriṣa = urine and feces (inst. pl.)
• māṃsa-lavaṇa = meat and salt (inst. pl.)
• prāṇa-yāma = breath control (inst. pl.)
• -aiḥ = instrumental ending
• siddham = is proven/established (nom. sg.) 		Sensory perception (indriyapratyakṣam) is proven (siddham) by [the sūtra references to] explanation (vyākhyāna), ascetic practice (tapaḥ), urine and feces (mūtrapuriṣa), meat and salt (māṃsalavaṇa), and breath control (prāṇayāmaiḥ). Establishes the reality of indriyapratyakṣa through scriptural citations: vyākhyāna (PS 1.1), tapas (PS 2.16), mūtra-puriṣa (PS 1.12 - bodily excretions perceived), māṃsa-lavaṇa (PS 5.16 - food items perceived), prāṇāyāma (PS 1.16 - breath perceived and controlled). [The reality of] sensory perception is proven (siddha) by 'explanation' (PS 1.1), 'ascetic practice' (PS 2.16), 'urine and feces' (PS 1.12), 'meat and salt' (PS 5.16) and 'breath control' (PS 1.16).
ātmapratyakṣaṃ tadupahārakṛtsnatapoduhkhāntādivacanāt siddham • ātma-pratyakṣam = inner perception (nom. sg.)
• tad-upahāra = offering to Him (compound)
• kṛtsna-tapas = complete asceticism (compound)
• duḥkha-anta = end of suffering (compound)
• ādi = etc.
• vacanāt = from the statement (abl. sg.)
• siddham = is proven (nom. sg.) 		Inner perception (ātmapratyakṣam) is proven (siddham) from the statements (vacanāt) about offering to Him (tadupahāra), complete asceticism (kṛtsnatapas), end (anta) of suffering (duḥkha), etc. (ādi). Establishes ātmapratyakṣa through sūtra references to inner states: tadupahāra (PS 1.8 - offering to the Lord, requires inner awareness), kṛtsnatapas (PS 3.19 - complete ascetic focus), duḥkhānta (PS 5.40 - recognition of suffering's end). These require introspective self-perception. [The reality of] inner perception is proven by the Sūtra sayings: 'offering' to Him (PS 1.8), 'complete asceticism' (PS 3.19), 'the end to sufferings' (PS 5.40) etc.
yathā prasthena mito vrīhiḥ prasthaḥ • yathā = just as (adv.)
• prasthena = by a prastha/measure (inst. sg.)
• mitaḥ = measured (past participle, nom. sg.)
• vrīhiḥ = rice (nom. sg.)
• prasthaḥ = is a prastha/pound (nom. sg.) 		Just as (yathā) rice (vrīhiḥ) measured (mitaḥ) by the pound (prasthena) [measure] is [called] a pound (prasthaḥ). Analogy: just as rice takes its measure-name from the measuring instrument (prastha), so perception is validated by what it measures/reveals. The pramāṇa (means of knowledge) establishes the prameya (object of knowledge). Just as rice measured by the pound establishes a pound.
paramārthatas tv [indriyapratyakṣam] indriyārthasambhandhavyañjakasāmagryaṃ dharmādharmaprakāśadeśakālacodanādyanugṛhītaṃ sat pramāṇam utpadyate • paramārthataḥ = ultimately/in reality (adv.)
• tu = but/however
• indriya-artha-sambandha = connection between sense and object
• vyañjaka = manifesting (nom. sg.)
• sāmagryam = aggregate (nom. sg.)
• dharma-adharma = merit and demerit
• prakāśa = light
• deśa = space
• kāla = time
• codana = stimulus
• ādi = etc.
• anugṛhītam = sustained/supported (past participle)
• sat = being (present participle)
• pramāṇam = valid means (nom. sg.)
• utpadyate = arises (3rd sg. pres. middle) 		But (tu) ultimately (paramārthataḥ), [sensory perception] is an aggregate (sāmagryam) that manifests (vyañjaka) the connection (sambandha) between sense organ (indriya) and object (artha), and when sustained (anugṛhītam) (sat) by dharma (dharma), adharma (adharma), light (prakāśa), space (deśa), time (kāla), stimulus (codanā), etc. (ādi), it arises (utpadyate) as a valid means of knowledge (pramāṇam). Technical definition of indriyapratyakṣa: it is a sāmagrī (aggregate/complex) that vyañjaka (manifests) the indriyārtha-sambandha (contact between sense faculty and sense object). This perception functions as valid pramāṇa only when supported (anugṛhīta) by auxiliary conditions: dharma/adharma (karmic merit/demerit), prakāśa (illumination), deśa (spatial proximity), kāla (temporal simultaneity), codanā (attentional stimulus), etc. The true meaning, however, is that sensory perception is an aggregate that makes manifest the connection between sense organ and object and operates properly as a valid way of cognition when it is sustained (anugṛhīta) by dharma and adharma, light, space, time, stimuli (codanā), etc.
ātmapratyakṣaṃ tu cittātmāntaḥkaraṇasambandhasāmagryam • ātma-pratyakṣam = inner perception (nom. sg.)
• tu = but/however
• citta-ātman = conscious self/subject
• antaḥkaraṇa = inner organ/mind
• sambandha = connection
• sāmagryam = aggregate (nom. sg.) 		But (tu) inner perception (ātmapratyakṣam) is an aggregate (sāmagryam) [that manifests] the connection (sambandha) [between] the conscious self (cittātman) and the inner organ (antaḥkaraṇa). Technical definition of ātmapratyakṣa: unlike external perception (sense-object contact), inner perception is a sāmagrī manifesting cittātman-antaḥkaraṇa-sambandha - the connection between the conscious subject (cittātman) and the internal mental organ (antaḥkaraṇa/manas). This enables introspection and self-awareness. Inner perception on the other hand is an aggregate that connects the subject (cittātman) and the inner organ (i.e. mind).
anumānam api pratyakṣapūrvakaṃ cittātmāntaḥkaraṇasambandhasāmagryaṃ ca dharmādharmaprakāśadeśakālacodanādi- • anumānam = inference (nom. sg.)
• api = also
• pratyakṣa-pūrvakam = preceded by perception (nom. sg.)
• citta-ātman = conscious subject
• antaḥkaraṇa = inner organ
• sambandha = connection
• sāmagryam = aggregate (nom. sg.)
• ca = and
• dharma-adharma-prakāśa-deśa-kāla-codana-ādi = dharma, adharma, light, space, time, stimulus, etc. (compound, incomplete) 		Inference (anumānam) also (api), preceded (pūrvakam) by perception (pratyakṣa), is an aggregate (sāmagryam) and (ca) [manifesting] the connection (sambandha) [between] the conscious self (cittātman) and the inner organ (antaḥkaraṇa), and [sustained by] dharma (dharma), adharma (adharma), light (prakāśa), space (deśa), time (kāla), stimulus (codanā), etc. (ādi)... Technical definition of anumāna (inference): (1) it is pratyakṣapūrvaka - dependent upon and following from prior perception, (2) like inner perception, it operates through cittātman-antaḥkaraṇa connection, (3) it requires the same auxiliary conditions as sensory perception (dharma/adharma, light, space, time, stimuli, etc.). [Sentence appears incomplete in source text.] Reasoning (anumāna) then—preceded by perception and being also an aggregate that connects the subject and the inner organ—is prompted by the recollection (smṛti) [sustained by] dharma and adharma, light, space, time, stimuli, etc.

THE FIRST TWELVE SŪTRAS OF PĀŚUPATASŪTRA CHAPTER 1

Complete Sūtra Text with Word-by-Word Analysis

Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Interpretative Meaning Reference
PS 1.1 - THE OPENING APHORISM
athātaḥ paśupateḥ pāśupataṃ yogavidhiṃ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ • atha = certainly, now (particle)
• ataḥ = therefore, because of this (particle)
• paśu-pateḥ = of Paśupati, Lord of Souls (gen. sg. m.)
• pāśupatam = Pāśupata, relating to Paśupati (acc. sg. n.)
• yoga-vidhim = praxis/method of union (acc. sg. m.)
• vi-ā-khyāsyāmaḥ = we shall explain (1st pl. fut. from vi-ā-√khyā) 		Certainly (atha), therefore (ataḥ), [regarding] Paśupati (paśupateḥ), we shall explain (vyākhyāsyāmaḥ) the Pāśupata (pāśupatam) praxis (vidhim) of union (yoga). The foundational sūtra establishing the text's purpose and authority. Paśupati = Lord of bound souls. Yoga-vidhi = systematic method of union with the divine. Now therefore, we shall explain the Pāśupata yoga-method (yoga-vidhi) of Paśupati, the Lord of Bound Souls. Key terms: atha (auspicious beginning particle), ataḥ (therefore, from this), paśupati (lord of paśus/bound souls), pāśupata (relating to Paśupati), yoga-vidhi (systematic method of union), vyākhyāsyāmaḥ (we shall expound).
PS 1.2 - TRIPLE ASH BATHING
bhasmanā triṣavaṇaṃ snāyīta • bhasmanā = with ashes (instr. sg. n.)
• tri-ṣavaṇam = three times (acc. sg. n.)
• snāyīta = one should bathe (3rd sg. opt. from √snā) 		With ashes (bhasmanā) three times (triṣavaṇam) one should bathe (snāyīta). Prescribes the fundamental purificatory practice of bathing with sacred ashes (bhasma) three times daily. Triṣavaṇa = morning, noon, and evening ablutions. One should bathe (snāyīta) with sacred ashes (bhasma) three times daily (triṣavaṇa) - at morning, noon, and evening. This ash-bath (bhasma-snāna) is the fundamental purificatory practice of the Pāśupata. Key terms: bhasma (sacred ash from fire sacrifice), triṣavaṇam (the three savanas/pressings = dawn, midday, dusk), snāyīta (optative from √snā 'to bathe').
PS 1.3 - LYING IN ASHES
bhasmani śayīta • bhasmani = in ashes (loc. sg. n.)
• śayīta = one should lie down (3rd sg. opt. from √śī) 		In ashes (bhasmani) one should lie down (śayīta). Prescribes lying or sleeping in ashes as a form of ascetic practice and identification with Śiva who is smeared with ashes. One should lie down (śayīta) in ashes (bhasmani). This practice represents identification with Śiva who is smeared with cremation ashes. Key terms: bhasmani (locative of bhasman), śayīta (optative from √śī 'to lie down, sleep').
PS 1.4 - POST-BATHING ABLUTIONS
anusnānam • anu-snānam = post-bathing, ablution after (nom. sg. n.) Post-bathing (anusnānam) [rituals]. Refers to ritual ablutions performed after the main ash-bath. Anu-snāna = secondary or follow-up bathing practices. Then post-bathing ablutions (anusnāna). Secondary ritual washing performed after the main ash-bath. Key terms: anu-snānam (follow-up bathing, from anu 'after' + snāna 'bath').
PS 1.5 - WEARING GARLANDS
nirmālyam • nirmālyam = garland, floral offering (nom. sg. n.) Garlands (nirmālyam) [should be worn]. Prescribes wearing garlands or flowers, particularly those offered to Śiva. Nirmālya can also refer to flowers already offered to the deity. One should wear garlands (nirmālya). Particularly flowers that have been offered to Śiva. Key terms: nirmālyam (garland, floral offering; can refer to prasāda flowers from deity worship).
PS 1.6 - BEARING THE LIṄGA
liṅgadhārī • liṅga-dhārī = bearer of the liṅga (nom. sg. m.) [One should be] a bearer (dhārī) of the liṅga (liṅga). Prescribes carrying or wearing the liṅga symbol of Śiva. This could refer to a physical liṅga emblem or the symbolic mark on the body. One should be a bearer of the liṅga (liṅga-dhārī). This means carrying or wearing the sacred symbol of Śiva on one's person. Key terms: liṅga (the phallic emblem of Śiva), dhārī (bearer, wearer, from √dhṛ 'to hold').
PS 1.7 - DWELLING IN A SANCTUARY
āyatanavāsī • āyatana-vāsī = dweller in a sanctuary (nom. sg. m.) [One should be] a dweller (vāsī) in a sanctuary (āyatana). Prescribes residence in a temple or sacred precinct (āyatana). The practitioner should dwell in proximity to Śiva's sanctuary. One should be a dweller in a sanctuary (āyatana-vāsī). The practitioner should reside in or near a temple of Śiva. Key terms: āyatana (sanctuary, temple, sacred precinct), vāsī (dweller, resident, from √vas 'to dwell').
PS 1.8 - THE SEVEN MODES OF WORSHIP
hasitagītanṛttaḍuṃḍuṃkāranamaskārajapyopahāreṇopatiṣṭhet • hasita = laughter (nom. sg. n.)
• gīta = song (nom. sg. n.)
• nṛtta = dance (nom. sg. n.)
• ḍuṃḍuṃkāra = huḍuṃkāra sounds (nom. sg. m.)
• namaskāra = prostration (nom. sg. m.)
• japya = muttering, recitation (nom. sg. n.)
• upahāreṇa = through offering (instr. sg. m.)
• upa-tiṣṭhet = one should worship (3rd sg. opt. from upa-√sthā) 		Through laughter (hasita), song (gīta), dance (nṛtta), huḍuṃkāra sounds (ḍuṃḍuṃkāra), prostration (namaskāra), muttering (japya), and offering (upahāreṇa) one should worship (upatiṣṭhet). Prescribes seven modes of worship combining physical (dance, prostration), vocal (laughter, song, sacred sounds, mantra recitation), and ritual (offerings) elements. Ḍuṃḍuṃkāra = the sacred syllable "huṃ." One should worship (upatiṣṭhet) through seven modes: laughter (hasita), song (gīta), dance (nṛtta), huḍukkāra sounds (ḍuṃḍuṃkāra), prostration (namaskāra), muttering/japa (japya), and offering (upahāra). Key terms: hasita (laughing), gīta (singing), nṛtta (dancing), ḍuṃḍuṃkāra (sacred syllable sounds like 'huṃ'), namaskāra (bowing down), japya (mantra recitation), upahāra (ritual offering), upatiṣṭhet (optative from upa-√sthā 'to worship, attend upon').
PS 1.9 - THE SOUTHERN IMAGE
mahādevasya dakṣiṇāmūrteḥ • mahā-devasya = of Mahādeva (gen. sg. m.)
• dakṣiṇā-mūrteḥ = of the southern image/form (gen. sg. f.) 		[Worship] of Mahādeva (mahādevasya) the southern (dakṣiṇā) image (mūrteḥ). Specifies that worship should be directed toward the dakṣiṇāmūrti - the southern-facing form of Śiva as the supreme teacher, or the south-facing image in the temple. One should worship the southern image (dakṣiṇā-mūrti) of Mahādeva. This refers to the south-facing form of Śiva as the supreme teacher. Key terms: mahā-deva (Great God = Śiva), dakṣiṇā (southern), mūrti (image, form, embodiment).
PS 1.10 - SINGLE GARMENT
ekavāsāḥ • eka-vāsāḥ = [wearing] a single garment (nom. sg. m.) [One should wear] a single (eka) garment (vāsāḥ). Prescribes wearing only one cloth as a mark of ascetic simplicity and renunciation. One should wear a single garment (eka-vāsas). A mark of ascetic simplicity and renunciation of worldly concerns. Key terms: eka (one, single), vāsas (garment, cloth).
PS 1.11 - OR NO GARMENT
avāsā vā • avāsāḥ = without garments, naked (nom. sg. m.)
• vā = or (particle) 		Or (vā) without garments (avāsāḥ). Provides an alternative: complete nakedness. This represents a higher level of renunciation and identification with Śiva as Digambara (sky-clad). Or (vā) one may be without garments (avāsas), i.e., naked. This represents a higher level of renunciation, identifying with Śiva as Digambara (sky-clad). Key terms: avāsas (without clothes, naked), vā (or, alternative particle).
PS 1.12 - NOT LOOKING AT BODILY WASTES
mūtrapurīṣaṃ nāvekṣet • mūtra-purīṣam = urine and feces (acc. sg. n.)
• na = not (negative particle)
• avekṣet = one should look at (3rd sg. opt. from ava-√īkṣ) 		Urine and feces (mūtrapurīṣam) not (na) one should look at (avekṣet). Prescribes not looking at one's own bodily excretions as a practice of detachment from the physical body and its functions. Indicates transcendence of bodily identification. One should not look at (na avekṣet) urine and feces (mūtra-purīṣa). This practice cultivates detachment from the physical body and transcendence of bodily identification. Key terms: mūtra (urine), purīṣa (feces), na (not), avekṣet (optative from ava-√īkṣ 'to look at, observe').

Complete Document - All Sentences Analyzed with Introductory Section and First Twelve Sūtras

Pāśupatasūtra Adhyāya 1 (34 Sūtras)

Sūtrapāṭha edition by Peter Bisschop (2006)

Sanskrit Text Translation Dictionary
1.1 athātaḥ paśupateḥ pāśupataṃ yogavidhim vyākhyāsyāmaḥ Now (atha) therefore (ataḥ), we shall explain (vyākhyāsyāmaḥ) the Pāśupata (pāśupatam) yoga-method (yogavidhim) of Paśupati (paśupateḥ), the Lord of Bound Souls. atha = now, certainly (particle)
ataḥ = therefore (particle)
paśupateḥ = of Paśupati (gen. sg. m.)
pāśupatam = Pāśupata (acc. sg. n.)
yoga-vidhim = method of union (acc. sg. m.)
vyākhyāsyāmaḥ = we shall explain (1st pl. fut. from vi-ā-√khyā)
1.2 bhasmanā triṣavaṇaṃ snāyīta One should bathe (snāyīta) with ashes (bhasmanā) three times daily (triṣavaṇam). bhasmanā = with ashes (inst. sg. n.)
triṣavaṇam = three times daily (acc. sg. n.)
snāyīta = one should bathe (3rd sg. opt. from √snā)
1.3 bhasmani śayīta One should lie down (śayīta) in ashes (bhasmani). bhasmani = in ashes (loc. sg. n.)
śayīta = one should lie down (3rd sg. opt. from √śī)
1.4 anusnānam [Then] post-bathing ablutions (anusnānam). anu-snānam = post-bathing ablution (nom. sg. n.)
1.5 nirmālyam [One should wear] garlands (nirmālyam). nirmālyam = garland, floral offering (nom. sg. n.)
1.6 liṅgadhārī [One should be] a bearer of the liṅga (liṅgadhārī). liṅga-dhārī = bearer of the liṅga (nom. sg. m.)
1.7 āyatanavāsī [One should be] a dweller in a sanctuary (āyatanavāsī). āyatana-vāsī = dweller in a sanctuary (nom. sg. m.)
1.8 hasitagītanṛtyahuḍuṃkāranamaskārajapyopahāreṇopatiṣṭhet One should worship (upatiṣṭhet) through laughter (hasita), song (gīta), dance (nṛtya), huḍuṃkāra sounds (huḍuṃkāra), prostration (namaskāra), muttering (japya), and offering (upahāreṇa). hasita = laughter
gīta = song
nṛtya = dance
huḍuṃkāra = huḍuk sounds
namaskāra = prostration
japya = muttering
upahāreṇa = through offering (inst.)
upatiṣṭhet = one should worship (3rd sg. opt. from upa-√sthā)
1.9 mahādevasya dakṣiṇāmūrtim [One should worship] the southern image (dakṣiṇāmūrtim) of Mahādeva (mahādevasya). mahādevasya = of Mahādeva (gen. sg. m.)
dakṣiṇā-mūrtim = southern image (acc. sg. f.)
1.10 ekavāsāḥ [One should wear] a single garment (ekavāsāḥ). eka-vāsāḥ = wearing a single garment (nom. sg. m.)
1.11 avāsā vā Or (vā) [one may be] without garments (avāsāḥ). avāsāḥ = without garments (nom. sg. m.)
vā = or (particle)
1.12 mūtrapurīṣaṃ nāvekṣet One should not (na) look at (avekṣet) urine and feces (mūtrapurīṣam). mūtra-purīṣam = urine and feces (acc. sg. n.)
na = not
avekṣet = one should look at (3rd sg. opt. from ava-√īkṣ)
1.13 strīśūdraṃ nābhibhāṣet One should not (na) address (abhibhāṣet) a woman or a śūdra (strīśūdram). strī-śūdram = woman and śūdra (acc. sg.)
na = not
abhibhāṣet = one should address (3rd sg. opt. from abhi-√bhāṣ)
1.14 yady avekṣed yady abhibhāṣet If (yadi) one should look at (avekṣet) [them], if (yadi) one should address (abhibhāṣet) [them]... yadi = if
avekṣet = one should look at (3rd sg. opt. from ava-√īkṣ)
yadi = if
abhibhāṣet = one should address (3rd sg. opt. from abhi-√bhāṣ)
1.15 upaspṛśya ...having touched water (upaspṛśya) [for purification]. upaspṛśya = having touched water (abs. from upa-√spṛś)
1.16 prāṇāyāmaṃ kṛtvā Having performed (kṛtvā) breath control (prāṇāyāmam)... prāṇāyāmam = breath control (acc. sg.)
kṛtvā = having done (abs. from √kṛ)
1.17 raudrīṃ gāyatrīṃ bahurūpīṃ vā japet ...one should mutter (japet) the Rudra Gāyatrī (raudrīṃ gāyatrīm) or (vā) the Bahurūpī (bahurūpīm) [mantra]. raudrīm = of Rudra (acc. sg. f.)
gāyatrīm = Gāyatrī [mantra] (acc. sg. f.)
bahurūpīm = many-formed [mantra]
vā = or
japet = one should mutter (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
1.18 akaluṣamateś caratas tato 'sya yogaḥ pravartate Then (tataḥ), for one practicing (carataḥ) with unpolluted mind (akaluṣamateḥ), his (asya) yoga (yogaḥ) begins (pravartate). akaluṣa-mateḥ = of one with unpolluted mind (gen. sg.)
carataḥ = practicing (gen. sg. pres. part. from √car)
tataḥ = then
asya = his
yogaḥ = union
pravartate = begins (3rd sg. pres. from pra-√vṛt)
1.19 dūrād darśanaśravaṇamananavijñānāni cāsya pravartante And (ca) his (asya) seeing (darśana), hearing (śravaṇa), thinking (manana), and knowledge (vijñānāni) from afar (dūrāt) arise (pravartante). dūrāt = from afar (abl.)
darśana = seeing
śravaṇa = hearing
manana = thinking
vijñānāni = knowledges (nom. pl.)
ca = and
asya = his
pravartante = arise (3rd pl. pres. from pra-√vṛt)
1.20 sarvajñatā [He attains] omniscience (sarvajñatā). sarva-jñatā = omniscience (nom. sg. f.)
1.21 manojavitvam [He attains] speed of mind (manojavitvam). mano-javitvam = speed of mind (nom. sg. n.)
1.22 kāmarūpitvam vikaraṇadharmitvam ca [He attains] the ability to assume any form (kāmarūpitvam) and (ca) to act without organs (vikaraṇadharmitvam). kāma-rūpitvam = ability to assume any form (nom. sg. n.)
vikaraṇa-dharmitvam = ability to act without organs
ca = and
1.23 sarve cāsya vaśyā bhavanti And (ca) all (sarve) become (bhavanti) subject to his (asya) control (vaśyāḥ). sarve = all (nom. pl.)
ca = and
asya = his
vaśyāḥ = subject to control
bhavanti = become (3rd pl. pres. from √bhū)
1.24 sarveṣāṃ cāvaśyo bhavati And (ca) he becomes (bhavati) not subject to control (avaśyaḥ) by anyone (sarveṣām). sarveṣām = of all (gen. pl.)
ca = and
avaśyaḥ = not subject to control
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
1.25 sarvāṃś cāviśati And (ca) he enters (āviśati) all (sarvān) [beings]. sarvān = all (acc. pl.)
ca = and
āviśati = he enters (3rd sg. pres. from ā-√viś)
1.26 sarveṣāṃ cānāveśyo bhavati And (ca) he becomes (bhavati) not to be entered (anāveśyaḥ) by anyone (sarveṣām). sarveṣām = of all (gen. pl.)
ca = and
anāveśyaḥ = not to be entered
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
1.27 sarve cāsya vadhyā bhavanti And (ca) all (sarve) become (bhavanti) liable to be killed (vadhyāḥ) by him (asya). sarve = all (nom. pl.)
ca = and
asya = his
vadhyāḥ = liable to be killed
bhavanti = become (3rd pl. pres. from √bhū)
1.28 sarveṣāṃ cāvadhyo bhavati And (ca) he becomes (bhavati) not liable to be killed (avadhyaḥ) by anyone (sarveṣām). sarveṣām = of all (gen. pl.)
ca = and
avadhyaḥ = not liable to be killed
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
1.29 abhīto 'kṣayo 'jaro 'maraḥ sarvatra cāpratihatagatir bhavati He becomes (bhavati) fearless (abhītaḥ), imperishable (akṣayaḥ), unaging (ajaraḥ), immortal (amaraḥ), and (ca) of unobstructed movement (apratihatagatiḥ) everywhere (sarvatra). abhītaḥ = fearless
akṣayaḥ = imperishable
ajaraḥ = unaging
amaraḥ = immortal
sarvatra = everywhere
ca = and
apratihata-gatiḥ = of unobstructed movement
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
1.30 ity etair guṇair yukto bhagavato mahādevasya mahāgaṇapatir bhavati Thus (iti) endowed (yuktaḥ) with these (etaiḥ) qualities (guṇaiḥ), he becomes (bhavati) the great lord of hosts (mahāgaṇapatiḥ) of Lord (bhagavataḥ) Mahādeva (mahādevasya). iti = thus
etaiḥ = with these (inst. pl.)
guṇaiḥ = qualities
yuktaḥ = endowed
bhagavataḥ = of the Lord (gen.)
mahādevasya = of Mahādeva
mahā-gaṇa-patiḥ = great lord of hosts
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
1.31 atredaṃ brahma japet Here (atra) one should mutter (japet) this (idam) sacred formula (brahma): atra = here
idam = this
brahma = sacred formula
japet = one should mutter (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
1.32 sadyojātaṃ prapadyāmi sadyojātāya vai namaḥ I take refuge in (prapadyāmi) the Newly-born (sadyojātam); homage (namaḥ) indeed (vai) to the Newly-born (sadyojātāya)! sadyojātam = the Newly-born (acc.)
prapadyāmi = I take refuge in (1st sg. pres. mid. from pra-√pad)
sadyojātāya = to the Newly-born (dat.)
vai = indeed
namaḥ = homage
1.33 bhave bhave nātibhave bhavasva mām In existence after existence (bhave bhave), not (na) in excessive existence (atibhave), be (bhavasva) [favorable] to me (mām)! bhave bhave = in existence after existence
na = not
atibhave = in excessive existence
bhavasva = be (2nd sg. imper. from √bhū)
mām = me
1.34 bhavodbhavāya namaḥ Homage (namaḥ) to the one arisen from existence (bhavodbhavāya)! bhava-udbhavāya = to the one arisen from existence (dat.)
namaḥ = homage

Pāśupatasūtra Adhyāya 2 (14 Sūtras)

Sūtrapāṭha edition by Peter Bisschop (2006)

Sanskrit Text Translation Dictionary
2.1 vāmadevasya jyeṣṭhasya śreṣṭhasya rudrasya kalitāsanam [This is] the seat (kalitāsanam) made by Vāmadeva (vāmadevasya), the eldest (jyeṣṭhasya), the best (śreṣṭhasya), by Rudra (rudrasya). vāmadevasya = of Vāmadeva (gen. sg. m.)
jyeṣṭhasya = of the eldest (gen. sg. m.)
śreṣṭhasya = of the best (gen. sg. m.)
rudrasya = of Rudra (gen. sg. m.)
kalita-āsanam = seat made/arranged (nom. sg. n.)
2.2 sarvakāmika ity ācakṣate They call (ācakṣate) [this] "fulfilling all desires" (sarvakāmika iti). sarva-kāmika = fulfilling all desires (nom. sg. m.)
iti = thus (quotative particle)
ācakṣate = they call (3rd pl. pres. ā-√cakṣ)
2.3 amaṅgalaṃ cātra maṅgalaṃ bhavati And (ca) here (atra), the inauspicious (amaṅgalam) becomes (bhavati) auspicious (maṅgalam). amaṅgalam = inauspicious (nom. sg. n.)
ca = and
atra = here
maṅgalam = auspicious (nom. sg. n.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
2.4 apasavyaṃ ca pradakṣiṇam And (ca) the left-handed/inauspicious (apasavyam) [becomes] circumambulation/auspicious (pradakṣiṇam). apasavyam = left-handed, inauspicious (nom. sg. n.)
ca = and
pradakṣiṇam = right-handed circumambulation, auspicious (nom. sg. n.)
2.5 tasmād ubhayathā yaṣṭavyo devavat pitṛvac ca Therefore (tasmāt), [Rudra] should be worshipped (yaṣṭavyaḥ) in both ways (ubhayathā): like a god (devavat) and (ca) like the ancestors (pitṛvat). tasmāt = therefore (abl. sg.)
ubhayathā = in both ways (adv.)
yaṣṭavyaḥ = to be worshipped (fut. pass. part. from √yaj)
deva-vat = like a god (adv.)
pitṛ-vat = like the ancestors (adv.)
ca = and
2.6 ubhaye tu rudre devāḥ pitaraś ca But (tu) both (ubhaye) the gods (devāḥ) and (ca) the ancestors (pitaraḥ) [are] in Rudra (rudre). ubhaye = both (nom. pl. m.)
tu = but, indeed
rudre = in Rudra (loc. sg. m.)
devāḥ = gods (nom. pl. m.)
pitaraḥ = ancestors (nom. pl. m.)
ca = and
2.7 harṣāpramādī [One should be] joyful (harṣa) and vigilant (apramādī). harṣa = joy, delight (stem)
apramādī = vigilant, not careless (nom. sg. m.)
2.8 caryāyāṃ caryāyāṃ māhātmyam avāpnoti In each observance (caryāyām caryāyām), one attains (avāpnoti) greatness (māhātmyam). caryāyām caryāyām = in each observance (loc. sg. f., distributive repetition)
māhātmyam = greatness, majesty (acc. sg. n.)
avāpnoti = one attains (3rd sg. pres. from ava-√āp)
2.9 atidattam atigūḍham [This is] supremely given (atidattam), supremely secret (atigūḍham). ati-dattam = supremely given (nom. sg. n., PPP from √dā)
ati-gūḍham = supremely secret (nom. sg. n., PPP from √guh)
2.10 atitaptaṃ tapas tathā Likewise (tathā) [this is] supremely performed austerity (atitaptam tapas). ati-taptam = supremely performed/heated (nom. sg. n., PPP from √tap)
tapas = austerity (nom. sg. n.)
tathā = likewise, so (adv.)
2.11 atyāgatiṃ gamayate [This] leads (gamayate) to the supreme goal (atyāgatim). ati-āgatim = supreme goal/destination (acc. sg. f.)
gamayate = causes to go, leads (3rd sg. caus. pres. from √gam)
2.12 tasmād bhūyas tapaś caret | nānyabhaktis tu śaṃkare Therefore (tasmāt), one should perform (caret) more (bhūyas) austerity (tapas). But (tu) [one should have] no (na) devotion to another (anyabhaktiḥ) [than] Śaṃkara (śaṃkare). tasmāt = therefore (abl.)
bhūyas = more, further (adv.)
tapas = austerity (acc. sg. n.)
caret = one should perform (3rd sg. opt. from √car)
na = not
anya-bhaktiḥ = devotion to another (nom. sg. f.)
tu = but
śaṃkare = in/to Śaṃkara (loc. sg. m.)
2.13 atredaṃ brahma japet Here (atra), one should mutter (japet) this (idam) sacred formula (brahma). atra = here (adv.)
idam = this (nom. sg. n.)
brahma = sacred formula, Veda (acc. sg. n.)
japet = one should mutter (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
2.14 vāmadevāya namo jyeṣṭhāya namaḥ śreṣṭhāya namo rudrāya namaḥ kālāya namaḥ kalavikaraṇāya namo balavikaraṇāya namo balāya namo balapramathanāya namaḥ sarvabhūtadamanāya namo manonmanāya namaḥ Homage (namaḥ) to Vāmadeva (vāmadevāya)! Homage to the Eldest (jyeṣṭhāya)! Homage to the Best (śreṣṭhāya)! Homage to Rudra (rudrāya)! Homage to Kāla (kālāya)! Homage to the Transformer of Time (kalavikaraṇāya)! Homage to the Transformer of Strength (balavikaraṇāya)! Homage to the Strong One (balāya)! Homage to the Crusher of Strength (balapramathanāya)! Homage to the Subduer of All Beings (sarvabhūtadamanāya)! Homage to the One Who Elevates the Mind (manonmanāya)! vāmadevāya = to Vāmadeva (dat. sg. m.)
namaḥ = homage (nom. sg. n.)
jyeṣṭhāya = to the eldest (dat. sg. m.)
śreṣṭhāya = to the best (dat. sg. m.)
rudrāya = to Rudra (dat. sg. m.)
kālāya = to Kāla/Time (dat. sg. m.)
kala-vikaraṇāya = to the transformer of time (dat. sg. m.)
bala-vikaraṇāya = to the transformer of strength (dat. sg. m.)
balāya = to the strong one (dat. sg. m.)
bala-pramathanāya = to the crusher of strength (dat. sg. m.)
sarva-bhūta-damanāya = to the subduer of all beings (dat. sg. m.)
manas-unmanāya = to the one who elevates the mind (dat. sg. m.)

Pāśupatasūtra Adhyāya 3 (18 Sūtras)

Sūtrapāṭha edition by Peter Bisschop (2006)

Sanskrit Text Translation Dictionary
3.1 avyaktaliṅgī vyaktācāraḥ [He should be] one with concealed marks (avyaktaliṅgī) [but with] manifest conduct (vyaktācāraḥ). avyakta-liṅgī = one with concealed marks (nom. sg. m.)
vyakta-ācāraḥ = with manifest conduct (nom. sg. m.)
3.2 avamataḥ sarvabhūteṣu [He should be] despised (avamataḥ) among all beings (sarvabhūteṣu). avamataḥ = despised (nom. sg. m. pp. from ava-√man)
sarva-bhūteṣu = among all beings (loc. pl. n.)
3.3 paribhūyamānaś caret Being despised (paribhūyamānaḥ), one should practice (caret). paribhūyamānaḥ = being despised (nom. sg. m. pres. pass. part. from pari-√bhū)
caret = one should practice (3rd sg. opt. from √car)
3.4 apahatapāpmā pareṣāṃ parivādāt [He becomes] one whose sins are removed (apahatapāpmā) through the slander (parivādāt) of others (pareṣām). apahata-pāpmā = one whose sins are removed (nom. sg. m.)
pareṣām = of others (gen. pl. m.)
parivādāt = from slander (abl. sg. m.)
3.5 pāpaṃ ca tebhyo dadāti And (ca) he gives (dadāti) [his] sin (pāpam) to them (tebhyaḥ). pāpam = sin (acc. sg. n.)
ca = and
tebhyaḥ = to them (dat. pl. m.)
dadāti = he gives (3rd sg. pres. from √dā)
3.6 sukṛtaṃ ca teṣām ādatte And (ca) he takes (ādatte) their (teṣām) merit (sukṛtam). sukṛtam = merit, good deeds (acc. sg. n.)
ca = and
teṣām = their (gen. pl. m.)
ādatte = he takes (3rd sg. pres. mid. from ā-√dā)
3.7 tasmāt pretavac caret Therefore (tasmāt), one should practice (caret) like a ghost (pretavat). tasmāt = therefore (abl. sg.)
preta-vat = like a ghost (ind.)
caret = one should practice (3rd sg. opt. from √car)
3.8 krātheta vā Or (vā) one should moan/groan (krātheta). krātheta = one should moan (3rd sg. opt. from √krath)
vā = or (particle)
3.9 spandeta vā Or (vā) one should tremble/quiver (spandeta). spandeta = one should tremble (3rd sg. opt. from √spand)
vā = or (particle)
3.10 maṇṭeta vā Or (vā) one should walk unsteadily (maṇṭeta). maṇṭeta = one should walk unsteadily (3rd sg. opt. from √maṇṭ)
vā = or (particle)
3.11 śṛṅgāreta vā Or (vā) one should act amorously (śṛṅgāreta). śṛṅgāreta = one should act amorously (3rd sg. opt. from √śṛṅgāray)
vā = or (particle)
3.12 apitatkuryāt One should do (kuryāt) something improper (api tat). api = even, improper
tat = that (acc. sg. n.)
kuryāt = one should do (3rd sg. opt. from √kṛ)
3.13 apitadbhāṣet One should speak (bhāṣet) something improper (api tat). api = even, improper
tat = that (acc. sg. n.)
bhāṣet = one should speak (3rd sg. opt. from √bhāṣ)
3.14 yena parebhyaḥ paribhavaṃ gacchet By which (yena) one would incur (gacchet) contempt (paribhavam) from others (parebhyaḥ). yena = by which (inst. sg.)
parebhyaḥ = from others (abl. pl. m.)
paribhavam = contempt (acc. sg. m.)
gacchet = one would incur (3rd sg. opt. from √gam)
3.15 paribhūyamāno hi vidvān kṛtsnatapā bhavati For (hi) a wise one (vidvān) being despised (paribhūyamānaḥ) becomes (bhavati) one who has completed all austerities (kṛtsnatapāḥ). paribhūyamānaḥ = being despised (nom. sg. m. pres. pass. part. from pari-√bhū)
hi = for, indeed
vidvān = wise one (nom. sg. m.)
kṛtsna-tapāḥ = one who has completed all austerities (nom. sg. m.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. from √bhū)
3.16 atredaṃ brahma japet Here (atra) one should mutter (japet) this (idam) sacred formula (brahma). atra = here (ind.)
idam = this (acc. sg. n.)
brahma = sacred formula (acc. sg. n.)
japet = one should mutter (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
3.17 aghorebhyo 'tha ghorebhyo ghoraghoratarebhyaḥ [Homage] to the non-terrible ones (aghorebhyaḥ), then (atha) to the terrible ones (ghorebhyaḥ), to the more terrible than terrible (ghoraghoratarebhyaḥ). aghorebhyaḥ = to the non-terrible ones (dat. pl. m.)
atha = then
ghorebhyaḥ = to the terrible ones (dat. pl. m.)
ghora-ghora-tarebhyaḥ = to the more terrible than terrible (dat. pl. m.)
3.18 sarvebhyaḥ sarvaśarvebhyo namas te astu rudrarūpebhyaḥ May there be (astu) homage (namaḥ) to you (te) in all your forms (sarvebhyaḥ sarvaśarvebhyaḥ), to [all your] Rudra forms (rudrarūpebhyaḥ). sarvebhyaḥ = to all (dat. pl. m.)
sarva-śarvebhyaḥ = to all Śarvas (dat. pl. m.)
namaḥ = homage (nom. sg. n.)
te = to you (dat. sg.)
astu = may it be (3rd sg. imper. from √as)
rudra-rūpebhyaḥ = to [your] Rudra forms (dat. pl. n.)

Pāśupatasūtra Adhyāya 4 (17 Sūtras)

Sūtrapāṭha edition by Peter Bisschop (2006)

Sanskrit Text Translation Dictionary
4.1 gūḍhavidyā taponantyāya prakāśate The hidden knowledge (gūḍhavidyā) shines forth (prakāśate) for the sake of endless austerity (taponantyāya). gūḍhavidyā = hidden knowledge (nom. sg. f.)
tapas = austerity (n.)
ānantyāya = for the sake of endlessness (dat. sg.)
prakāśate = shines forth, becomes manifest (3rd sg. pres. ātm. from √kāś)
4.2 gūḍhavrato gūḍhapavitravāṇiḥ [One should be] of hidden vow (gūḍhavrata) and of hidden, pure speech (gūḍhapavitravāṇiḥ). gūḍhavrato = of hidden vow (nom. sg. m. BV cpd.)
gūḍhapavitravāṇiḥ = of hidden, pure speech (nom. sg. m. BV cpd.)
vrata = vow, observance (n.)
pavitra = pure (adj.)
vāṇī = speech (f.)
4.3 sarvāṇi dvārāṇi pidhāya buddhyā Having closed (pidhāya) all (sarvāṇi) the doors [of the senses] (dvārāṇi) by means of intellect (buddhyā). sarvāṇi = all (acc. pl. n.)
dvārāṇi = doors, gates [of the senses] (acc. pl. n.)
pidhāya = having closed, having covered (abs. from √dhā with api)
buddhyā = by means of intellect, with the mind (inst. sg. f.)
4.4 unmattavad eko vicareta loke One should wander (vicareta) alone (eko) in the world (loke) like a madman (unmattavat). unmattavat = like a madman (ind.)
ekaḥ = alone, single (nom. sg. m.)
vicareta = one should wander (3rd sg. opt. from √car with vi)
loke = in the world (loc. sg. m.)
4.5 kṛtānnam utsṛṣṭam upādadīta One should take up (upādadīta) cooked food (kṛtānnam) that has been thrown away (utsṛṣṭam). kṛtānnam = cooked food (acc. sg. n.)
utsṛṣṭam = thrown away, discarded (acc. sg. n. PPP from √sṛj with ut)
upādadīta = one should take up, accept (3rd sg. opt. ātm. from √dā with upa-ā)
4.6 unmatto mūḍha ity evaṃ manyante itare janāḥ Other people (itare janāḥ) think (manyante) thus (evam): "He is mad (unmattaḥ), he is a fool (mūḍhaḥ)." unmattaḥ = mad, insane (nom. sg. m.)
mūḍhaḥ = fool, deluded one (nom. sg. m. PPP from √muh)
iti = thus (quotative particle)
evam = thus, in this way (ind.)
manyante = they think (3rd pl. pres. ātm. from √man)
itare = other (nom. pl. m.)
janāḥ = people (nom. pl. m.)
4.7 asaṃmāno hi jantūnāṃ sarveṣām uttamaḥ smṛtaḥ For (hi) dishonor (asaṃmānaḥ) is remembered (smṛtaḥ) as the highest (uttamaḥ) [state] for all (sarveṣām) living beings (jantūnām). asaṃmānaḥ = dishonor, disrespect (nom. sg. m.)
hi = for, indeed (particle)
jantūnām = of living beings (gen. pl. m.)
sarveṣām = of all (gen. pl. m.)
uttamaḥ = highest, best (nom. sg. m.)
smṛtaḥ = remembered, considered (nom. sg. m. PPP from √smṛ)
4.8 indro vā agre asureṣu pāśupatam acarat Indra (indraḥ) indeed (vā) in the beginning (agre) practiced (acarat) the Pāśupata [vow] (pāśupatam) among the Asuras (asureṣu). indraḥ = Indra (nom. sg. m.)
vā = indeed, or (particle)
agre = in the beginning, at first (loc. sg.)
asureṣu = among the Asuras (loc. pl. m.)
pāśupatam = the Pāśupata [vow/practice] (acc. sg. n.)
acarat = practiced, performed (3rd sg. impf. from √car)
4.9 sa teṣām iṣṭāpūrttam ādatta māyayā sukṛtayā samavindata He (saḥ) took away (ādatta) their (teṣām) merit from sacrifices and pious works (iṣṭāpūrttam); by his skillful deception (māyayā sukṛtayā), he obtained [it] (samavindata). saḥ = he (nom. sg. m.)
teṣām = their (gen. pl. m.)
iṣṭāpūrttam = merit from sacrifice and pious works (acc. sg. n.)
ādatta = took away (3rd sg. perf. ātm. from √dā with ā)
māyayā = by deception, by magic (inst. sg. f.)
sukṛtayā = well-made, skillful (inst. sg. f.)
samavindata = he obtained, he found (3rd sg. impf. ātm. from √vid with sam)
4.10 nindā hy eṣām anindā For (hi) the blame (nindā) of these [practitioners] (eṣām) is [in fact] non-blame (anindā). nindā = blame, censure (nom. sg. f.)
hi = for, indeed (particle)
eṣām = of these (gen. pl.)
anindā = non-blame, praise (nom. sg. f.)
4.11 tasmān nindyamānaś caret Therefore (tasmāt) one should practice (caret) while being blamed (nindyamānaḥ). tasmāt = therefore (abl. sg.)
nindyamānaḥ = being blamed (nom. sg. m. pres. pass. part. from √nind)
caret = one should practice, should conduct oneself (3rd sg. opt. from √car)
4.12 sarvaviśiṣṭo 'yaṃ panthāḥ satpathaḥ This (ayam) path (panthāḥ) is distinguished from all [others] (sarvaviśiṣṭaḥ); it is the true path (satpathaḥ). sarvaviśiṣṭaḥ = distinguished from all (nom. sg. m. PPP from √śiṣ with vi)
ayam = this (nom. sg. m.)
panthāḥ = path, way (nom. sg. m.)
satpathaḥ = true path, good path (nom. sg. m.)
4.13 kupathās tv anye But (tu) the others (anye) [are] bad paths (kupathāḥ). kupathāḥ = bad paths, wrong ways (nom. pl. m.)
tu = but, however (particle)
anye = other (nom. pl. m.)
4.14 anena vidhinā rudrasamīpaṃ gatvā na kaścid brāhmaṇaḥ punar āvartate Having gone (gatvā) near to Rudra (rudrasamīpam) by this method (anena vidhinā), no (na) Brahmin (kaścid brāhmaṇaḥ) returns (āvartate) again (punar). anena = by this (inst. sg. m.)
vidhinā = by the method, by the rule (inst. sg. m.)
rudrasamīpam = near to Rudra, proximity of Rudra (acc. sg. n.)
gatvā = having gone (abs. from √gam)
na = not (particle)
kaścid = anyone, any (nom. sg. m.)
brāhmaṇaḥ = Brahmin (nom. sg. m.)
punar = again (ind.)
āvartate = returns (3rd sg. pres. ātm. from √vṛt with ā)
4.15 atredaṃ brahma japet Here (atra) one should recite (japet) this (idam) sacred formula (brahma). atra = here, at this point (ind.)
idam = this (acc. sg. n.)
brahma = sacred formula, Vedic mantra (acc. sg. n.)
japet = one should recite, should mutter (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
4.16 tatpuruṣāya vidmahe mahādevāya dhīmahi We know (vidmahe) that Supreme Person (tatpuruṣāya); we meditate (dhīmahi) upon the Great God (mahādevāya). tatpuruṣāya = to/for that Supreme Person (dat. sg. m.)
vidmahe = we know (1st pl. perf. ātm. from √vid)
mahādevāya = to/for the Great God (dat. sg. m.)
dhīmahi = we meditate upon (1st pl. opt. ātm. from √dhī)
4.17 tan no rudraḥ pracodayāt May Rudra (rudraḥ) impel (pracodayāt) us (naḥ) toward that (tat). tat = that (acc. sg. n.)
naḥ = us (acc. pl.)
rudraḥ = Rudra (nom. sg. m.)
pracodayāt = may he impel, may he inspire (3rd sg. opt. caus. from √cud with pra)

Pāśupatasūtra Adhyāya 5 (29 Sūtras)

Sūtrapāṭha edition by Peter Bisschop (2006)

Sanskrit Text Translation Dictionary
5.1 asaṅgayogī nityātmā ajo maitro 'bhijāyate The unattached yogin (asaṅgayogī), of eternal self (nityātmā), unborn (ajaḥ), friendly (maitraḥ), is born (abhijāyate). asaṅgayogī = unattached yogin (nom. sg. m.)
nityātmā = of eternal self (nom. sg. m.)
ajaḥ = unborn (nom. sg. m.)
maitraḥ = friendly (nom. sg. m.)
abhijāyate = is born (3rd sg. pres. pass. from √jan)
5.2 indriyāṇām abhijayāt From the complete conquest (abhijayāt) of the senses (indriyāṇām). indriyāṇām = of the senses (gen. pl. n.)
abhijayāt = from complete conquest (abl. sg. m. from √ji)
5.3 rudraḥ provāca tāvat Rudra (rudraḥ) spoke (provāca) thus far (tāvat). rudraḥ = Rudra (nom. sg. m.)
provāca = spoke (3rd sg. perf. from pra + √vac)
tāvat = thus far, so much (ind.)
5.4 śūnyāgāraguhāvāsī Dwelling in an empty house or cave (śūnyāgāraguhāvāsī). śūnya = empty (adj.)
āgāra = house (n.)
guhā = cave (f.)
vāsī = dweller (nom. sg. m. from √vas)
5.5 devanityo jitendriyaḥ Constantly devoted to God (devanityaḥ), with conquered senses (jitendriyaḥ). devanityaḥ = constantly devoted to God (nom. sg. m.)
jitendriyaḥ = with conquered senses (nom. sg. m., BV compound from √ji)
5.6 ṣaṇmāsān nityayuktasya bhūyiṣṭhaṃ sampravartate For one constantly yoked (nityayuktasya), within six months (ṣaṇmāsān), [yoga] proceeds (sampravartate) abundantly (bhūyiṣṭham). ṣaṇmāsān = six months (acc. pl. m.)
nityayuktasya = of one constantly yoked (gen. sg. m.)
bhūyiṣṭham = most abundantly (ind.)
sampravartate = proceeds, commences (3rd sg. pres. from sam + pra + √vṛt)
5.7 bhaikṣyaṃ pātrāgataṃ māṃsam aduṣyaṃ lavaṇena vā Alms food (bhaikṣyam), what has come into the bowl (pātrāgatam), meat (māṃsam), that which is not defiled (aduṣyam), or with salt (lavaṇena vā). bhaikṣyam = alms food (nom./acc. sg. n.)
pātrāgatam = come into the bowl (nom./acc. sg. n.)
māṃsam = meat (nom./acc. sg. n.)
aduṣyam = not defiled (nom./acc. sg. n.)
lavaṇena = with salt (inst. sg. n.)
vā = or (ind.)
5.8 āpo vāpi yathākālam aśnīyād anupūrvaśaḥ Or even water (āpaḥ vā api), one should eat (aśnīyāt) at the proper time (yathākālam), in due order (anupūrvaśaḥ). āpaḥ = water (acc. pl. f.)
vā api = or even (ind.)
yathākālam = at the proper time (ind.)
aśnīyāt = should eat (3rd sg. opt. from √aś)
anupūrvaśaḥ = in due order (ind.)
5.9 godharmā mṛgadharmā vā Following the way of a cow (godharmā) or following the way of a deer (mṛgadharmā vā). godharmā = following the way of a cow (nom. sg. m.)
mṛgadharmā = following the way of a deer (nom. sg. m.)
vā = or (ind.)
5.10 adbhir eva śucir bhavet By water alone (adbhiḥ eva), one should become (bhavet) pure (śuciḥ). adbhiḥ = by water (inst. pl. n.)
eva = alone, only (ind.)
śuciḥ = pure (nom. sg. m.)
bhavet = should become (3rd sg. opt. from √bhū)
5.11 siddhayogī na lipyeta The perfected yogin (siddhayogī) is not smeared (na lipyeta). siddhayogī = perfected yogin (nom. sg. m.)
na = not (ind.)
lipyeta = is smeared, is stained (3rd sg. opt. pass. from √lip)
5.12 karmaṇā pātakena vā By action (karmaṇā) or by sin (pātakena vā). karmaṇā = by action (inst. sg. n.)
pātakena = by sin (inst. sg. n.)
vā = or (ind.)
5.13 ṛcam iṣṭām adhīyīta One should recite (adhīyīta) a desired verse (ṛcam iṣṭām). ṛcam = verse (acc. sg. f.)
iṣṭām = desired (acc. sg. f.)
adhīyīta = should recite (3rd sg. opt. from adhi + √i)
5.14 gāyatrīm ātmayantritaḥ The Gāyatrī (gāyatrīm), self-controlled (ātmayantritaḥ). gāyatrīm = Gāyatrī [mantra] (acc. sg. f.)
ātmayantritaḥ = self-controlled (nom. sg. m. from √yam)
5.15 raudrīṃ vā bahurūpīṃ vā Or the Raudrī (raudrīm vā), or the Bahurūpī (bahurūpīm vā). raudrīm = Raudrī [mantra] (acc. sg. f.)
bahurūpīm = Bahurūpī [mantra], having many forms (acc. sg. f.)
vā = or (ind.)
5.16 ato yogaḥ pravartate From this (ataḥ), yoga (yogaḥ) proceeds (pravartate). ataḥ = from this (ind.)
yogaḥ = yoga (nom. sg. m.)
pravartate = proceeds (3rd sg. pres. from pra + √vṛt)
5.17 oṃkāram abhidhyāyīta One should meditate upon (abhidhyāyīta) the syllable Om (oṃkāram). oṃkāram = the syllable Om (acc. sg. m.)
abhidhyāyīta = should meditate upon (3rd sg. opt. from abhi + √dhyai)
5.18 tat sad iti hṛdi kurvīta dhāraṇām One should make (kurvīta) concentration (dhāraṇām) in the heart (hṛdi) [on the mantra] "that is real" (tat sat iti). tat = that (nom. sg. n.)
sat = being, real (nom. sg. n.)
iti = thus (ind.)
hṛdi = in the heart (loc. sg. n.)
kurvīta = should make (3rd sg. opt. from √kṛ)
dhāraṇām = concentration (acc. sg. f.)
5.19 ṛṣir vipro mahān eṣa vāgviśuddho maheśvaraḥ This (eṣaḥ) is the seer (ṛṣiḥ), the brahmin (vipraḥ), the great one (mahān), purified in speech (vāgviśuddhaḥ), the great Lord (maheśvaraḥ). ṛṣiḥ = seer (nom. sg. m.)
vipraḥ = brahmin (nom. sg. m.)
mahān = great (nom. sg. m.)
eṣaḥ = this (nom. sg. m.)
vāgviśuddhaḥ = purified in speech (nom. sg. m.)
maheśvaraḥ = great Lord (nom. sg. m.)
5.20 śmaśānavāsī dharmātmā yathālabdhopajīvakaḥ Dwelling in the cremation ground (śmaśānavāsī), righteous in soul (dharmātmā), living on whatever is obtained (yathālabdhopajīvakaḥ). śmaśānavāsī = dwelling in the cremation ground (nom. sg. m. from √vas)
dharmātmā = righteous in soul (nom. sg. m.)
yathālabdhopajīvakaḥ = living on whatever is obtained (nom. sg. m. from √labh, √jīv)
5.21 labhate rudrasāyujyam He obtains (labhate) union with Rudra (rudrasāyujyam). labhate = obtains (3rd sg. pres. from √labh)
rudrasāyujyam = union with Rudra (acc. sg. n.)
5.22 sadā rudram anusmaret One should always (sadā) remember (anusmaret) Rudra (rudram). sadā = always (ind.)
rudram = Rudra (acc. sg. m.)
anusmaret = should remember (3rd sg. opt. from anu + √smṛ)
5.23 chittvā doṣāṇāṃ hetujālasya mūlam Having cut (chittvā) the root (mūlam) of the net of causes (hetujālasya) of faults (doṣāṇām). chittvā = having cut (abs. from √chid)
doṣāṇām = of faults (gen. pl. m.)
hetujālasya = of the net of causes (gen. sg. n.)
mūlam = root (acc. sg. n.)
5.24 buddhyā saṃcintya sthāpayitvā tu rudre Having reflected (saṃcintya) with the intellect (buddhyā), having established (sthāpayitvā) [the mind] indeed (tu) in Rudra (rudre). buddhyā = with the intellect (inst. sg. f.)
saṃcintya = having reflected (abs. from sam + √cint)
sthāpayitvā = having established (abs. caus. from √sthā)
tu = indeed (ind.)
rudre = in Rudra (loc. sg. m.)
5.25 ekaḥ kṣemyāṃsaṃ vītaśokaḥ Alone (ekaḥ), [attaining] security (kṣemyāṃsam), free from sorrow (vītaśokaḥ). ekaḥ = alone (nom. sg. m.)
kṣemyāṃsam = security, welfare (acc. sg. m.)
vītaśokaḥ = free from sorrow (nom. sg. m.)
5.26 apramādī gacched duḥkhānām antam īśaprasādāt Vigilant (apramādī), one should go (gacchet) to the end (antam) of sufferings (duḥkhānām), by the grace of the Lord (īśaprasādāt). apramādī = vigilant (nom. sg. m.)
gacchet = should go (3rd sg. opt. from √gam)
duḥkhānām = of sufferings (gen. pl. n.)
antam = end (acc. sg. m.)
īśaprasādāt = by the grace of the Lord (abl. sg. m.)
5.27 atredaṃ brahma japet Here (atra), one should recite (japet) this sacred formula (idam brahma). atra = here (ind.)
idam = this (nom./acc. sg. n.)
brahma = sacred formula (nom./acc. sg. n.)
japet = should recite (3rd sg. opt. from √jap)
5.28 īśānaḥ sarvavidyānām īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānām The ruler (īśānaḥ) of all knowledge (sarvavidyānām), the Lord (īśvaraḥ) of all beings (sarvabhūtānām). īśānaḥ = ruler (nom. sg. m.)
sarvavidyānām = of all knowledge (gen. pl. f.)
īśvaraḥ = Lord (nom. sg. m.)
sarvabhūtānām = of all beings (gen. pl. n.)
5.29 brahmādipati brahmaṇo 'dhipati brahmā śivo me astu sadāśivom Lord of Brahmā (brahmādhipatiḥ), overlord of the sacred word (brahmaṇaḥ adhipatiḥ), may Brahmā (brahmā), the auspicious one (śivaḥ), be (astu) mine (me), the eternally auspicious Om (sadāśivom). brahmādhipatiḥ = lord of Brahmā (nom. sg. m.)
brahmaṇaḥ = of the sacred word (gen. sg. n.)
adhipatiḥ = overlord (nom. sg. m.)
brahmā = Brahmā (nom. sg. m.)
śivaḥ = auspicious one (nom. sg. m.)
me = to me (dat. sg.)
astu = may be (3rd sg. impv. from √as)
sadāśivom = eternally auspicious Om (nom. sg.)

Vivekamārtaṇḍa - Comprehensive Scholarly Analysis

Verse-by-Verse Translation with Linguistic Analysis

Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Verse Summary Jim's Translation
śrīguruṃ paramānandaṃ vande svānandavigraham |
yasya sānnidhyamātreṇa cidānandāyate tanuḥ ||1||

De-sandhi:
śrī-gurum parama-ānandam vande sva-ānanda-vigraham |
yasya sānnidhya-mātreṇa cit-ānanda-āyate tanuḥ ||		 śrī-gurum = blessed guru (acc.)
parama-ānandam = supreme bliss (acc.)
vande = I bow (1st sg.)
sva-ānanda-vigraham = embodiment of self-bliss (acc.)
yasya = whose (gen.)
sānnidhya-mātreṇa = by mere presence (inst.)
cit-ānanda-āyate = becomes consciousness-bliss (3rd sg.)
tanuḥ = body (nom.)		 I bow (vande) to the blessed guru (śrīgurum), supreme bliss (paramānandam), embodiment of own-bliss (svānandavigraham), by whose (yasya) mere presence (sānnidhyamātreṇa) the body (tanuḥ) becomes consciousness-bliss (cidānandāyate). Salutation to the guru
[√vand - to honor, √ā-yam - to become]		 I venerate the glorious guru, who is supreme bliss, who is the manifestation of his own bliss, merely by being in whose presence one's body becomes consciousness and bliss.
antar-niścalita-ātma-dīpa-kalikā-svādhāra-bandhādibhir
yo yogī yuga-kalpa-kāla-kalanāt tattvam ca jegīyate |
jñāna-āmoda-mahodadhiḥ samabhavad yatrādināthaḥ svayaṃ
vyakta-avyakta-guṇa-adhikaṃ tamaniśaṃ śrīmīnanāthaṃ bhaje ||2||

De-sandhi:
antaḥ-niścalita-ātma-dīpa-kalikā-sva-ādhāra-bandha-ādibhiḥ
yaḥ yogī yuga-kalpa-kāla-kalanāt tattvam ca jegīyate |
jñāna-āmoda-mahā-udadhiḥ samabhavat yatra Ādināṭhaḥ svayam
vyakta-avyakta-guṇa-adhikam tam aniśam śrī-Mīnanātham bhaje ||		 antaḥ-niścalita-ātma-dīpa-kalikā = in flame of soul-lamp steadied within
sva-ādhāra-bandha-ādibhiḥ = by locking own base etc. (inst.)
yaḥ yogī = which yogi (nom.)
yuga-kalpa-kāla-kalanāt = through understanding yugas, kalpas, time
tattvam = reality (acc.)
jegīyate = is thoroughly proclaimed
jñāna-āmoda-mahā-udadhiḥ = great ocean of knowledge-bliss (nom.)
samabhavat = became/occurred (3rd sg. past)
yatra = where (rel. adv.)
Ādināṭhaḥ = Ādinātha (nom.)
svayam = himself
vyakta-avyakta-guṇa-adhikam = greater than manifest-unmanifest qualities (acc.)
tam = him (acc.)
aniśam = constantly
Mīnanātham = Matsyendranātha (acc.)
bhaje = I worship (1st sg.)		 The yogi (yaḥ yogī) by locking own base etc. (svādhārabandhādibhiḥ) in the flame of the soul-lamp steadied within (antarniścalitātmadīpakalikā), through understanding (kalanāt) of yugas, kalpas, and time (yugakalpakāla), by whom reality (tattvam) is thoroughly proclaimed (jegīyate), who became (samabhavat) [that state] where (yatra) Ādinātha himself (Ādināṭhaḥ svayam) [appeared as] a great ocean of knowledge-bliss (jñānāmodamahodadhiḥ), [who is] greater than manifest and unmanifest qualities (vyaktāvyaktaguṇādhikam)—him (tam), the glorious Matsyendranātha (śrīmīnanātham), I constantly (aniśam) worship (bhaje). Praise of Matsyendranātha as embodiment of Ādinātha
[√bhaj - to worship, √jñā - to know, √bhū - to become]		 The yogi who, by means of locking the base and so forth in the buds (flames, kalikās) of the internally stilled lamp of the self, is extolled as (extols?) the essence of creating the yugas, kalpas and time (?), in whom Ādinātha himself appeared, the great ocean of knowledge and pleasure; him do I constantly worship as the glorious Mīnanātha, who abounds in the manifest and unmanifest qualities (?).
namaskṛtya guruṃ bhaktyā gorakṣo jñānamuttamam |
abhīṣṭaṃ yogināṃ brūte paramānandakārakam ||3||

De-sandhi:
namaskṛtya gurum bhaktyā Gorakṣaḥ jñānam uttamam |
abhīṣṭam yoginām brūte parama-ānanda-kārakam ||		 namaskṛtya = having saluted (abs.)
gurum = guru (acc.)
bhaktyā = with devotion (inst.)
Gorakṣaḥ = Gorakṣa (nom.)
jñānam uttamam = supreme knowledge (acc.)
abhīṣṭam = desired (acc.)
yoginām = of yogis (gen.)
brūte = speaks (3rd sg.)
parama-ānanda-kārakam = causing supreme bliss (acc.)		 Having saluted (namaskṛtya) the guru (gurum) with devotion (bhaktyā), Gorakṣa (Gorakṣaḥ) speaks (brūte) supreme knowledge (jñānam uttamam), desired (abhīṣṭam) of yogis (yoginām), causing supreme bliss (paramānandakārakam). Gorakṣa's teaching of supreme knowledge
[√kṛ - to do, √brū - to speak]		 Having devotedly made obeisance to [him, his] guru, Gorakṣa teaches the highest knowledge, which is longed for by yogis and brings about supreme bliss.
gorakṣaśatakaṃ vakti yogināṃ hitakāmyayā |
dhruvaṃ yasyāvabodhena jāyate paramaṃ padam ||4||

De-sandhi:
gorakṣa-śatakam vakti yoginām hita-kāmyayā |
dhruvam yasya avabodhena jāyate paramam padam ||		 gorakṣa-śatakam = hundred verses of Gorakṣa (acc.)
vakti = speaks (3rd sg.)
yoginām = of yogis (gen.)
hita-kāmyayā = with desire for welfare (inst.)
dhruvam = certainly (ind.)
yasya = of which (gen.)
avabodhena = by understanding (inst.)
jāyate = arises (3rd sg.)
paramam padam = supreme state (nom.)		 He speaks (vakti) the hundred verses of Gorakṣa (gorakṣaśatakam) with desire for the welfare (hitakāmyayā) of yogis (yoginām), by understanding (avabodhena) of which (yasya) certainly (dhruvam) arises (jāyate) the supreme state (paramaṃ padam). Purpose of the Gorakṣaśataka
[√vac - to speak, √jan - to arise]		 Out of a desire for the good of yogis, he teaches the hundred [verses] of Gorakṣa, understanding of which is sure to bring about the supreme state.
etat vimukti-sopānam etat kālasya vañcanam |
yad vyāvṛttaṃ mano bhogād āsaktaṃ paramātmani ||1||

De-sandhi:
etat vimukti-sopānam etat kālasya vañcanam |
yad vyā-āvṛttam manaḥ bhogāt āsaktam parama-ātmani ||		 vimukti-sopānam = ladder to liberation (TP, nom.)
kālasya = of time/death (gen.)
vañcanam = deceiving (nom.)
yad = when/because (rel.)
vyā-āvṛttam = turned away (PPP, nom.)
manaḥ = mind (nom.)
bhogāt = from enjoyment (abl.)
āsaktam = attached (PPP, nom.)
parama-ātmani = in supreme Self (loc.)		 This (etat) is the ladder to liberation (vimuktisopānam), this is deceiving death (kālasya vañcanam)—when (yad) the mind (manaḥ) is turned away (vyāvṛttam) from worldly enjoyment (bhogāt) and attached (āsaktam) to the supreme Self (paramātmani). Mind turned from enjoyment to Supreme Self
[√vṛt - to turn, √sañj - to attach, √vañc - to deceive]		 This is the stairway to complete liberation, this is the cheating of death, by which the mind is turned away from worldly enjoyment and fixed in the supreme self.
dvija-sevita-śākhasya śruti-kalpa-taroḥ phalam |
śamanaṃ bhava-tāpasya yogaṃ bhajeta sattamāḥ ||2||

De-sandhi:
dvija-sevita-śākhasya śruti-kalpa-taroḥ phalam |
śamanam bhava-tāpasya yogam bhajeta sat-tamāḥ ||		 dvija-sevita-śākhasya = whose branches served by twice-born (BV, gen.)
śruti-kalpa-taroḥ = of wish-fulfilling tree of Vedas (gen.)
phalam = fruit (nom.)
śamanam = destroyer (nom.)
bhava-tāpasya = of pain of worldly existence (gen.)
yogam = yoga (acc.)
bhajeta = should resort to (opt.)
sat-tamāḥ = best persons (nom. pl.)		 O best of people (sattamāḥ), partake of (bhajeta) yoga (yogam), the fruit (phalam) of the wish-fulfilling tree of Vedic revelation (śrutikalpa-taroḥ) whose branches are frequented by the twice-born (dvijasevitaśākhasya), which soothes (śamanam) the torment of existence (bhavatāpasya). Yoga as fruit of Vedic wisdom
[√bhaj - to partake, √śam - to soothe]		 O excellent people, partake of yoga, the reward of the wish-fulfilling tree of revelation frequented by the twice-born, which soothes the torment of existence.
āsanaṃ prāṇa-saṃrodhaḥ pratyāhāraś ca dhāraṇā |
dhyānaṃ samādhir etāni yogāṅgāni bhavanti ṣaṭ ||3||

De-sandhi:
āsanam prāṇa-saṃrodhaḥ pratyāhāraḥ ca dhāraṇā |
dhyānam samādhiḥ etāni yoga-aṅgāni bhavanti ṣaṭ ||		 āsanam = posture (nom.)
prāṇa-saṃrodhaḥ = breath restraint (TP, nom.)
pratyāhāraḥ = withdrawal (nom.)
dhāraṇā = concentration (nom.)
dhyānam = meditation (nom.)
samādhiḥ = absorption (nom.)
etāni = these (nom. pl.)
yoga-aṅgāni = limbs of Yoga (TP, nom. pl.)
bhavanti = are (3rd pl.)
ṣaṭ = six		 Posture (āsanam), breath restraint (prāṇasaṃrodhaḥ), withdrawal (pratyāhāraḥ), concentration (dhāraṇā), meditation (dhyānam), and absorption (samādhiḥ)—these (etāni) are the six (ṣaṭ) limbs of Yoga (yogāṅgāni). Six limbs of yoga enumerated
[√bhū - to be, √rudh - to restrain]		 Posture, breath restraint (prāṇasaṃrodha), withdrawal, fixation, meditation and samādhi: these are the six auxiliaries of yoga.
āsanāni ca tāvanti yāvantyo jīva-jātayaḥ |
eteṣām akhilān bhedān vijānāti maheśvaraḥ ||4||

De-sandhi:
āsanāni ca tāvanti yāvantyaḥ jīva-jātayaḥ |
eteṣām akhilān bhedān vijānāti maheśvaraḥ ||		 āsanāni = postures (nom. pl.)
tāvanti = so many (nom. pl.)
yāvantyaḥ = as many as (nom. pl. f.)
jīva-jātayaḥ = species of living beings (TP, nom. pl.)
eteṣām = of these (gen.)
akhilān = all (acc. pl.)
bhedān = variations (acc. pl.)
vijānāti = knows (3rd sg.)
maheśvaraḥ = the great Lord, Shiva (nom.)		 There are as many (tāvanti) postures (āsanāni) as there are (yāvantyaḥ) species of living beings (jīvajātayaḥ). Maheśvara (maheśvaraḥ) knows (vijānāti) all (akhilān) their variations (bhedān). As many postures as living beings
[√jñā - to know]		 There are as many postures as there are types of living being. Maheśvara knows all their varieties.
catur-āśīti-lakṣāṇām ekaikaṃ samudāhṛtam |
ataḥ śivena pīṭhānāṃ ṣoḍaśonaṃ śataṃ kṛtam ||5||

De-sandhi:
catuḥ-aśīti-lakṣāṇām ekaikam sam-ud-āhṛtam |
ataḥ śivena pīṭhānām ṣoḍaśa-ūnam śatam kṛtam ||		 catuḥ-aśīti-lakṣāṇām = of 84 hundred-thousands (gen. pl.)
ekaikam = one each (nom.)
sam-ud-āhṛtam = was selected (PPP, nom.)
ataḥ = therefore
śivena = by Shiva (inst.)
pīṭhānām = of postures (gen. pl.)
ṣoḍaśa-ūnam = diminished by sixteen
śatam = hundred (nom.)
kṛtam = was made (PPP, nom.)		 From the 8,400,000 (caturaśītilakṣāṇām), one each (ekaikam) was selected (samudāhṛtam); therefore (ataḥ) Śiva (śivena) made (kṛtam) a hundred diminished by sixteen (ṣoḍaśonaṃ śatam) postures (pīṭhānām). 84 primary postures selected
[√kṛ - to make, √ūn - to lack/lessen]		 Śiva taught the 84 lakh positions one by one, then made them 84.
āsanebhyaḥ samastebhyo dvayam etad udāhṛtam |
ekaṃ siddhāsanaṃ proktaṃ dvitīyaṃ kamalāsanam ||6||

De-sandhi:
āsanebhyaḥ samastebhyaḥ dvayam etad ud-āhṛtam |
ekam siddha-āsanam proktam dvitīyam kamala-āsanam ||		 āsanebhyaḥ = from the postures (abl. pl.)
samastebhyaḥ = from all (abl. pl.)
dvayam = pair (nom.)
etad = this (nom.)
ud-āhṛtam = is selected/mentioned (PPP, nom.)
ekam = first (nom.)
siddha-āsanam = perfect posture (TP, nom.)
proktam = is called (PPP, nom.)
dvitīyam = second (nom.)
kamala-āsanam = lotus posture (TP, nom.)		 From all (samastebhyaḥ) the postures (āsanebhyaḥ), this pair (dvayam etad) is selected (udāhṛtam): the first (ekam) is called (proktam) the perfect posture (siddhāsanam), the second (dvitīyam) the lotus posture (kamalāsanam). Two chief postures: Siddha and Padma
[√sidh - to accomplish, √pad - to go/be]		 From all the postures this pair is taught: one called siddhāsana, "the adept's posture", the other kamalāsana, "the lotus posture".
yoni-sthānakam āṅghri-mūla-ghaṭitaṃ kṛtvā dṛḍhaṃ vinyasen
meḍhre pādam athaikam eva hṛdaye dhṛtvā hanuṃ su-sthiram |
sthāṇuḥ saṃyamitendriyoacala-dṛśā paśyed bhruvor antaraṃ
hy etan mokṣa-kapāṭa-bheda-janakaṃ siddhāsanaṃ procyate ||7||

De-sandhi:
yoni-sthānakam āṅghri-mūla-ghaṭitam kṛtvā dṛḍham vinyaset
meḍhre pādam atha ekam eva hṛdaye dhṛtvā hanum su-sthiram |
sthāṇuḥ saṃyamita-indriyaḥ acala-dṛśā paśyet bhruvoḥ antaram
hi etat mokṣa-kapāṭa-bheda-janakam siddha-āsanam procyate ||		 yoni-sthānakam = perineum (acc.)
āṅghri-mūla-ghaṭitam = connected with heel (acc.)
kṛtvā = having made (abs.)
dṛḍham = firmly (adv.)
vinyaset = should place (opt.)
meḍhre = on genitals (loc.)
pādam = foot (acc.)
atha = then
ekam eva = one only
hṛdaye = at heart (loc.)
dhṛtvā = having held (abs.)
hanum = chin (acc.)
su-sthiram = very firmly
sthāṇuḥ = motionless (nom.)
saṃyamita-indriyaḥ = with controlled senses (BV, nom.)
acala-dṛśā = with steady gaze (inst.)
paśyet = should look (opt.)
bhruvoḥ = of eyebrows (gen. dual)
antaram = between (acc.)
mokṣa-kapāṭa-bheda-janakam = causing breaking of door to liberation (BV, nom.)
siddha-āsanam = perfect posture (nom.)
procyate = is called (pass.)		 Having firmly (dṛḍham kṛtvā) placed the root of the heel (āṅghrimūlaghaṭitam) at the perineum (yonisthānakam), one should place (vinyaset) the other foot (pādam ekam eva) on the genitals (meḍhre). Having held (dhṛtvā) the chin (hanum) very firmly (su-sthiram) at the heart (hṛdaye), motionless (sthāṇuḥ), with controlled senses (saṃyamitendriyaḥ) and steady gaze (acaladṛśā), one should look (paśyet) between the eyebrows (bhruvoḥ antaram). This (etat) is called (procyate) the perfect posture (siddhāsanam), which breaks open the door to liberation (mokṣakapāṭabhedajanakam). Siddhasana description and benefits
[√kṛ - to make, √dhṛ - to hold, √paś - to see, √bhid - to break]		 [The yogi] should place the root of the heel at the perineum and firmly place the other foot on the penis. He should hold his jaw very steadily on his chest and, motionless, with his sense-organs restrained, stare with fixed gaze at the space between the eyebrows. This is called the adept's posture. It opens the doorway to liberation.
vāmorūpari dakṣiṇaṃ ca caraṇaṃ saṃsthāpya vāmaṃ tathā
yāmyorūpari paścimena vidhinā dhṛtvā karābhyāṃ dṛḍham |
aṅguṣṭhe hṛdaye nidhāya cibukam nāsāgramālokayet
etad dhādhivikārahāri yamināṃ padmāsanaṃ procyate ||8||

De-sandhi:
vāma-ūru-upari dakṣiṇam ca caraṇam saṃsthāpya vāmam tathā
yāmya-ūru-upari paścimena vidhinā dhṛtvā karābhyām dṛḍham |
aṅguṣṭhe hṛdaye nidhāya cibukam nāsā-agram ālokayet
etat vyādhi-vikāra-hāri yaminām padma-āsanam procyate ||		 vāma-ūru-upari = upon left thigh
dakṣiṇam = right (acc.)
caraṇam = foot (acc.)
saṃsthāpya = having placed (abs.)
vāmam = left (acc.)
tathā = likewise
yāmya-ūru-upari = upon right thigh
paścimena vidhinā = by back method (inst.)
dhṛtvā = having held (abs.)
karābhyām = with both hands (inst. du.)
dṛḍham = firmly (adv.)
aṅguṣṭhe = big toes (loc.)
hṛdaye = at heart (loc.)
nidhāya = placing (abs.)
cibukam = chin (acc.)
nāsā-agram = tip of nose (acc.)
ālokayet = should gaze (opt.)
vyādhi-vikāra-hāri = remover of disease-disorder (BV)
yaminām = of self-restrained (gen. pl.)
padma-āsanam = lotus-posture (nom.)
procyate = is called (pass.)		 Having placed (saṃsthāpya) the right foot (dakṣiṇaṃ caraṇam) on the left thigh (vāmorūpari), and likewise (tathā) the left (vāmam) on the right thigh (yāmyorūpari), and having firmly held (dhṛtvā dṛḍham) the big toes (aṅguṣṭhe) with both hands (karābhyām) by the back method (paścimena vidhinā), placing (nidhāya) the chin (cibukam) at the heart (hṛdaye), one should gaze (ālokayet) at the tip of the nose (nāsāgram). This (etat) is called (procyate) the lotus posture (padmāsanam) of the self-restrained (yaminām), which removes disease and disorder (vyādhivikārahāri). Padmasana description and benefits
[√sthā - to place, √dhṛ - to hold, √lok - to gaze, √hṛ - to remove]		 [The yogi] should place his right foot on his left thigh and his left foot on his right thigh, with his hands going behind his back hold firmly on to his big toes, place his chin on his chest and look at the tip of his nose. This is called the lotus posture. It removes sicknesses and diseases from those who restrain themselves.
ṣaṭcakraṃ ṣoḍaśādhāraṃ trailokyaṃ vyomapañcakam |
svadehe ye na jānanti kathaṃ sidhyanti yoginaḥ ||9||

De-sandhi:
ṣaṭ-cakram ṣoḍaśa-ādhāram trai-lokyam vyoma-pañcakam |
sva-dehe ye na jānanti katham sidhyanti yoginaḥ ||		 ṣaṭ-cakram = six chakras (acc.)
ṣoḍaśa-ādhāram = sixteen supports (acc.)
trai-lokyam = three worlds (acc.)
vyoma-pañcakam = five voids (acc.)
sva-dehe = in own body (loc.)
ye = who (nom. pl.)
jānanti = know (3rd pl.)
katham = how
sidhyanti = attain perfection (3rd pl.)
yoginaḥ = yogis (nom. pl.)		 Those who (ye) do not know (na jānanti) in their own bodies (svadehe) the six chakras (ṣaṭcakram), sixteen supports (ṣoḍaśādhāram), three worlds (trailokyam), and five voids (vyomapañcakam)—how (katham) can yogis (yoginaḥ) attain perfection (sidhyanti)? Knowledge of six chakras essential for perfection
[√jñā - to know, √sidh - to perfect]		 Those who do not recognise the six cakras, sixteen supports, three worlds [and] five voids in their own bodies, how can they become perfected yogis?
ekastambhaṃ navadvāraṃ triśūnyaṃ pañcadaivatam |
svadehe ye na jānanti kathaṃ sidhyanti yoginaḥ ||10||

De-sandhi:
eka-stambham nava-dvāram tri-śūnyam pañca-daivatam |
sva-dehe ye na jānanti katham sidhyanti yoginaḥ ||		 eka-stambham = one pillar (acc.)
nava-dvāram = nine gates (acc.)
tri-śūnyam = three voids (acc.)
pañca-daivatam = five deities (acc.)
sva-dehe = in own body (loc.)
ye = who (nom. pl.)
jānanti = know (3rd pl.)
katham = how
sidhyanti = attain perfection (3rd pl.)
yoginaḥ = yogis (nom. pl.)		 Those who (ye) do not know (na jānanti) in their own bodies (svadehe) the single pillar (ekastambham), nine gates (navadvāram), three voids (triśūnyam), and five deities (pañcadaivatam)—how (katham) can yogis (yoginaḥ) attain perfection (sidhyanti)? Knowledge of body's pillars and gates essential
[√jñā - to know, √sidh - to perfect]		 Those who do not recognise the single column, the nine doors, the three voids [and] the five deities in their own bodies, how can they become perfected yogis?
caturdalaṃ syād ādhāraṃ svādhiṣṭhānaṃ ca ṣaḍdalam |
nābhau daśadalaṃ padmaṃ sūryasaṅkhyādalaṃ hṛdi ||11||

De-sandhi:
catuḥ-dalam syāt ādhāram svādhiṣṭhānam ca ṣaṭ-dalam |
nābhau daśa-dalam padmam sūrya-saṅkhyā-dalam hṛdi ||		 catuḥ-dalam = four-petaled (nom.)
syāt = is (opt.)
ādhāram = ādhāra/mūlādhāra (nom.)
svādhiṣṭhānam = svādhiṣṭhāna (nom.)
ṣaṭ-dalam = six-petaled (nom.)
nābhau = at navel (loc.)
daśa-dalam = ten-petaled (nom.)
padmam = lotus (nom.)
sūrya-saṅkhyā-dalam = sun-number-petaled (nom.)
hṛdi = at heart (loc.)		 The Base (ādhāram) is (syāt) four-petaled (caturdalam), the Svādhiṣṭhāna (svādhiṣṭhānam) is six-petaled (ṣaḍdalam). At the navel (nābhau) is a ten-petaled (daśadalam) lotus (padmam), and at the heart (hṛdi) one with the number of the sun's petals (sūryasaṅkhyādalam, i.e., twelve). Four lower chakras with petal counts
[√as - to be (syāt)]		 The Base (ādhāra) had four petals and the Svādhiṣṭhāna has six petals. In the navel there is a lotus with ten petals [and the lotus] in the heart has twelve petals.
kaṇṭhe syāt ṣoḍaśadalaṃ bhrumadhye dvidalaṃ tathā |
sahasradalam ākhyātam brahmarandhre mahāpathe ||12||

De-sandhi:
kaṇṭhe syāt ṣoḍaśa-dalam bhru-madhye dvi-dalam tathā |
sahasra-dalam ākhyātam brahma-randhre mahā-pathe ||		 kaṇṭhe = in throat (loc.)
syāt = is (opt.)
ṣoḍaśa-dalam = sixteen-petaled (nom.)
bhru-madhye = between eyebrows (loc.)
dvi-dalam = two-petaled (nom.)
tathā = likewise
sahasra-dalam = thousand-petaled (nom.)
ākhyātam = called (PPP, nom.)
brahma-randhre = at brahmarandhra (loc.)
mahā-pathe = on great path (loc.)		 In the throat (kaṇṭhe) is (syāt) a sixteen-petaled (ṣoḍaśadalam) [lotus], and likewise (tathā) between the eyebrows (bhrumadhye) a two-petaled one (dvidalam). The thousand-petaled (sahasradalam) [lotus] is said (ākhyātam) to be at the brahmarandhra (brahmarandhre), on the great path (mahāpathe). Three upper chakras with petal counts
[√as - to be (syāt), √khyā - to call]		 In the throat is [a lotus] with sixteen petals and between the eyebrows [a lotus] with two petals. [The lotus] that is taught to have a thousand petals is in the great pathway at the aperture of Brahman.
ādhāraṃ prathamaṃ cakraṃ svādhiṣṭhānaṃ dvitīyakam |
yonisthānaṃ tayoḥ madhye kāmarūpam pracakṣate ||13||

De-sandhi:
ādhāram prathamam cakram svādhiṣṭhānam dvitīyakam |
yoni-sthānam tayoḥ madhye kāma-rūpam pracakṣate ||		 ādhāram = ādhāra/mūlādhāra (nom.)
prathamam = first (nom.)
cakram = cakra (nom.)
svādhiṣṭhānam = svādhiṣṭhāna (nom.)
dvitīyakam = second (nom.)
yoni-sthānam = yonisthāna/perineal region (acc.)
tayoḥ = of those two (gen. dual)
madhye = in middle (loc.)
kāma-rūpam = kāmarūpa (acc.)
pracakṣate = they call (3rd pl. mid.)		 The Base (ādhāram) is the first (prathamam) chakra (cakram), Svādhiṣṭhāna (svādhiṣṭhānam) is the second (dvitīyakam). The perineal region (yonisthānam) in the middle (madhye) of those two (tayoḥ) is called (pracakṣate) Kāmarūpa (kāmarūpam). Location of Kāmarūpa between two chakras
[√cakṣ - to call/see]		 The Base (ādhāram) is the first cakra, Svādhiṣṭhāna is second. The perineal region (yonisthānam) between them is called Kāmarūpa.
adhomukhaṃ gudasthāne paṅkajam yaccaturdalam |
tanmadhye procyate yoniḥ kāmākhyā siddhavanditā ||14||

De-sandhi:
adhaḥ-mukham guda-sthāne paṅkajam yat catuḥ-dalam |
tat-madhye procyate yoniḥ kāmākhyā siddha-vanditā ||		 adhaḥ-mukham = downward-facing (nom.)
guda-sthāne = at place of anus (loc.)
paṅkajam = lotus (nom.)
yat = which (rel. pron.)
catuḥ-dalam = four-petaled (nom.)
tat-madhye = in middle of that (loc.)
procyate = is proclaimed (pass.)
yoniḥ = yoni/source (nom.)
kāmākhyā = called Kāmākhyā (nom.)
siddha-vanditā = by-Siddhas-worshipped (nom.)		 The downward-facing (adhomukham) four-petaled (yaccaturdalam) lotus (paṅkajam) at the location of the anus (gudasthāne)—in its middle (tanmadhye) is proclaimed (procyate) the yoni (yoniḥ), called Kāmākhyā (kāmākhyā), worshipped by the Siddhas (siddhavanditā). Mūlādhāra lotus with yoni Kāmākhyā
[√vand - to worship]		 The lotus with four petals at the location of the anus faces downwards. The yoni in its middle is called Kāmā and is worshipped by adepts.
yonimadhye mahāliṅgam paścimābhimukhaṃ sthitam |
mastake maṇivad bhinnam yo jānāti sa yogavit ||15||

De-sandhi:
yoni-madhye mahā-liṅgam paścima-abhimukham sthitam |
mastake maṇi-vat bhinnam yaḥ jānāti sa yoga-vit ||		 yoni-madhye = in middle of yoni (loc.)
mahā-liṅgam = great liṅga (nom.)
paścima-abhimukham = facing westward (nom.)
sthitam = situated (PPP, nom.)
mastake = at top (loc.)
maṇi-vat = like jewel (adv.)
bhinnam = split (PPP, nom.)
yaḥ = who (rel. pron.)
jānāti = knows (3rd sg.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 In the middle of the yoni (yonimadhye) is situated (sthitam) the great liṅga (mahāliṅgam) facing rearward (paścimābhimukham), split (bhinnam) at the top (mastake) like a jewel (maṇivat). He who (yo) knows (jānāti) this is (sa) a knower of yoga (yogavit). Great liṅga in yoni facing rearward
[√jñā - to know, √sthā - to stand, √bhid - to split]		 In the middle of the yoni is situated a great liṅga facing rearwards. He who knows the blown [lotus] in the head that is like a jewel knows yoga.
taptacāmīkarābhāsam taḍillekheva visphurat |
caturasram puram vahner adho meḍhāt pratiṣṭitam ||16||

De-sandhi:
tapta-cāmīkara-ābhāsam taḍit-lekhā iva visphurat |
