catuḥ-asram puram vahneḥ adhaḥ meḍhāt pratiṣṭhitam ||		 tapta-cāmīkara-ābhāsam = heated-gold-shining (nom.)
taḍit-lekhā iva = like lightning-streak
visphurat = flashing (pres. part.)
catuḥ-asram = four-cornered/square (nom.)
puram = city (nom.)
vahneḥ = of Fire (gen.)
adhaḥ = below
meḍhāt = from penis (abl.)
pratiṣṭhitam = situated (PPP, nom.)		 Below (adhaḥ) the penis (meḍhāt) is situated (pratiṣṭhitam) the four-cornered (caturasram) city (puram) of Fire (vahneḥ), shining (ābhāsam) like heated gold (taptacāmīkara), flashing (visphurat) like a streak of lightning (taḍillekheva). Fire element chakra below genitals
[√sphur - to flash/sparkle, √pra-sthā - to be established]		 Below the penis is situated the four-sided abode of fire, looking like burnished gold, glittering like a streak of lightning.
yat samādhau paraṃ jyotir anantam viśvatomukham |
tasmin dṛṣṭe mahāyoge yātāyātam na vidyate ||17||

De-sandhi:
yat samādhau param jyotiḥ anantam viśvataḥ-mukham |
tasmin dṛṣṭe mahā-yoge yāta-āyātam na vidyate ||		 yat = which (rel. pron.)
samādhau = in samādhi (loc.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
anantam = infinite (nom.)
viśvataḥ-mukham = all-directions-facing (nom.)
tasmin = in that (loc.)
dṛṣṭe = when seen (PPP, loc.)
mahā-yoge = in great yoga (loc.)
yāta-āyātam = going-coming (nom.)
vidyate = is found (pass.)		 That (yat) supreme (param) light (jyotir) in samādhi (samādhau), infinite (anantam) and facing all directions (viśvatomukham)—when that (tasmin) is seen (dṛṣṭe) in the great yoga (mahāyoge), there is (vidyate) no (na) more coming and going (yātāyātam). Supreme light ends rebirth cycle
[√dṛś - to see, √yā - to go, √ā-yā - to come, √vid - to find]		 When in absorption the supreme boundless light [shining] in all directions is seen [and] the great yoga has arisen, then there is no [more] coming and going [for the yogi].
svaśabdena bhavet prāṇaḥ adhiṣṭhānam ca tadāśrayaḥ |
svādhiṣṭhānam ataḥ cakram meḍhram eva nigadyate ||18||

De-sandhi:
sva-śabdena bhavet prāṇaḥ adhiṣṭhānam ca tat-āśrayaḥ |
sva-adhiṣṭhānam ataḥ cakram meḍhram eva nigadyate ||		 sva-śabdena = by syllable 'sva' (inst.)
bhavet = becomes (opt.)
prāṇaḥ = prāṇa (nom.)
adhiṣṭhānam = foundation (nom.)
tat-āśrayaḥ = that-as-support (nom.)
sva-adhiṣṭhānam = svādhiṣṭhāna (nom.)
ataḥ = therefore
cakram = chakra (nom.)
meḍhram = genital region (acc.)
eva = exactly
nigadyate = is called (pass.)		 By the syllable 'sva' (svaśabdena) prāṇa (prāṇaḥ) is indicated (bhavet), the foundation (adhiṣṭhānam) and that which rests upon it (tadāśrayaḥ). Therefore (ataḥ) the chakra (cakram) at the genital region (meḍhram eva) is called (nigadyate) Svādhiṣṭhāna (svādhiṣṭhānam). Svādhiṣṭhāna chakra etymology
[√bhū - to become, √ā-śri - to support/rest upon]		 Prāṇa arises with the word sva and the adhiṣṭhāna (base??) is its support. Because of this the penis is called the Svādhiṣṭhāna cakra.
maṇivat tantunā yatra vāyunā pūryate vapuḥ |
tan nābhimaṇḍalaṃ cakram procyate maṇipūrakam ||19||

De-sandhi:
maṇi-vat tantunā yatra vāyunā pūryate vapuḥ |
tat nābhi-maṇḍalam cakram procyate maṇi-pūrakam ||		 maṇi-vat = like jewel (adv.)
tantunā = by thread (inst.)
yatra = where (rel. adv.)
vāyunā = by air/breath (inst.)
pūryate = is filled (pass.)
vapuḥ = body (nom.)
nābhi-maṇḍalam = navel-region (nom.)
cakram = chakra (nom.)
procyate = is called (pass.)
maṇi-pūrakam = jewel-city (nom.)		 Where (yatra) the body (vapuḥ) is filled (pūryate) by breath (vāyunā) like jewels (maṇivat) on a thread (tantunā), that (tan) navel region (nābhimaṇḍalam) chakra (cakram) is called (procyate) Maṇipūraka (maṇipūrakam). Maṇipūraka chakra at navel
[√pṝ - to fill, √vac - to call/speak]		 The navel region (nābhimaṇḍalam), where the body is filled with breath like a jewel by a thread, that navel region is called the Maṇipūraka cakra.
dvādaśāre mahācakre puṇyapāpaniyantritaḥ |
tāvat jīvo bhramati eva yāvat tattvam na vindati ||20||

De-sandhi:
dvādaśa-āre mahā-cakre puṇya-pāpa-niyantritaḥ |
tāvat jīvaḥ bhramati eva yāvat tattvam na vindati ||		 dvādaśa-āre = in twelve-spoked (loc.)
mahā-cakre = in great chakra (loc.)
puṇya-pāpa-niyantritaḥ = merit-sin-controlled (nom.)
tāvat = so long (correlative adv.)
jīvaḥ = soul (nom.)
bhramati = wanders (pres.)
eva = indeed
yāvat = as long as (correlative adv.)
tattvam = essence/reality (acc.)
vindati = finds/realizes (pres.)		 In the twelve-spoked (dvādaśāre) great chakra (mahācakre), controlled by merit and sin (puṇyapāpaniyantritaḥ), the soul (jīvaḥ) wanders (bhramati) for as long (tāvat eva) as (yāvat) it does not find (na vindati) reality (tattvam). Twelve-spoked chakra and karma
[√bhram - to wander, √vid - to know/find]		 Bound by its good and bad deeds, the vital principle (jīvaḥ) wanders in the great cakra (anāhata?) with twelve spokes until it finds the ultimate reality (tattvam).
ūrdhvam meḍhrād adho nābheḥ kando yoniḥ khagāṇḍavat |
tatra nāḍyaḥ samutpannāḥ sahasrāṇi dvisaptatiḥ ||21||

De-sandhi:
ūrdhvam meḍhrāt adhaḥ nābheḥ kandaḥ yoniḥ khaga-āṇḍa-vat |
tatra nāḍyaḥ samutpannāḥ sahasrāṇi dvi-saptatiḥ ||		 ūrdhvam = above
meḍhrāt = from genitals (abl.)
adhaḥ = below
nābheḥ = from navel (abl.)
kandaḥ = bulb/knot (nom.)
yoniḥ = source (nom.)
khaga-āṇḍa-vat = bird-egg-like
tatra = therein
nāḍyaḥ = channels (nom. pl.)
samutpannāḥ = have arisen (PPP, nom. pl.)
sahasrāṇi = thousands (nom. pl.)
dvi-saptatiḥ = two-seventy/seventy-two (nom.)		 Above the genitals (meḍhrāt) and below the navel (nābheḥ) is the bulb (kandaḥ), the source (yoniḥ), like a bird's egg (khagāṇḍavat). Therein (tatra) have arisen (samutpannāḥ) 72,000 (dvisaptatiḥ sahasrāṇi) channels (nāḍyaḥ). 72,000 nāḍīs from kanda bulb
[√ut-pad - to arise, √sthā - to be situated]		 Above the penis and below the navel is a bulb, a yoni like a bird's egg. In it arise the 72,000 channels.
teṣu nāḍīsahasreṣu dvisaptatiḥ udāhṛtāḥ |
pradhānāḥ prāṇavāhinyo bhūyaḥ tatra daśa smṛtāḥ ||22||

De-sandhi:
teṣu nāḍī-sahasreṣu dvi-saptatiḥ udāhṛtāḥ |
pradhānāḥ prāṇa-vāhinyaḥ bhūyaḥ tatra daśa smṛtāḥ ||		 teṣu = among them (loc. pl.)
nāḍī-sahasreṣu = in thousands of channels (loc. pl.)
dvi-saptatiḥ = seventy-two (nom.)
udāhṛtāḥ = are declared (PPP, nom. pl.)
pradhānāḥ = principal (nom. pl.)
prāṇa-vāhinyaḥ = carrying life-force (nom. pl. fem.)
bhūyaḥ = again/moreover
tatra = among them
daśa = ten
smṛtāḥ = are remembered (PPP, nom. pl.)		 Among those thousands of channels (nāḍīsahasreṣu), 72 (dvisaptatiḥ) are declared (udāhṛtāḥ) to be principal (pradhānāḥ), carrying prāṇa (prāṇavāhinyaḥ); moreover (bhūyaḥ), among them (tatra), ten (daśa) are remembered (smṛtāḥ). 72 principal nāḍīs, 10 most important
[√vah - to carry, √smṛ - to remember]		 Among those thousands of channels, 72 important ones are said to carry prāṇa; additionally, of those ten are taught.
iḍā ca piṅgalā ca eva suṣumṇā ca tṛtīyakā |
gāndhārī hastijihvā ca pūṣā ca eva yaśasvinī ||23||

De-sandhi:
iḍā ca piṅgalā ca eva suṣumṇā ca tṛtīyakā |
gāndhārī hastijihvā ca pūṣā ca eva yaśasvinī ||		 iḍā = Iḍā (nom.)
piṅgalā = Piṅgalā (nom.)
eva = indeed
suṣumṇā = Suṣumṇā (nom.)
tṛtīyakā = third one (nom. fem.)
gāndhārī = Gāndhārī (nom.)
hastijihvā = Hastijihvā (nom.)
pūṣā = Pūṣā (nom.)
yaśasvinī = Yaśasvinī (nom.)		 Iḍā (iḍā) and Piṅgalā (piṅgalā), and indeed (eva) Suṣumṇā (suṣumṇā) the third one (tṛtīyakā); Gāndhārī (gāndhārī), Hastijihvā (hastijihvā), and Pūṣā (pūṣā), and indeed (eva) Yaśasvinī (yaśasvinī). Names of ten nāḍīs (part 1 of 2)
[List: Iḍā, Piṅgalā, Suṣumṇā, Gāndhārī, Hastijihvā, Pūṣā, Yaśasvinī]		 Iḍā and Piṅgalā and Suṣumṇā the third; Gāndhārī and Hastijihvā and Pūṣā and Yaśasvinī;
alambuṣā kuhūś ca eva śaṅkhinī daśamī smṛtā |
evaṃ nāḍīmayaṃ cakraṃ jñātavyaṃ yogibhiḥ sadā ||24||

De-sandhi:
alambuṣā kuhūḥ ca eva śaṅkhinī daśamī smṛtā |
evam nāḍī-mayam cakram jñātavyam yogibhiḥ sadā ||		 alambuṣā = Alambuṣā (nom.)
kuhūḥ = Kuhū (nom.)
eva = indeed
śaṅkhinī = Śaṅkhinī (nom.)
daśamī = tenth (nom. fem.)
smṛtā = remembered (PPP, nom. fem.)
evam = thus
nāḍī-mayam = made of nāḍīs (nom.)
cakram = wheel/system (nom.)
jñātavyam = should be known (gerundive, nom.)
yogibhiḥ = by yogis (inst. pl.)
sadā = always		 Alambuṣā (alambuṣā), Kuhū (kuhūḥ), and indeed (eva) Śaṅkhinī (śaṅkhinī) is remembered (smṛtā) as the tenth (daśamī). Thus (evam) this wheel-system (cakram) made of nāḍīs (nāḍīmayam) should always be known (jñātavyam sadā) by yogis (yogibhiḥ). Names of ten nāḍīs (part 2 of 2)
[√jñā - to know, √smṛ - to remember; List: Alambuṣā, Kuhū, Śaṅkhinī]		 Alambuṣā and Kuhū; Śaṅkhinī is taught as the tenth. Yogis should always recognise the system of the channels to be thus.
iḍā vāme sthitā bhāge piṅgalā dakṣiṇe sthitā |
suṣumṇā madhyadeśe tu gāndhārī vāmacakṣuṣi ||25||

De-sandhi:
iḍā vāme sthitā bhāge piṅgalā dakṣiṇe sthitā |
suṣumṇā madhya-deśe tu gāndhārī vāma-cakṣuṣi ||		 iḍā = Iḍā (nom.)
vāme = on left (loc.)
sthitā = situated (PPP, nom. fem.)
bhāge = side (loc.)
piṅgalā = Piṅgalā (nom.)
dakṣiṇe = on right (loc.)
suṣumṇā = Suṣumṇā (nom.)
madhya-deśe = in middle region (loc.)
gāndhārī = Gāndhārī (nom.)
vāma-cakṣuṣi = in left eye (loc.)		 Iḍā (iḍā) is situated (sthitā) on the left side (vāme bhāge), Piṅgalā (piṅgalā) is situated on the right (dakṣiṇe sthitā); Suṣumṇā (suṣumṇā) is in the middle region (madhyadeśe), Gāndhārī (gāndhārī) in the left eye (vāmacakṣuṣi). Location of main nāḍīs in body
[√sthā - to stand/be situated]		 Iḍā is situated on the left side, Piṅgalā on the right. Suṣumṇā is in the central region, Gāndhārī in the left eye.
dakṣiṇe hastijihvā ca pūṣā karṇe ca dakṣiṇe |
yaśasvinī vāmakarṇe hy ānane cāpy alambuṣā ||26||

De-sandhi:
dakṣiṇe hastijihvā ca pūṣā karṇe ca dakṣiṇe |
yaśasvinī vāma-karṇe hi ānane ca api alambuṣā ||		 dakṣiṇe = in right [eye] (loc.)
hastijihvā = Hastijihvā (nom.)
pūṣā = Pūṣā (nom.)
karṇe = in ear (loc.)
dakṣiṇe = in right (loc.)
yaśasvinī = Yaśasvinī (nom.)
vāma-karṇe = in left ear (loc.)
ānane = in mouth (loc.)
alambuṣā = Alambuṣā (nom.)		 Hastijihvā (hastijihvā) is in the right [eye] (dakṣiṇe), and Pūṣā (pūṣā) in the right ear (karṇe dakṣiṇe). Yaśasvinī (yaśasvinī) is in the left ear (vāmakarṇe), and also (api) Alambuṣā (alambuṣā) in the mouth (ānane). Location of nāḍīs in sense organs
[√sthā - to be situated (implied)]		 In the right [eye] is Hastijihvā and Pūṣā is in the right ear. Yaśasvinī is in the left ear and Alambuṣā is in the mouth.
kuhūḥ ca liṅgadehe tu mūlasthāne śaṅkhinī |
evaṃ dvāram samāśritya tiṣṭhanti daśa nāḍikāḥ ||27||

De-sandhi:
kuhūḥ ca liṅga-dehe tu mūla-sthāne śaṅkhinī |
evam dvāram samāśritya tiṣṭhanti daśa nāḍikāḥ ||		 kuhūḥ = Kuhū (nom.)
liṅga-dehe = in genital area (loc.)
mūla-sthāne = in root-place (loc.)
śaṅkhinī = Śaṅkhinī (nom.)
evam = thus/so
dvāram = door (acc.)
samāśritya = having been placed (abs.)
tiṣṭhanti = stay (3rd pl.)
daśa = ten
nāḍikāḥ = nāḍīs (nom. pl.)		 Kuhū (kuhūḥ) is in the genital area (liṅgadehe), and Śaṅkhinī (śaṅkhinī) at the root-place (mūlasthāne). Thus (evam), having been placed (samāśritya) at doors (dvāram), the ten nāḍīs (daśa nāḍikāḥ) remain (tiṣṭhanti). Final locations: ten nāḍīs at body gates
[√ā-śri - to take shelter/rest, √sthā - to stand]		 Kuhū is in the region of the penis and Śaṅkhini is at the bottom. The ten channels are thus [each] situated at an orifice.
satatam prāṇavāhinyaḥ tisro nāḍyaḥ prakīrtitāḥ |
iḍāpiṅgalāsuṣumṇāḥ soma-sūrya-agni-devatāḥ ||28||

De-sandhi:
satatam prāṇa-vāhinyaḥ tisraḥ nāḍyaḥ prakīrtitāḥ |
iḍā-piṅgalā-suṣumṇāḥ soma-sūrya-agni-devatāḥ ||		 satatam = always/constantly (adv.)
prāṇa-vāhinyaḥ = carrying life-force (nom. pl. fem.)
tisraḥ = three (nom. pl. fem.)
nāḍyaḥ = channels (nom. pl. fem.)
prakīrtitāḥ = are proclaimed (PPP, nom. pl. fem.)
iḍā-piṅgalā-suṣumṇāḥ = Iḍā, Piṅgalā and Suṣumṇā (dvandva, nom. pl. fem.)
soma-sūrya-agni-devatāḥ = having Soma, Sūrya and Agni as deities (BV, nom. pl. fem.)		 Three channels (tisraḥ nāḍyaḥ) constantly (satatam) carrying prāṇa (prāṇavāhinyaḥ) are proclaimed (prakīrtitāḥ): Iḍā, Piṅgalā and Suṣumṇā (iḍāpiṅgalāsuṣumṇāḥ), having Soma, Sūrya and Agni as their deities (somasūryāgnidevatāḥ). Three main nāḍīs with lunar/solar/fire deities
[√vah - to carry, √kīrt - to proclaim]		 The three channels Iḍā, Piṅgalā and Suṣumṇā are said to be constantly conveying prāṇa and have Soma, Sūrya and Agni as their deities.
iḍā vāme sthitā bhāge piṅgalā dakṣiṇe sthitā |
suṣumṇā madhyadeśe tu prāṇamārgāśrayāḥ smṛtāḥ ||29||

De-sandhi:
iḍā vāme sthitā bhāge piṅgalā dakṣiṇe sthitā |
suṣumṇā madhya-deśe tu prāṇa-mārga-āśrayāḥ smṛtāḥ ||		 iḍā = Iḍā (nom.)
vāme = on left (loc.)
sthitā = situated (PPP, nom. fem.)
bhāge = side (loc.)
piṅgalā = Piṅgalā (nom.)
dakṣiṇe = on right (loc.)
suṣumṇā = Suṣumṇā (nom.)
madhya-deśe = in middle region (loc.)
prāṇa-mārga-āśrayāḥ = source/base of path of prāṇa (nom. pl.)
smṛtāḥ = known/remembered (PPP, nom. pl.)		 Iḍā (iḍā) is situated (sthitā) on the left side (vāme bhāge), Piṅgalā (piṅgalā) on the right (dakṣiṇe sthitā), and Suṣumṇā (suṣumṇā) in the middle region (madhyadeśe)—they are remembered (smṛtāḥ) as the foundation (āśrayāḥ) of the path of prāṇa (prāṇamārgāḥ). Three nāḍīs as foundation of prāṇa pathways
[√sthā - to stand, √smṛ - to remember, √ā-śri - to support]		 Iḍā is situated on the left side, Piṅgalā on the right, Suṣumṇā in the central region. They are said to be the loci of the pathways of prāṇa.
prāṇo apānaḥ samānaś ca udāno vyāna eva ca |
nāgaḥ kūrmaḥ kṛkaraḥ ca devadatto dhanañjayaḥ ||30||

De-sandhi:
prāṇaḥ apānaḥ samānaḥ ca udānaḥ vyānaḥ eva ca |
nāgaḥ kūrmaḥ kṛkaraḥ ca devadattaḥ dhanañjayaḥ ||		 prāṇaḥ = Prāṇa (nom.)
apānaḥ = Apāna (nom.)
samānaḥ = Samāna (nom.)
udānaḥ = Udāna (nom.)
vyānaḥ = Vyāna (nom.)
eva = indeed
nāgaḥ = Nāga (nom.)
kūrmaḥ = Kūrma (nom.)
kṛkaraḥ = Kṛkara (nom.)
devadattaḥ = Devadatta (nom.)
dhanañjayaḥ = Dhanañjaya (nom.)		 Prāṇa (prāṇaḥ), Apāna (apānaḥ), and Samāna (samānaḥ); Udāna (udānaḥ) and indeed (eva) Vyāna (vyānaḥ); Nāga (nāgaḥ), Kūrma (kūrmaḥ), and Kṛkara (kṛkaraḥ); Devadatta (devadattaḥ) and Dhanañjaya (dhanañjayaḥ). Names of ten vital airs (vāyus)
[List: Prāṇa, Apāna, Samāna, Udāna, Vyāna, Nāga, Kūrma, Kṛkara, Devadatta, Dhanañjaya]		 Prāṇa, Apāna and Samāna; Udāna and Vyāna; Nāga, Kūrma and Kṛkara; Devadatta [and] Dhanañjaya.
hṛdi prāṇo vasen nityam apāno gudamaṇḍale |
samāno nābhideśe tu udānaḥ kaṇṭhamadhyagaḥ ||31||

De-sandhi:
hṛdi prāṇaḥ vaset nityam apānaḥ guda-maṇḍale |
samānaḥ nābhi-deśe tu udānaḥ kaṇṭha-madhya-gaḥ ||		 hṛdi = in heart (loc.)
prāṇaḥ = Prāṇa (nom.)
vaset = dwells (opt.)
nityam = always
apānaḥ = Apāna (nom.)
guda-maṇḍale = in anus-region (loc.)
samānaḥ = Samāna (nom.)
nābhi-deśe = in navel-region (loc.)
udānaḥ = Udāna (nom.)
kaṇṭha-madhya-gaḥ = located in middle of throat		 Prāṇa (prāṇaḥ) always dwells (vaset nityam) in the heart (hṛdi), Apāna (apānaḥ) in the anal region (gudamaṇḍale), Samāna (samānaḥ) in the navel region (nābhideśe), and Udāna (udānaḥ) is located in the middle of the throat (kaṇṭhamadhyagaḥ). Location of four main vāyus in body
[√vas - to dwell, √gam - to go]		 Prāṇa always dwells in the chest, Apāna in the region of the anus, Samāna in the region of the navel and Udāna in the throat.
vyāno vyāpī śarīreṣu pradhānāḥ pañca vāyavaḥ |
prāṇādhyāḥ pañca vikhyātā nāgādhyāḥ pañca vāyavaḥ ||32||

De-sandhi:
vyānaḥ vyāpī śarīreṣu pradhānāḥ pañca vāyavaḥ |
prāṇa-ādhyāḥ pañca vikhyātāḥ nāga-ādhyāḥ pañca vāyavaḥ ||		 vyānaḥ = Vyāna (nom.)
vyāpī = pervading (nom.)
śarīreṣu = bodies (loc. pl.)
pradhānāḥ = principal (nom. pl.)
pañca = five
vāyavaḥ = vital airs (nom. pl.)
prāṇa-ādhyāḥ = beginning with Prāṇa (nom. pl.)
pañca = five
vikhyātāḥ = well-known (PPP, nom. pl.)
nāga-ādhyāḥ = beginning with Nāga (nom. pl.)
pañca = five
vāyavaḥ = vital airs (nom. pl.)		 Vyāna (vyānaḥ) is all-pervading (vyāpī) in the body (śarīreṣu)—these are the five principal (pañca pradhānāḥ) vital airs (vāyavaḥ). Five (pañca) well-known (vikhyātāḥ) beginning with Prāṇa (prāṇādhyāḥ), and five (pañca) vital airs (vāyavaḥ) beginning with Nāga (nāgādhyāḥ). Vyāna pervades; 5+5 classification of vāyus
[√vi-āp - to pervade, √khyā - to be known]		 Vyāna pervades bodies. The five chief winds [are]: Five breaths beginning with Prāṇa are taught [and] five beginning with Nāga.
udgāre nāga ākhyātaḥ kūrma unmīlane smṛtaḥ |
kṛkaraḥ kṣutkṛj jñeyo devadatto vijṛmbhaṇe ||33||

De-sandhi:
udgāre nāgaḥ ākhyātaḥ kūrmaḥ unmīlane smṛtaḥ |
kṛkaraḥ kṣut-kṛt jñeyaḥ devadattaḥ vijṛmbhaṇe ||		 udgāre = in belching (loc.)
nāgaḥ = Nāga (nom.)
ākhyātaḥ = is called (PPP, nom.)
kūrmaḥ = Kūrma (nom.)
unmīlane = in opening eyes (loc.)
smṛtaḥ = is remembered (PPP, nom.)
kṛkaraḥ = Kṛkara (nom.)
kṣut-kṛt = causing hunger
jñeyaḥ = should be known
devadattaḥ = Devadatta (nom.)
vijṛmbhaṇe = in yawning (loc.)		 Nāga (nāgaḥ) is called (ākhyātaḥ) the one in belching (udgāre), Kūrma (kūrmaḥ) is remembered (smṛtaḥ) in opening the eyes (unmīlane). Kṛkara (kṛkaraḥ) should be known (jñeyaḥ) as causing hunger (kṣutkṛt), and Devadatta (devadattaḥ) in yawning (vijṛmbhaṇe). Functions of subsidiary vāyus
[√khyā - to call/declare, √smṛ - to remember, √jñā - to know]		 Nāga is taught [to be active] when belching, Kūrma when blinking. Kṛkara should be known to create thirst. Devadatta is operational when yawning.
na jahāti mṛtaṃ cāpi sarvavyāpī dhanañjayaḥ |
ete nāḍīsahasreṣu vartante jīvarūpiṇaḥ ||34||

De-sandhi:
na jahāti mṛtam ca api sarva-vyāpī dhanañjayaḥ |
ete nāḍī-sahasreṣu vartante jīva-rūpiṇaḥ ||		 na = not
jahāti = leaves (3rd sg.)
mṛtam = dead [body] (acc.)
sarva-vyāpī = all-pervading (nom.)
dhanañjayaḥ = Dhanañjaya (nom.)
ete = these (nom. pl.)
nāḍī-sahasreṣu = in thousands of nāḍīs (loc. pl.)
vartante = exist (3rd pl. mid.)
jīva-rūpiṇaḥ = having form of life (nom. pl.)		 The all-pervading (sarvavyāpī) Dhanañjaya (dhanañjayaḥ) does not leave (na jahāti) even (api) the dead body (mṛtam). These [ten vāyus] (ete) exist (vartante) in the thousands of nāḍīs (nāḍīsahasreṣu), having the form of the vital principle (jīvarūpiṇaḥ). Dhanañjaya remains even after death
[√hā - to leave, √vṛt - to exist/dwell]		 Dhanañjaya is all-pervasive and does not leave even a dead [body]. These [breaths] exist in the thousands of channels in the form of the vital principle (jīva).
prāṇāpānavaśo jīvo adhaś ca ūrdhvaṃ pradhāvati |
vāmadakṣiṇamārgeṇa cañcalatvāt na dṛśyate ||35||

De-sandhi:
prāṇa-apāna-vaśaḥ jīvaḥ adhaḥ ca ūrdhvam pradhāvati |
vāma-dakṣiṇa-mārgeṇa cañcalatvāt na dṛśyate ||		 prāṇa-apāna-vaśaḥ = under control of prāṇa and apāna (nom.)
jīvaḥ = vital principle (nom.)
adhaḥ = down
ūrdhvam = up
pradhāvati = rushes (3rd sg.)
vāma-dakṣiṇa-mārgeṇa = through left and right paths (inst.)
cañcalatvāt = due to restlessness (abl.)
dṛśyate = is seen (pass.)		 The vital principle (jīvaḥ), under the control of prāṇa and apāna (prāṇāpānavaśaḥ), rushes (pradhāvati) downward and upward (adhaḥ ca ūrdhvam) through the left and right paths (vāmadakṣiṇamārgeṇa). Due to its restlessness (cañcalatvāt), it is not seen (na dṛśyate). Jīva rushes up and down via prāṇa-apāna
[√dhāv - to rush/run, √dṛś - to see]		 Under the control of prāṇa and apāna the vital principle rushes up and down through the left and right paths. It is so restless that it is imperceptible.
ākṣipto bhujadaṇḍena yathoccalati kandukaḥ |
prāṇāpānasamākṣiptaḥ tadvaj jīvo na viśramet ||36||

De-sandhi:
ākṣiptaḥ bhuja-daṇḍena yathā uccalati kandukaḥ |
prāṇa-apāna-samākṣiptaḥ tadvat jīvaḥ na viśramet ||		 ākṣiptaḥ = tossed (PPP, nom.)
bhuja-daṇḍena = by arm/stick (inst.)
yathā = as/like
uccalati = jumps (3rd sg.)
kandukaḥ = ball (nom.)
prāṇa-apāna-samākṣiptaḥ = tossed about by prāṇa and apāna (PPP, nom.)
tadvat = likewise
jīvaḥ = vital principle (nom.)
viśramet = rests (opt.)		 Just as (yathā) a ball (kandukaḥ) tossed (ākṣiptaḥ) by a stick (bhujadaṇḍena) jumps up (uccalati), likewise (tadvat) the vital principle (jīvaḥ), tossed about by prāṇa and apāna (prāṇāpānasamākṣiptaḥ), does not rest (na viśramet). Ball analogy: jīva tossed by prāṇa-apāna
[√kṣip - to throw/toss, √ut-cal - to jump, √vi-śram - to rest]		 In the same way that a ball jumps when hit by a stick, so the vital principle, tossed about by prāṇa and apāna, does not rest.
rajjubaddho yathā śyeno gato api ākṛṣyate punaḥ |
guṇabaddhaḥ tathā jīvaḥ prāṇāpānena kṛṣyate ||37||

De-sandhi:
rajju-baddhaḥ yathā śyenaḥ gataḥ api ākṛṣyate punaḥ |
guṇa-baddhaḥ tathā jīvaḥ prāṇa-apānena kṛṣyate ||		 rajju-baddhaḥ = tied by rope (BV, nom.)
yathā = as/like
śyenaḥ = hawk (nom.)
gataḥ = moved (PPP, nom.)
ākṛṣyate = is pulled back (pass.)
punaḥ = again
guṇa-baddhaḥ = bound by guṇas (BV, nom.)
tathā = likewise
jīvaḥ = vital principle (nom.)
prāṇa-apānena = by prāṇa and apāna (inst.)
kṛṣyate = is pulled (pass.)		 Just as (yathā) a hawk (śyenaḥ) bound by a rope (rajjubaddhaḥ), even though it has flown away (gataḥ api), is pulled back (ākṛṣyate punaḥ), likewise (tathā) the vital principle (jīvaḥ), bound by the guṇas (guṇabaddhaḥ), is pulled (kṛṣyate) by prāṇa and apāna (prāṇāpānena). Hawk analogy: jīva bound by guṇas
[√bandh - to bind, √gam - to go, √kṛṣ - to pull/draw]		 In the same way that a hawk tied by a rope is pulled back when it moves, so the vital principle bound by the guṇas is pulled by prāṇa and apāna.
apānaḥ karṣati prāṇam prāṇaḥ apānam ca karṣati |
khagarajjuvad iti etau yaḥ jānāti sa yogavit ||38||

De-sandhi:
apānaḥ karṣati prāṇam prāṇaḥ apānam ca karṣati |
khaga-rajju-vat iti etau yaḥ jānāti sa yoga-vit ||		 apānaḥ = Apāna (nom.)
karṣati = pulls (3rd sg.)
prāṇam = Prāṇa (acc.)
prāṇaḥ = Prāṇa (nom.)
apānam = Apāna (acc.)
ca = and
khaga-rajju-vat = like bird-rope (simile)
iti = thus
etau = these two (nom. du.)
yaḥ = who (rel. pron.)
jānāti = knows (3rd sg.)
sa = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 Apāna (apānaḥ) pulls (karṣati) Prāṇa (prāṇam), and Prāṇa (prāṇaḥ) pulls (karṣati) Apāna (apānam). Thus (iti) these two (etau) are like a bird and rope (khagarajjuvat) — he who (yaḥ) knows (jānāti) this, he (sa) is a knower of yoga (yogavit). Prāṇa-apāna mutual relationship (bird-rope simile)
[√kṛṣ - to pull, √jñā - to know]		 Apāna pulls prāṇa and prāṇa pulls apāna. He who knows these two to be like a bird and a rope knows yoga.
hakāreṇa bahiryāti sakāreṇa viśetpunaḥ |
haṃsa haṃsa iti amum mantraṃ jīvaḥ japati sarvadā ||39||

De-sandhi:
ha-kāreṇa bahiḥ yāti sa-kāreṇa viśet punaḥ |
haṃsa haṃsa iti amum mantram jīvaḥ japati sarvadā ||		 ha-kāreṇa = with syllable 'ha' (inst.)
bahiḥ = out
yāti = goes (3rd sg.)
sa-kāreṇa = with syllable 'sa' (inst.)
viśet = enters (opt.)
punaḥ = again
haṃsa haṃsa = haṃsa haṃsa
iti = thus
amum = this (acc.)
mantram = mantra (acc.)
jīvaḥ = vital principle (nom.)
japati = repeats (3rd sg.)
sarvadā = always		 By the syllable 'ha' (hakāreṇa) it goes out (bahiryāti), by the syllable 'sa' (sakāreṇa) it enters (viśet) again (punaḥ). "Haṃsa haṃsa" (haṃsa haṃsa) — thus (iti) this (amum) mantra (mantram) the vital principle (jīvaḥ) repeats (japati) constantly (sarvadā). Haṃsa mantra of breath
[√yā - to go, √viś - to enter, √jap - to repeat]		 The vital principle goes out with the syllable ha [and] goes in and down with the syllable sa; it constantly repeats this mantra, haṃsa haṃsa.
ṣaṭśatāni divā rātrau sahasrāṇi ekaviṃśatiḥ |
etat saṅkhyānvitam mantram jīvaḥ japati sarvadā ||40||

De-sandhi:
ṣaṭ-śatāni divā rātrau sahasrāṇi eka-viṃśatiḥ |
etat saṅkhyā-anvitam mantram jīvaḥ japati sarvadā ||		 ṣaṭ-śatāni = six hundreds (nom. pl.)
divā = by day
rātrau = by night (loc.)
sahasrāṇi = thousands (nom. pl.)
eka-viṃśatiḥ = twenty-one (nom.)
etat = this (acc.)
saṅkhyā-anvitam = accompanied by number (acc.)
mantram = mantra (acc.)
jīvaḥ = vital principle (nom.)
japati = repeats (3rd sg.)
sarvadā = always		 Twenty-one (ekaviṃśatiḥ) thousand (sahasrāṇi) six hundred (ṣaṭśatāni) [times] by day and by night (divā rātrau) — this (etat) mantra (mantram) accompanied by [its] number (saṅkhyānvitam) the vital principle (jīvaḥ) constantly repeats (japati sarvadā). 21,600 daily breaths
[√jap - to repeat, √i - to go (anvitam)]		 The vital principle always repeats this mantra 21,600 times in a day and a night.
ajapā nāma gāyatrī yoginām mokṣadāyinī |
asyāḥ saṅkalpamātreṇa naraḥ pāpād vimucyate ||41||

De-sandhi:
ajapā nāma gāyatrī yoginām mokṣa-dāyinī |
asyāḥ saṅkalpa-mātreṇa naraḥ pāpāt vimucyate ||		 ajapā = Ajapā (nom.)
nāma = named
gāyatrī = Gāyatrī (nom.)
yoginām = of yogis (gen. pl.)
mokṣa-dāyinī = bestowing liberation (nom. fem.)
asyāḥ = of this (gen. fem.)
saṅkalpa-mātreṇa = by mere thought (inst.)
naraḥ = man (nom.)
pāpāt = from sin (abl.)
vimucyate = is freed (pass.)		 The Gāyatrī (gāyatrī) named (nāma) Ajapā (ajapā) is liberation-bestowing (mokṣadāyinī) for yogis (yoginām). By mere intention (saṅkalpamātreṇa) regarding this (asyāḥ), a person (naraḥ) is released (vimucyate) from sin (pāpāt). Ajapā Gāyatrī liberates
[√dā - to give, √muc - to release]		 [This], the Gāyatrī called Ajapā, bestows liberation upon yogis. Merely by thinking of it a man is freed from sin.
ajapāsadṛśī vidyā ajapāsadṛśaṃ tapaḥ |
ajapāsadṛśaṃ puṇyam na bhūtam na bhaviṣyati ||42||

De-sandhi:
ajapā-sadṛśī vidyā ajapā-sadṛśam tapaḥ |
ajapā-sadṛśam puṇyam na bhūtam na bhaviṣyati ||		 ajapā-sadṛśī = like Ajapā (nom. fem.)
vidyā = knowledge (nom.)
ajapā-sadṛśam = like Ajapā (nom. n.)
tapaḥ = austerity (nom.)
puṇyam = merit (nom.)
na = not
bhūtam = has been (PPP)
na = not
bhaviṣyati = will be (fut.)		 A knowledge (vidyā) similar to Ajapā (ajapāsadṛśī), an austerity (tapaḥ) similar to Ajapā (ajapāsadṛśam), a merit (puṇyam) similar to Ajapā (ajapāsadṛśam) — has not existed (na bhūtam) and will not exist (na bhaviṣyati). Supremacy of Ajapā
[√bhū - to be, √bhū (bhaviṣyati) - to become]		 There never has been nor never will be a mantra like Ajapā, an austerity like Ajapā or a meritorious action like Ajapā.
kuṇḍalinyāḥ samudbhūtā gāyatrī prāṇadhāriṇī |
praṇavādyā tathā vidyā yas tām vetti sa yogavit ||43||

De-sandhi:
kuṇḍalinyāḥ samudbhūtā gāyatrī prāṇa-dhāriṇī |
praṇava-ādyā tathā vidyā yaḥ tām vetti sa yoga-vit ||		 kuṇḍalinyāḥ = from Kuṇḍalinī (abl. fem.)
samudbhūtā = arisen (PPP, nom. fem.)
gāyatrī = Gāyatrī (nom.)
prāṇa-dhāriṇī = bearing prāṇa (nom. fem.)
praṇava-ādyā = beginning with Om (nom. fem.)
tathā = likewise
vidyā = knowledge/mantra (nom.)
yaḥ = who (rel. pron.)
tām = her (acc.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
sa = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 Gāyatrī (gāyatrī), having arisen (samudbhūtā) from Kuṇḍalinī (kuṇḍalinyāḥ), is prāṇa-bearing (prāṇadhāriṇī); and likewise (tathā) the knowledge (vidyā) beginning with Praṇava [Om] (praṇavādyā) — whoever (yaḥ) knows (vetti) her (tām), that one (sa) is a knower of yoga (yogavit). Gāyatrī from Kuṇḍalinī
[√bhū - to arise, √vid - to know, √dhṛ - to hold]		 And Gāyatrī, the mantra (vidyā) starting with Om, which bears Prāṇa, arises from Kuṇḍalinī. Who knows her knows yoga.
kandordhvaṃ kuṇḍalī śaktir aṣṭadhā kuṭilikṛtā |
brahmadvāramukhaṃ nityaṃ sukhena āvṛtya tiṣṭhati ||44||

De-sandhi:
kanda-ūrdhvam kuṇḍalī śaktiḥ aṣṭa-dhā kuṭilī-kṛtā |
brahma-dvāra-mukham nityam sukhena āvṛtya tiṣṭhati ||		 kanda-ūrdhvam = above the bulb
kuṇḍalī = Kuṇḍalinī (nom.)
śaktiḥ = power (nom.)
aṣṭa-dhā = eightfold
kuṭilī-kṛtā = made coiled (PPP)
brahma-dvāra-mukham = mouth of Brahman's door (acc.)
nityam = constantly
sukhena = with ease (inst.)
āvṛtya = covering (abs.)
tiṣṭhati = dwells (3rd sg.)		 Above the bulb (kandordhvam), the power (śaktiḥ) Kuṇḍalinī (kuṇḍalī), made coiled eightfold (aṣṭadhā kuṭilikṛtā), dwells (tiṣṭhati) constantly (nityam), having covered (āvṛtya) the opening of Brahman's door (brahmadvāramukham) comfortably (sukhena). Kuṇḍalinī location and posture
[√sthā - to stand/dwell, √vṛ - to cover]		 Above the Bulb is the Śakti Kuṇḍalinī which is coiled eight times and constantly dwells at ease covering the opening of the doorway of Brahman.
yena margeṇa gantavyaṃ brahmasthānam nirāmayam |
mukhenā āchādya tad dvāraṃ prasupta parameśvarī ||45||

De-sandhi:
yena margeṇa gantavyam brahma-sthānam nir-āmayam |
mukhena āchādya tat dvāram prasupta parama-īśvarī ||		 yena = by which (inst.)
margeṇa = by path (inst.)
gantavyam = to be gone (gerundive)
brahma-sthānam = place of Brahman (nom.)
nir-āmayam = disease-free (nom.)
mukhena = with mouth (inst.)
āchādya = covering (abs.)
tat = that (acc.)
dvāram = door (acc.)
prasupta = sleeping (PPP, nom. fem.)
parama-īśvarī = supreme goddess (nom.)		 By which (yena) path (margeṇa) one should go to (gantavyam) the disease-free (nirāmayam) abode of Brahman (brahmasthānam), that (tat) door (dvāram) the supreme goddess (parameśvarī), sleeping (prasupta), covers (āchādya) with her mouth (mukhena). Sleeping goddess guards door to Brahman
[√gam - to go, √chād - to cover, √svap - to sleep]		 The sleeping supreme goddess covers with her mouth the opening of the path by which the disease-free place of Brahman is to be reached.
prabuddhā vahniyogena manasā marutā saha |
śucīvad guṇam ādāya vrajaty ūrdhvaṃ suṣumṇayā ||46||

De-sandhi:
prabuddhā vahni-yogena manasā marutā saha |
śucī-vat guṇam ādāya vrajati ūrdhvam suṣumṇayā ||		 prabuddhā = awakened (PPP, nom. fem.)
vahni-yogena = by application of fire (inst.)
manasā = with mind (inst.)
marutā = with breath (inst.)
saha = together
śucī-vat = like a needle
guṇam = thread (acc.)
ādāya = taking (abs.)
vrajati = goes (3rd sg.)
ūrdhvam = upward
suṣumṇayā = by Suṣumṇā (inst.)		 When awakened (prabuddhā) by union with fire (vahniyogena), together with (saha) the mind (manasā) and the breath (marutā), like a needle (śucīvat) taking (ādāya) a thread (guṇam), she goes (vrajati) upward (ūrdhvam) through Suṣumṇā (suṣumṇayā). Awakening Kuṇḍalinī (needle-thread simile)
[√budh - to awaken, √vraj - to go, √ā-dā - to take]		 Awakened by the application of fire, together with the mind [and] the breath, she goes upwards along the Suṣumṇā like a needle taking a thread.
prasphuradbhujagākārā padmatantunubhā śubhā |
prabuddhā vahniyogena vrajaty ūrdhvam suṣumṇayā ||47||

De-sandhi:
prasphurat-bhujaga-ākārā padma-tantu-nibhā śubhā |
prabuddhā vahni-yogena vrajati ūrdhvam suṣumṇayā ||		 prasphurat-bhujaga-ākārā = having form of glistening snake (nom. fem.)
padma-tantu-nibhā = resembling lotus thread (nom. fem.)
śubhā = goodly/auspicious (nom. fem.)
prabuddhā = awakened (PPP, nom. fem.)
vahni-yogena = by application of fire (inst.)
vrajati = goes (3rd sg.)
ūrdhvam = upward
suṣumṇayā = by Suṣumṇā (inst.)		 Having the appearance (ākārā) of a shimmering (prasphurat) serpent (bhujaga), resembling (nibhā) a lotus filament (padmatantu), the auspicious one (śubhā), awakened (prabuddhā) by union with fire (vahniyogena), travels (vrajati) upward (ūrdhvam) through Suṣumṇā (suṣumṇayā). Kuṇḍalinī appearance
[√sphur - to shimmer, √vraj - to go, √budh - to awaken]		 Having the form of a glistening snake [and] resembling a lotus thread, the goodly [Kuṇḍalinī], awakened by the application of fire, goes upwards along the Suṣumṇā.
uddhaṭayet kapāṭaṃ tu yathā kuñcikayā haṭhāt |
kuṇḍalinyā tathā mokṣadvāram vibhedayet ||48||

De-sandhi:
uddhaṭayet kapāṭam tu yathā kuñcikayā haṭhāt |
kuṇḍalinyā tathā mokṣa-dvāram vibhedayet ||		 uddhaṭayet = one might open (opt.)
kapāṭam = door (acc.)
tu = indeed
yathā = as/just as
kuñcikayā = with key (inst.)
haṭhāt = by force
kuṇḍalinyā = with Kuṇḍalinī (inst.)
tathā = so/likewise
mokṣa-dvāram = door to liberation (acc.)
vibhedayet = should break open (opt.)		 Just as (yathā) one might forcibly open (uddhaṭayet haṭhāt) a bolt (kapāṭam) with a key (kuñcikayā), similarly (tathā) one should pierce open (vibhedayet) the door of liberation (mokṣadvāram) with Kuṇḍalinī (kuṇḍalinyā). Opening liberation's door (key analogy)
[√haṭ - to open forcibly, √bhid - to pierce/break]		 In the same way that one might compel a door to open using a key, so does the yogi break open the doorway to liberation using Kuṇḍalinī.
kandordhvaṃ kuṇḍalī śaktiḥ suptā mokṣāya yoginām |
bandhanāya ca mūḍhānāṃ yas tāṃ vetti sa yogavit ||49||

De-sandhi:
kanda-ūrdhvam kuṇḍalī śaktiḥ suptā mokṣāya yoginām |
bandhanāya ca mūḍhānām yaḥ tām vetti sa yoga-vit ||		 kanda-ūrdhvam = above the bulb
kuṇḍalī = Kuṇḍalinī (nom.)
śaktiḥ = power (nom.)
suptā = sleeping (PPP, nom. fem.)
mokṣāya = for liberation (dat.)
yoginām = of yogis (gen. pl.)
bandhanāya = for bondage (dat.)
ca = and
mūḍhānām = of fools (gen. pl.)
yaḥ = who (rel. pron.)
tām = her (acc.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
sa = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 Above the bulb (kandordhvam), the power (śaktiḥ) Kuṇḍalinī (kuṇḍalī), sleeping (suptā), [exists] for the liberation (mokṣāya) of yogis (yoginām), and (ca) for the bondage (bandhanāya) of the confused (mūḍhānām). Whoever (yaḥ) knows (vetti) her (tām), that one (sa) is a knower of yoga (yogavit). Kuṇḍalinī for liberation or bondage
[√svap - to sleep, √vid - to know, √bandh - to bind]		 The Śakti Kuṇḍalinī who sleeps above the Bulb leads to liberation for yogis and bondage for fools. Who knows her knows yoga.
kṛtvā sampuṭitau karau dṛḍhataraṃ baddhvā atha padmāsanam
gāḍhaṃ vakṣasi sannidhāya cibukaṃ dhyāyaṃś ca tad cetasi |
vāraṃ vāram apānam ūrdhvam anilaṃ proccālayan pūritam
muñcan prāṇam upaiti bodham atulam śaktiprabhāvān naraḥ ||50||

De-sandhi:
kṛtvā sampuṭitau karau dṛḍha-taram baddhvā atha padma-āsanam
gāḍham vakṣasi sannidhāya cibukam dhyāyan ca tat cetasi |
vāram vāram apānam ūrdhvam anilam proccālayan pūritam
muñcan prāṇam upaiti bodham atulam śakti-prabhāvāt naraḥ ||		 kṛtvā = having made (abs.)
sampuṭitau = cupped together (acc. du.)
karau = hands (acc. du.)
dṛḍha-taram = very firmly (adv.)
baddhvā = having bound (abs.)
atha = then
padma-āsanam = lotus posture (acc.)
gāḍham = firmly
vakṣasi = on chest (loc.)
sannidhāya = placing (abs.)
cibukam = chin (acc.)
dhyāyan = meditating (pres. part.)
tat = on that
cetasi = in mind (loc.)
vāram vāram = again and again
apānam = Apāna (acc.)
ūrdhvam = upward
anilam = breath (acc.)
proccālayan = raising up (causative pres. part.)
pūritam = filled (PPP, acc.)
muñcan = releasing (pres. part.)
prāṇam = Prāṇa (acc.)
upaiti = attains (3rd sg.)
bodham = knowledge (acc.)
atulam = incomparable (acc.)
śakti-prabhāvāt = from power of Śakti (abl.)
naraḥ = man (nom.)		 Having made (kṛtvā) the two hands (karau) joined together (sampuṭitau), having very firmly bound (dṛḍhataram baddhvā) the lotus posture (padmāsanam), firmly (gāḍham) placing (sannidhāya) the chin (cibukam) on the chest (vakṣasi), and meditating (dhyāyan) on that (tat) in the heart (cetasi), repeatedly (vāraṃ vāram) driving upward (proccālayan ūrdhvam) Apāna (apānam) — the vital air (anilam) being filled (pūritam) — releasing (muñcan) Prāṇa (prāṇam), a person (naraḥ) attains (upaiti) incomparable (atulam) awakening (bodham) from the power of Śakti (śaktiprabhāvāt). Mahāmudrā technique
[√kṛ - to do, √bandh - to bind, √dhyai - to meditate, √muc - to release, √i - to attain]		 Having cupped the hands together and bound the lotus posture very firmly, firmly placing the chin on the chest and meditating on that in the mind, again and again raising Apāna upward, the breath filled, releasing Prāṇa, a man attains incomparable knowledge from the power of Śakti.
aṅgānāṃ mardanaṃ kṛtvā śramasaṃjātavāriṇā |
kaṭvāmlalavaṇatyāgī bhūtvā bhojanam ācaret ||51||

De-sandhi:
aṅgānām mardanam kṛtvā śrama-saṃjāta-vāriṇā |
kaṭu-āmla-lavaṇa-tyāgī bhūtvā bhojanam ācaret ||		 aṅgānām = of limbs (gen. pl.)
mardanam = rubbing/massage (acc.)
kṛtvā = having done (abs.)
śrama-saṃjāta-vāriṇā = with water born of exertion (inst.)
kaṭu-āmla-lavaṇa-tyāgī = abandoning pungent, sour, salty (nom.)
bhūtvā = having become (abs.)
bhojanam = food (acc.)
ācaret = should take (opt.)		 After having rubbed (mardanam kṛtvā) the limbs (aṅgānām) with sweat—water produced from exertion (śramasaṃjātavāriṇā)—and having become (bhūtvā) one who rejects (tyāgī) the pungent, sour, and salty (kaṭvāmlalavaṇa), one should consume (ācaret) food (bhojanam). Post-practice care and diet
[√mṛd - to rub/massage, √kṛ - to do, √tyaj - to abandon, √car - to practice]		 Having massaged the limbs with water born of exertion [sweat], having abandoned pungent, sour and salty [foods], one should take food.
brahmacārī mitāhārī yogī yogaparāyaṇaḥ |
abdād ūrdhvaṃ bhavet siddho nātra kāryā vicāraṇā ||52||

De-sandhi:
brahmacārī mita-āhārī yogī yoga-parāyaṇaḥ |
abdāt ūrdhvam bhavet siddhaḥ na atra kāryā vicāraṇā ||		 brahmacārī = celibate (nom.)
mita-āhārī = moderate eater (nom.)
yogī = yogi (nom.)
yoga-parāyaṇaḥ = devoted to yoga (nom.)
abdāt = from year (abl.)
ūrdhvam = after
bhavet = becomes (opt.)
siddhaḥ = perfected (nom.)
na = not
atra = here
kāryā = to be done
vicāraṇā = inquiry (nom.)		 The yogi (yogī) who practices celibacy (brahmacārī), eats moderately (mitāhārī), and is completely devoted to yoga (yogaparāyaṇaḥ) will become (bhavet) accomplished (siddhaḥ) within a year (abdād ūrdhvam). Here (atra), no (na) further deliberation (vicāraṇā) need be undertaken (kāryā). Brahmacarī practices: perfection in one year
[√bhū - to become, √sidh - to accomplish]		 A celibate, moderate eater, yogi devoted to yoga becomes perfected after a year — no inquiry should be made about this.
susnigdhaṃ madhurāhāram caturthāmśavivarjitam
bhuñhate surasamprītyai mitāhārī sa ucyate

De-sandhi:
su-snigdham madhura-āhāram caturtha-aṃśa-vivarjitam
bhuñhate sura-samprītyai mita-āhārī saḥ ucyate		 su-snigdham = very unctuous/pleasant (acc. sg. n.)
madhura-āhāram = sweet food (acc. sg.)
caturtha-aṃśa-vivarjitam = leaving fourth part aside (acc. sg.)
bhuñhate = eats (3rd sg. mid.)
sura-samprītyai = for gods' satisfaction (dat.)
mita-āhārī = moderate eater (nom. sg.)
saḥ = he (nom.)
ucyate = is called (3rd sg. pass.)		 He who eats (bhuñhate) very unctuous (susnigdham), sweet food (madhurāhāram), leaving aside the fourth part (caturthāmśavivarjitam), in order to please (samprītyai) the gods (sura)—he (saḥ) is called (ucyate) a moderate eater (mitāhārī). Definition of mitāhāra (moderate eating)
[√bhuj - to eat, √vac - to call, √vṛj - to abandon]
Eating pleasant food while leaving 1/4 of stomach empty.		 He who, in order to please the gods, eats unctuous, sweet food leaving aside a quarter of it is said to be one who has a restricted diet.
kandordhaṃ kuṇḍalī śakriḥ suptā mokṣāya yoginām
banshanāya ca mūḍānām yastāṃ vetti sa yogavit

De-sandhi:
kanda-ūrdhvam kuṇḍalī śakriḥ suptā mokṣāya yoginām
banshanāya ca mūḍānām yaḥ tām vetti saḥ yoga-vit		 kanda-ūrdhvam = above the bulb (adv.)
kuṇḍalī = coiled (nom. sg. f.)
śakriḥ = Śakti/power (nom. sg.)
suptā = sleeping (nom. sg. f.)
mokṣāya = for liberation (dat.)
yoginām = of yogis (gen. pl.)
banshanāya = for bondage (dat.)
ca = and
mūḍānām = of fools (gen. pl.)
yaḥ = who (nom.)
tām = her (acc.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
saḥ = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 Above the kanda-bulb (kandordhvam) the coiled (kuṇḍalī) Śakti (śakriḥ) lies sleeping (suptā), for the liberation (mokṣāya) of yogis (yoginām) and (ca) for the bondage (banshanāya) of fools (mūḍānām). Who (yaḥ) knows (vetti) her (tām), he (saḥ) [is] a knower of yoga (yogavit). Kuṇḍalinī: liberation or bondage
[√vid - to know]
The coiled Śakti brings liberation to yogis who know her, bondage to fools who don't.		 The coiled goddess sleeps above the bulb for the liberation of yogis. Because it binds bondage it is called a lock.
MUDRĀBANDHĀḤ (SEALS AND LOCKS)
mahāmudṛām nabhomudrāmuddīyāṇam jalandharam
mūlabandhaṃ ca yo vetti sa yogī muktibhājanam

De-sandhi:
mahā-mudṛām nabha-mudrām uḍḍīyāṇam jalandharam
mūla-bandham ca yaḥ vetti saḥ yogī mukti-bhājanam		 mahā-mudrām = great seal (acc. sg. f.)
nabha-mudrām = sky seal (acc. sg. f.)
uḍḍīyāṇam = uḍḍīyāṇa (acc. sg. n.)
jalandharam = jālandhara (acc. sg. n.)
mūla-bandham = root lock (acc.)
ca = and
yaḥ = who (nom.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
saḥ = he (nom.)
yogī = yogi (nom.)
mukti-bhājanam = vessel/receptacle of liberation (nom. sg. n.)		 Whoever (yaḥ) knows (vetti) mahāmudrā (mahāmudrām), nabhomudrā (nabhomudrām), uḍḍīyāṇa (uḍḍīyāṇam), jālandhara (jalandharam), and (ca) mūlabandha (mūlabandham)—that (saḥ) yogi (yogī) [is] a vessel of liberation (muktibhājanam). List of the five main mudrās/bandhas
[√vid - to know, √bhaj - to partake/share]
Enumeration of the essential practices. The yogi becomes a receptacle/vessel (bhājana) for liberation.		 The yogi who knows Mahāmudrā, Nabhomudrā, Uḍḍīyāna, Jālandhara and Mūlabandha is worthy of liberation.
apānaprāṇayoraikyam kṣayo mūtrapurīṣayoḥ
yuvā bhvati vṛddho api satatam mūlabandhanāt

De-sandhi:
apāna-prāṇayoḥ aikyam kṣayaḥ mūtra-purīṣayoḥ
yuvā bhavati vṛddhaḥ api satatam mūla-bandhanāt		 apāna-prāṇayoḥ = of apāna and prāṇa (gen. dual)
aikyam = unity (nom. sg.)
kṣayaḥ = decrease (nom.)
mūtra-purīṣayoḥ = of urine and feces (gen. dual)
yuvā = youth (nom.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg.)
vṛddhaḥ = old man (nom.)
api = even
satatam = constantly (adv.)
mūla-bandhanāt = from root lock (abl.)		 Unity (aikyam) of apāna and prāṇa (apānaprāṇayoḥ), reduction (kṣayaḥ) of urine and feces (mūtrapurīṣayoḥ)—even (api) an old person (vṛddhaḥ) becomes (bhavati) youthful (yuvā) by constant (satatam) [practice of] mūlabandha (mūlabandhanāt). Benefits of mūlabandha
[√bhū - to become, √kṣi - to diminish]
Uniting the two main breaths and rejuvenation.		 The union of Prāṇa and Apāna, reduction of urine and faeces, even an old man becoming young: [these arise] from the continuous application of Mūlabandha.
pārṣṇibhāgena sampīḍya yonimākuṇcyed gudam
apānamūrdhvamākṛṣya mulabandho ayamucyate

De-sandhi:
pārṣṇi-bhāgena sampīḍya yonim ākuñcyet gudam
apānam ūrdhvam ākṛṣya mūla-bandhaḥ ayam ucyate		 pārṣṇi-bhāgena = with heel part (inst.)
sampīḍya = pressing (abs.)
yonim = perineum (acc.)
ākuñcyet = should contract (3rd sg. opt.)
gudam = anus (acc.)
apānam = apāna breath (acc.)
ūrdhvam = upward (adv.)
ākṛṣya = drawing (abs.)
mūla-bandhaḥ = root lock (nom.)
ayam = this (nom.)
ucyate = is called (3rd sg. pass.)		 Having pressed (sampīḍya) the perineum (yonim) with the heel-part (pārṣṇibhāgena), one should contract (ākuñcyet) the anus (gudam), drawing (ākṛṣya) apāna (apānam) upward (ūrdhvam). This (ayam) is called (ucyate) 'mūlabandha' (mūlabandhaḥ). Technique of mūlabandha
[√pīḍ - to press, √kuñc - to contract, √kṛṣ - to draw, √vac - to call]
Press perineum with heel, contract anus, draw apāna upward.		 Press the perineum with the side of the heel and clench the anus. When Apāna is drawn up this is called Mūlabandha, the Root Lock.
uddīṇam kurute yasmād aviśrāntaṃ mahākhagaḥ
uddiyānaṃ tadeva syātttra bandho vidhīyate

De-sandhi:
uḍḍīyanam kurute yasmāt aviśrāntam mahā-khagaḥ
uḍḍiyānam tat eva syāt tatra bandhaḥ vidhīyate		 uḍḍīyanam = upward flight (acc.)
kurute = makes/does (3rd sg. mid.)
yasmāt = because (abl.)
aviśrāntam = unceasingly, without rest (adv.)
mahā-khagaḥ = great bird (nom.)
uḍḍiyānam = uḍḍīyāna (nom.)
tat = that (nom. n.)
eva = indeed
syāt = should be (3rd sg. opt.)
tatra = there
bandhaḥ = lock (nom.)
vidhīyate = is prescribed (3rd sg. pass.)		 Because (yasmāt) the great bird (mahākhagaḥ) performs (kurute) upward flight (uḍḍīyanam) unceasingly (aviśrāntam), that (tat) indeed (eva) would be (syāt) 'uḍḍīyāna' (uḍḍiyānam). There (tatra) the bandha (bandhaḥ) is prescribed (vidhīyate). Etymology of uḍḍīyāna
[√kṛ - to make, √as - to be, √vidhā - to prescribe]
Named after the great bird (khaga) that flies upward without ceasing.		 Because the great bird tirelessly flies up, it is that which is Uḍḍiyāna... ?
udare paścime tāṇam nābherūrdhvaṃ ca kārayet
uddiyāno hyasau bandho mṛtyumātaṇgakesarī

De-sandhi:
udare paścime tāṇam nābheḥ ūrdhvam ca kārayet
uḍḍiyānaḥ hi asau bandhaḥ mṛtyu-mātaṇga-kesarī		 udare = in belly (loc.)
paścime = posterior (loc.)
tāṇam = stretching (acc.)
nābheḥ = of navel (gen.)
ūrdhvam = upward (adv.)
ca = and
kārayet = should make (3rd sg. caus. opt.)
uḍḍiyānaḥ = uḍḍīyāna (nom.)
hi = indeed
asau = that (nom.)
bandhaḥ = lock (nom.)
mṛtyu-mātaṇga-kesarī = lion to the elephant of death (nom.)		 One should cause (kārayet) stretching (tāṇam) of the belly (udare) backwards (paścime) and (ca) upwards (ūrdhvam) from the navel (nābheḥ). That (asau) uḍḍīyāna-bandha (uḍḍiyānaḥ bandhaḥ) indeed (hi) [is] a lion (kesarī) to the elephant (mātaṇga) of death (mṛtyu). Technique and metaphor of uḍḍīyāna
[√kṛ - to make]
Drawing the belly back and up; like a lion killing the elephant of death.		 [The yogi] should stretch the abdomen backwards and above the navel. That is the Uḍḍīyāna Lock, a lion to the elephant of death.
badhnāti hi śirājālamadhogāminabhojalam
tataḥ jālandharo bandhḥ kaṇṭhaduḥkhaudhanāśanaḥ

De-sandhi:
badhnāti hi śirā-jālam adhaḥ-gāmi nabhaḥ-jalam
tataḥ jālandharaḥ bandhaḥ kaṇṭha-duḥkha-udha-nāśanaḥ		 badhnāti = binds (3rd sg.)
hi = indeed
śirā-jālam = network of channels (acc.)
adhaḥ-gāmi = going downward (acc.)
nabhaḥ-jalam = sky-water/nectar (acc.)
tataḥ = therefore
jālandharaḥ = jālandhara (nom.)
bandhaḥ = lock (nom.)
kaṇṭha-duḥkha-udha-nāśanaḥ = destroyer of throat suffering (nom.)		 Indeed (hi) it binds (badhnāti) the network of channels (śirājālam), the downward-flowing (adhogāmi) sky-nectar (nabhojalam). Therefore (tataḥ) jālandhara-bandha (jālandharaḥ bandhaḥ) [is] the destroyer (nāśanaḥ) of throat-suffering (kaṇṭhaduḥkha). Function and benefit of jālandhara
[√bandh - to bind, √naś - to destroy]
Binds the downward flow of nectar; removes throat pain.		 It binds all the channels and the fluid flowing down from the void. That is why it is the Jālandhara Lock, which gets rid of all problems in the throat.
jālandhare kṛte bandhe kaṇṭhasaṃkocalakṣaṇe
na pīyūṣaṃ pataryagnau na ca vāyuḥ pradhāvati

De-sandhi:
jālandhare kṛte bandhe kaṇṭha-saṃkoca-lakṣaṇe
na pīyūṣam patati agnau na ca vāyuḥ pradhāvati		 jālandhare = jālandhara (loc.)
kṛte = done (loc.)
bandhe = lock (loc.)
kaṇṭha-saṃkoca-lakṣaṇe = characterized by throat contraction (loc.)
na = not
pīyūṣam = nectar (nom.)
patati = falls (3rd sg.)
agnau = in fire (loc.)
na ca = and not
vāyuḥ = breath (nom.)
pradhāvati = runs forth (3rd sg.)		 When jālandhara-bandha (jālandhare bandhe) is made (kṛte), characterized by throat-contraction (kaṇṭhasaṃkocalakṣaṇe), the nectar (pīyūṣam) does not (na) fall (patati) into the fire (agnau), and (ca) the vāyu (vāyuḥ) does not run forth (pradhāvati). Effects of jālandhara
[√kṛ - to do, √pat - to fall, √dhāv - to run]
Preserves the nectar from falling into fire; restrains breath.		 When the Jālandhara Lock is performed, its defining feature being the constriction of the throat, nectar does not fall in the fire and the breath does not rush forth.
kapālakuhare jihvā praviṣṭā viparītagā
bhruvorantargatā dṛṣṭirmudrābhavati khecarī

De-sandhi:
kapāla-kuhare jihvā praviṣṭā viparīta-gā
bhruvoḥ-antara-gatā dṛṣṭiḥ mudrā bhavati khecarī		 kapāla-kuhare = in skull cavity (loc.)
jihvā = tongue (nom.)
praviṣṭā = entered (nom. f.)
viparīta-gā = gone backwards (nom. f.)
bhruvoḥ-antara-gatā = gone between eyebrows (nom. f.)
dṛṣṭiḥ = gaze (nom.)
mudrā = seal (nom.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg.)
khecarī = sky-moving (nom. f.)		 The tongue (jihvā) having entered (praviṣṭā) the skull-cavity (kapālakuhare), gone backwards (viparītagā), the gaze (dṛṣṭiḥ) gone (gatā) between the eyebrows (bhruvorantara)—the mudrā (mudrā) becomes (bhavati) khecarī (khecarī). Description of khecarī mudrā
[√viś - to enter, √gam - to go, √bhū - to become]
Tongue turned back into skull with upward gaze.		 The tongue turned back and into the hollow of the skull, the gaze between the brows: this is khecarīmudrā.
na rogo maraṇaṃ tandrā na nidrā na kṣudhā tṛṣā
na ca mūrcchā bhavettasya yo mudrāṃ vetti khecarīm

De-sandhi:
na rogaḥ maraṇam tandrā na nidrā na kṣudhā tṛṣā
na ca mūrcchā bhavet tasya yaḥ mudrām vetti khecarīm		 na = not
rogaḥ = disease (nom.)
maraṇam = death (nom.)
tandrā = torpor (nom.)
na nidrā = not sleep (nom.)
na kṣudhā = not hunger (nom.)
tṛṣā = thirst (nom.)
na ca mūrcchā = and not fainting (nom.)
bhavet = occurs (3rd sg. opt.)
tasya = of him (gen.)
yaḥ = who (nom.)
mudrām = seal (acc.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
khecarīm = sky-moving (acc. f.)		 Neither disease (rogaḥ), death (maraṇam), torpor (tandrā), nor (na) sleep (nidrā), nor hunger (kṣudhā), thirst (tṛṣā), and nor (na ca) fainting (mūrcchā) would occur (bhavet) for him (tasya) who (yaḥ) knows (vetti) the khecarī mudrā (khecarīm mudrām). Benefits of khecarī mudrā (1)
[√bhū - to be/become]
Freedom from disease, death, and bodily needs.		 Neither disease, nor death, nor fatigue, nor sleep, nor hunger, nor fainting arise for he who knows khecarī.
pīḍyate na sarogeṇa lipyate na ca karmaṇā
bādhyate na sa kālena yo mudrāṃ vetti khecarim

De-sandhi:
pīḍyate na sa rogeṇa lipyate na ca karmaṇā
bādhyate na sa kālena yaḥ mudrām vetti khecarim		 pīḍyate = is afflicted (3rd sg. pass.)
na sa = not he
rogeṇa = by disease (inst.)
lipyate = is stained (3rd sg. pass.)
na ca = and not
karmaṇā = by karma (inst.)
bādhyate = is troubled (3rd sg. pass.)
na sa = not he
kālena = by time (inst.)
yaḥ = who (nom.)
mudrām = seal (acc.)
vetti = knows (3rd sg.)
khecarim = sky-moving (acc. f.)		 He (sa) is not (na) afflicted (pīḍyate) by disease (rogeṇa), not (na) stained (lipyate) by karma (karmaṇā), and (ca) not oppressed (bādhyate) by time (kālena)—who (yaḥ) knows (vetti) the khecarī mudrā (khecarim mudrām). Benefits of khecarī mudrā (2)
[√pīḍ - to afflict, √lip - to smear, √bādh - to trouble]
Transcending disease, karma, and time.		 He who knows khecarī is not afflicted by disease or tainted by karma or troubled by death.
cittam carati khe yasmājjihvā carati khe gatā
tenaiṣā khecarī nāma mudrā siddhairnamaskṛta

De-sandhi:
cittam carati khe yasmāt jihvā carati khe gatā
tena eṣā khecarī nāma mudrā siddhaiḥ namaskṛta		 cittam = mind (nom.)
carati = moves (3rd sg.)
khe = in space (loc.)
yasmāt = because (abl.)
jihvā = tongue (nom.)
carati = moves (3rd sg.)
khe = in space (loc.)
gatā = gone (nom. f.)
tena = therefore (inst.)
eṣā = this (nom. f.)
khecarī = space-moving (nom.)
nāma = by name
mudrā = seal (nom.)
siddhaiḥ = by perfected ones (inst. pl.)
namaskṛta = honored/saluted (nom. f.)		 Because (yasmāt) the mind (cittam) moves (carati) in space (khe) and the tongue (jihvā), having gone (gatā), moves (carati) in space (khe), therefore (tena) this (eṣā) mudrā (mudrā) is honored (namaskṛta) by the siddhas (siddhaiḥ) with the name (nāma) 'khecarī' (khecarī). Etymology of khecarī
[√car - to move, √gam - to go, √namas - to honor]
Both mind and tongue move in the space (kha) of consciousness.		 The mind moves (carati) in space (khe), because the tongue moves in space. This is the mudrā called khecarī, honoured by the adepts.
bindumūlaṃ śariram tu sirāstatra pratiṣṭhitāḥ
plāvayanti śarirem yā āpādatalamastakam

De-sandhi:
bindu-mūlam śarīram tu sirāḥ tatra pratiṣṭhitāḥ
plāvayanti śarīram yāḥ ā-pāda-tala-mastakam		 bindu-mūlam = root in bindu (nom. n.)
śarīram = body (nom.)
tu = but/indeed
sirāḥ = channels (nom. pl.)
tatra = there
pratiṣṭhitāḥ = established (nom. pl. f.)
plāvayanti = cause to float/fill (3rd pl.)
śarīram = body (acc.)
yāḥ = which (nom. pl. f.)
ā-pāda-tala-mastakam = from sole of foot to head (acc.)		 The body (śarīram) indeed (tu) has bindu as its root (bindumūlam). There (tatra) the channels (sirāḥ) are established (pratiṣṭhitāḥ), which (yāḥ) fill (plāvayanti) the body (śarīram) from the sole of the foot to the head (āpādatalamastakam). Bindu as foundation and channels
[√sthā - to stand, √plu - to float]
Body rooted in bindu; channels established there fill the entire body.		 The body is founded on Bindu. In it are found channels which flood the body from the soles of the feet to the head.
khecaryā mudrāṃ yena vivaram lambhikordhvataḥ
na tasya kṣarate binduḥ kāminyāliṅgitasya ca

De-sandhi:
khecaryā mudrām yena vivaram lambhikā ūrdhvataḥ
na tasya kṣarate binduḥ kāminyā āliṅgitasya ca		 khecaryā = by khecarī (inst.)
mudrām = seal (acc.)
yena = by which (inst.)
vivaram = opening/cavity (acc.)
lambhikā = uvula (nom.)
ūrdhvataḥ = from above
na = not
tasya = of him (gen.)
kṣarate = flows/drips (3rd sg.)
binduḥ = drop (nom.)
kāminyā = by woman (inst.)
āliṅgitasya = of embraced one (gen.)
ca = and		 By the khecarīmudrā (khecaryā mudrām), through which (yena) [the tongue reaches] the opening (vivaram) above (ūrdhvataḥ) the uvula (lambhikā), of him (tasya) bindu (binduḥ) does not (na) drip (kṣarate), even (ca) when embraced (āliṅgitasya) by a woman (kāminyā). Khecarī preserves bindu even in passion
[√kṣar - to flow/drip, √liṅg - to embrace]
Tongue reaching cavity above uvula prevents loss of vital essence.		 The bindu of he who has sealed the opening above the uvula with khecarī does not fall, even if he is embraced by a passionate woman.
yāvadbinduḥ sthito dehe tāvanmṛtyubhayaṃ kutaḥ |
yāvadbaddhā nabhomudrā tāvadbindurna gacchati ||52||

De-sandhi:
yāvat binduḥ sthitaḥ dehe tāvat mṛtyu-bhayam kutaḥ |
yāvat baddhā nabhaḥ-mudrā tāvat binduḥ na gacchati ||		 yāvat = as long as
binduḥ = drop/semen (nom.)
sthitaḥ = steady/remaining (PPP, nom.)
dehe = in body (loc.)
tāvat = so long
mṛtyu-bhayam = fear of death (nom.)
kutaḥ = whence/how?
baddhā = bound/applied (PPP, nom. f.)
nabhaḥ-mudrā = sky-seal (nom.)
na gacchati = does not go (3rd sg.)		 As long as (yāvat) bindu (binduḥ) remains (sthitaḥ) in the body (dehe), so long (tāvat) whence (kutaḥ) fear of death (mṛtyubhayam)? As long as (yāvat) the nabho-mudrā (nabhomudrā) is applied (baddhā), so long (tāvat) bindu (binduḥ) does not go (na gacchati). Bindu retention prevents death
[√sthā - to stand, √gam - to go, √bandh - to bind]
Nabhomudrā: tongue turned upward into nasal cavity.		 When bindu is steady in the body then there is no fear of death. When the sky mudrā (nabho-mudrā) is applied, then bindu does not move.
calito'pi yadā binduḥ samprāptaśca hutāśanam |
gacchatyūrdhvaṃ hataḥ śaktyā nibasṭho yonimudrāyā ||53||

De-sandhi:
calitaḥ api yadā binduḥ samprāptaḥ ca hutāśanam |
gacchati ūrdhvam hataḥ śaktyā nibaddhaḥ yoni-mudrāyā ||		 calitaḥ = moved (PPP, nom.)
api = even
yadā = when
binduḥ = drop (nom.)
samprāptaḥ = having reached (PPP, nom.)
ca = and
hutāśanam = fire (acc.)
gacchati = goes (3rd sg.)
ūrdhvam = upward
hataḥ = struck (PPP, nom.)
śaktyā = by śakti (inst.)
nibaddhaḥ = bound (PPP, nom.)
yoni-mudrāyā = by yoni-mudrā (inst.)		 When (yadā) bindu (binduḥ), even (api) having moved (calitaḥ) and (ca) having reached (samprāptaḥ) the fire (hutāśanam), goes (gacchati) upward (ūrdhvam), struck (hataḥ) by śakti (śaktyā), bound (nibaddhaḥ) by the yoni-mudrā (yonimudrāyā). Yonimudrā reverses bindu's descent
[√cal - to move, √gam - to go, √han - to strike, √bandh - to bind]
Even when bindu descends toward fire (genital region), yonimudrā reverses it.		 When bindu has moved and reached the fire it goes upwards on being checked by the yoni-mudrā and struck by the goddess (śakti).
sa eva dvividho binduḥ pāṇḍuro lohitastathā |
pāṇḍuraṃ śukramityāhurlohitaṃ tad rajaḥ mahārajaḥ ||54||

De-sandhi:
saḥ eva dvi-vidhaḥ binduḥ pāṇḍuraḥ lohitaḥ tathā |
pāṇḍuram śukram iti āhuḥ lohitam tat rajaḥ mahā-rajaḥ ||		 saḥ = that (nom.)
eva = indeed
dvi-vidhaḥ = of two kinds (nom.)
binduḥ = drop (nom.)
pāṇḍuraḥ = white (nom.)
lohitaḥ = red (nom.)
tathā = and
pāṇḍuram = white (acc.)
śukram = semen (acc.)
iti āhuḥ = thus they call (3rd pl.)
lohitam = red (acc.)
tat = that
rajaḥ = rajas (acc.)
mahā-rajaḥ = great rajas (nom.)		 That (saḥ) bindu (binduḥ) is indeed (eva) of two kinds (dvividhaḥ): white (pāṇḍuraḥ) and (tathā) red (lohitaḥ). They call (āhuḥ) the white (pāṇḍuram) semen (śukram), thus (iti), [and] that (tat) red (lohitam) [is] rajas (rajaḥ), the great rajas (mahārajaḥ). White bindu (śukra) and red rajas
[√ah - to say]
Two essences: male (white/lunar) and female (red/solar).		 Bindu is [in fact] of two kinds: white and red. White is bindu and red is the great rajas (female generative fluid).
vidrumadravasamkāśaṃ yonisthāne sthitaṃ rajaḥ |
śaśisthāne vasedbindurdvayoraikyaṃ sudurlabham ||55||

De-sandhi:
vidruma-drava-saṃkāśam yoni-sthāne sthitam rajaḥ |
śaśi-sthāne vaset binduḥ dvayoḥ aikyam su-durlabham ||		 vidruma-drava-saṃkāśam = resembling liquid coral (acc.)
yoni-sthāne = in perineal region (loc.)
sthitam = situated (PPP, nom.)
rajaḥ = rajas (nom.)
śaśi-sthāne = in moon's place (loc.)
vaset = would dwell (3rd sg. opt.)
binduḥ = bindu (nom.)
dvayoḥ = of both (gen. dual)
aikyam = unity (nom.)
su-durlabham = very difficult to attain (nom.)		 Rajas (rajaḥ), resembling liquid coral (vidrumadravasamkāśam), is situated (sthitam) in the perineal region (yonisthāne). Bindu (binduḥ) dwells (vaset) in the moon's place (śaśisthāne). The unity (aikyam) of the two (dvayoḥ) is very difficult to achieve (sudurlabham). Locations of rajas and bindu
[√sthā - to stand, √vas - to dwell]
Rajas at base; bindu at cranial moon; union is rare.		 Looking like liquid coral, rajas is situated in the perineal region. Bindu resides in the moon. Joining the two is very difficult.
binduḥ śivo rajaḥ śaktirbindurindū rajo raviḥ |
ubhayoḥ saṃgamādeva prāpyate paramaṃ padam ||56||

De-sandhi:
binduḥ śivaḥ rajaḥ śaktiḥ binduḥ induḥ rajaḥ raviḥ |
ubhayoḥ saṃgamāt eva prāpyate paramam padam ||		 binduḥ = bindu (nom.)
śivaḥ = Śiva (nom.)
rajaḥ = rajas (nom.)
śaktiḥ = Śakti (nom.)
induḥ = moon (nom.)
raviḥ = sun (nom.)
ubhayoḥ = of both (gen. dual)
saṃgamāt = from union (abl.)
eva = indeed
prāpyate = is attained (pass. 3rd sg.)
paramam padam = supreme state (nom.)		 Bindu (binduḥ) is Śiva (śivaḥ), rajas (rajaḥ) is Śakti (śaktiḥ). Bindu (binduḥ) is the moon (induḥ), rajas (rajaḥ) is the sun (raviḥ). Only (eva) from the union (saṃgamāt) of both (ubhayoḥ) is the supreme state (paramaṃ padam) attained (prāpyate). Cosmic correspondence of bindu-rajas
[√gam - to go, √āp - to attain]
Bindu = Śiva = Moon; Rajas = Śakti = Sun; union = liberation.		 Bindu is Śiva. Rajas is Śakti. Bindu is the moon. Rajas is the sun. Only by joining the two is the highest condition attained.
vāyunā śakticālena preritaṃ khe yadā rajaḥ |
bindorekatvamāyāti yo jānāti sa yogavit ||57||

De-sandhi:
vāyunā śakti-cālena preritam khe yadā rajaḥ |
bindoḥ ekatvam āyāti yaḥ jānāti saḥ yoga-vit ||		 vāyunā = by breath (inst.)
śakti-cālena = by moving śakti (inst.)
preritam = propelled (PPP, nom.)
khe = in space (loc.)
yadā = when
rajaḥ = rajas (nom.)
bindoḥ = with bindu (gen.)
ekatvam = oneness (acc.)
āyāti = attains (3rd sg.)
yaḥ = who (nom.)
jānāti = knows (3rd sg.)
saḥ = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 When (yadā) rajas (rajaḥ), propelled (preritam) by breath (vāyunā) through moving śakti (śakticālena), attains (āyāti) oneness (ekatvam) with bindu (bindoḥ) in space (khe)—he who (yaḥ) knows (jānāti) [this], he (saḥ) is a knower of yoga (yogavit). Union through breath and śakti
[√cal - to move, √pra-īr - to propel, √jñā - to know]
Rajas rises to unite with bindu through prāṇāyāma.		 When rajas is propelled by moving the goddess by means of the breath it joins with bindu. He who knows this knows yoga.
śukraṃ candreṇa saṃyuktaṃ rajaḥ sūryeṇa saṃyutam |
tayoḥ samarasaikatvam yo jānāti sa yogavit ||76||

De-sandhi:
śukram candreṇa saṃyuktam rajaḥ sūryeṇa saṃyutam |
tayoḥ sama-rasa-ekatvam yaḥ jānāti saḥ yoga-vit ||		 śukram = semen (nom.)
candreṇa = with moon (inst.)
saṃyuktam = united (PPP, nom.)
rajaḥ = rajas (nom.)
sūryeṇa = with sun (inst.)
saṃyutam = united (PPP, nom.)
tayoḥ = of those two (gen. dual)
sama-rasa-ekatvam = oneness of equal essence (acc.)
yaḥ = who (nom.)
jānāti = knows (3rd sg.)
saḥ = he (nom.)
yoga-vit = knower of yoga (nom.)		 Semen (śukram) is united (saṃyuktam) with the moon (candreṇa), rajas (rajaḥ) is united (saṃyutam) with the sun (sūryeṇa). He who (yaḥ) knows (jānāti) the oneness of equal essence (samarasaikatvam) of those two (tayoḥ), he (saḥ) is a knower of yoga (yogavit). Samarasa: union of solar and lunar
[√yuj - to unite, √jñā - to know]
Samarasa = equal essence/flavor; haṭhayoga goal.		 Semen (śukram) is associated with the moon, rajas with the sun. He who knows how to make them one knows yoga.
śodhanaṃ nāḍījālasya ghaṭanaṃ candrasūryayoḥ |
rasānāṃ śoṣaṇaṃ samyak mahāmudrābhidhīyate ||58||

De-sandhi:
śodhanam nāḍī-jālasya ghaṭanam candra-sūryayoḥ |
rasānām śoṣaṇam samyak mahā-mudrā abhidhīyate ||		 śodhanam = purification (nom.)
nāḍī-jālasya = of network of channels (gen.)
ghaṭanam = union/joining (nom.)
candra-sūryayoḥ = of moon and sun (gen. dual)
rasānām = of fluids (gen. pl.)
śoṣaṇam = drying up (nom.)
samyak = correctly/properly
mahā-mudrā = great seal (nom.)
abhidhīyate = is called (pass. 3rd sg.)		 Purification (śodhanam) of the network of channels (nāḍījālasya), union (ghaṭanam) of moon and sun (candrasūryayoḥ), and drying up (śoṣaṇam) of the [bodily] fluids (rasānām) correctly (samyak)—[when this happens] it is called (abhidhīyate) mahāmudrā (mahāmudrā). Three effects of Mahāmudrā
[√śudh - to purify, √ghaṭ - to join, √śuṣ - to dry, √dhā - to name]
Nāḍī purification + sun-moon union + fluid desiccation.		 Purification of all the channels, union of the sun and moon and the drying up of the bodily fluids: [when this happens] correctly, it is called Mahāmudrā.
vakṣonyastahanuḥ nipīḍya suciraṃ yoniṃ ca vāmāṅghriṇā
hastābhyāmapi dhārayetpravitataṃ pādaṃ tathā dakṣiṇam |
āpūrya śvasanena kukṣiyugalaṃ baddhvā śanairrecayed
eṣā pātakanāśinī khalu mahāmudrā nṛṇāṃ procyate ||59||

De-sandhi:
vakṣasi nyasta-hanuḥ nipīḍya suciram yonim ca vāma-aṅghriṇā
hastābhyām api dhārayet pravitatam pādam tathā dakṣiṇam |
āpūrya śvasanena kukṣi-yugalam baddhvā śanaiḥ recayet
eṣā pātaka-nāśinī khalu mahā-mudrā nṛṇām procyate ||		 vakṣasi = on chest (loc.)
nyasta-hanuḥ = with chin placed (BV, nom.)
nipīḍya = having pressed (abs.)
suciram = for very long
yonim = perineum (acc.)
ca = and
vāma-aṅghriṇā = with left heel (inst.)
hastābhyām = with both hands (inst. dual)
api = also
dhārayet = should hold (opt.)
pravitatam = extended (acc.)
pādam = foot (acc.)
dakṣiṇam = right (acc.)
āpūrya = having filled (abs.)
śvasanena = with breath (inst.)
kukṣi-yugalam = both lungs (acc.)
baddhvā = having held (abs.)
śanaiḥ = slowly
recayet = should exhale (opt.)
eṣā = this (nom. f.)
pātaka-nāśinī = destroyer of sins (nom. f.)
khalu = indeed
mahā-mudrā = great seal (nom.)
nṛṇām = of people (gen. pl.)
procyate = is taught (pass.)		 With the chin (hanuḥ) placed (nyasta) on the chest (vakṣasi), having pressed (nipīḍya) the perineum (yonim) for a very long time (suciram) with the left heel (vāmāṅghriṇā), one should hold (dhārayet) with both hands (hastābhyām) the extended (pravitatam) right foot (pādaṃ dakṣiṇam). Having filled (āpūrya) both lungs (kukṣiyugalam) with breath (śvasanena), having held (baddhvā) [it], one should slowly (śanaiḥ) exhale (recayet). This (eṣā) is indeed (khalu) taught (procyate) to be the great seal (mahāmudrā), destroyer of sins (pātakanāśinī), for people (nṛṇām). Mahāmudrā technique
[√as - to place, √pīḍ - to press, √dhṛ - to hold, √pūr - to fill, √ric - to empty]
Combines jālandhara-bandha, mūla-bandha with leg position and kumbhaka.		 With the jaw placed on the chest, the yogi should, for a very long time, press the perineum with the left heel and hold the extended right foot with both hands. He should fill both lungs with breath, hold it and slowly exhale. This is taught to be the Great Seal, which destroys people's sins.
candrāṅge tu samabhyasya sūryāṅge punarabhyaset |
yāvattulyā bhavetsaṅkhyā tato mudrāṃ visarjayet ||60||

De-sandhi:
candra-aṅge tu samabhyasya sūrya-aṅge punaḥ abhyaset |
yāvat tulyā bhavet saṅkhyā tataḥ mudrām visarjayet ||		 candra-aṅge = on lunar side (loc.)
tu = indeed
samabhyasya = having practiced (abs.)
sūrya-aṅge = on solar side (loc.)
punaḥ = again
abhyaset = should practice (opt.)
yāvat = until
tulyā = equal (nom. f.)
bhavet = becomes (opt.)
saṅkhyā = number (nom.)
tataḥ = then
mudrām = seal (acc.)
visarjayet = should release (opt.)		 Having practiced (samabhyasya) on the lunar side (candrāṅge), one should again (punaḥ) practice (abhyaset) on the solar side (sūryāṅge). When (yāvat) the number (saṅkhyā) becomes (bhavet) equal (tulyā), then (tataḥ) one should release (visarjayet) the mudrā (mudrām). Equal practice on both sides
[√bhyas - to practice, √sṛj - to release]
Lunar = left/iḍā; Solar = right/piṅgalā; balance required.		 After practising this on the lunar [side of the] body, one should again practise it on the solar [side of the] body. When the number [of rounds on each side] is equal, then [the yogi] should stop the [Mahā]mudrā.
na hi pathyamapathyaṃ vā rasāḥ sarve'pi nīrasāḥ |
api bhuktaṃ viṣaṃ ghoraṃ pīyūṣamiva jīryati ||61||

De-sandhi:
na hi pathyam apathyam vā rasāḥ sarve api nīrasāḥ |
api bhuktam viṣam ghoram pīyūṣam iva jīryati ||		 na = not
hi = indeed
pathyam = wholesome (nom.)
apathyam = unwholesome (nom.)
vā = or
rasāḥ = tastes (nom. pl.)
sarve = all (nom. pl.)
api = even
nīrasāḥ = tasteless (nom. pl.)
bhuktam = eaten (PPP, nom.)
viṣam = poison (nom.)
ghoram = terrible (nom.)
pīyūṣam = nectar (nom.)
iva = like
jīryati = is digested (3rd sg.)		 Indeed (hi) [for him there is] neither (na) wholesome (pathyam) nor (vā) unwholesome food (apathyam), and all (sarve) tastes (rasāḥ) are even (api) tasteless (nīrasāḥ). Even (api) terrible (ghoram) poison (viṣam) when eaten (bhuktam) is digested (jīryati) like (iva) nectar (pīyūṣam). Transcendence of food distinctions
[√jṝ - to digest, √bhuj - to eat]
Mahāmudrā practitioner beyond dietary rules; poison becomes amṛta.		 [To him] food is neither good nor bad, and all flavours are flavourless. Even terrible poison when consumed is digested like nectar.
kṣayakuṣṭhagudāvartagulmājīrṇajvaravyathāḥ |
tasya doṣāḥ kṣayaṃ yānti mahāmudrāṃ tu yo'bhyaset ||62||

De-sandhi:
kṣaya-kuṣṭha-guda-āvarta-gulma-ajīrṇa-jvara-vyathāḥ |
tasya doṣāḥ kṣayam yānti mahā-mudrām tu yaḥ abhyaset ||		 kṣaya = consumption (m.)
kuṣṭha = skin disease (n.)
guda-āvarta = constipation (m.)
gulma = splenitis/tumor (m.)
ajīrṇa = indigestion (n.)
jvara = fever (m.)
vyathāḥ = anxieties/pains (nom. pl.)
tasya = of him (gen.)
doṣāḥ = disorders (nom. pl.)
kṣayam = destruction (acc.)
yānti = go (3rd pl.)
mahā-mudrām = great seal (acc.)
tu = indeed
yaḥ = who (nom.)
abhyaset = should practice (opt.)		 Consumption (kṣaya), skin disease (kuṣṭha), constipation (gudāvarta), splenitis (gulma), indigestion (ajīrṇa), fever (jvara), and anxieties (vyathāḥ)—these disorders (doṣāḥ) of him (tasya) go (yānti) to destruction (kṣayam), who (yaḥ) practices (abhyaset) the mahāmudrā (mahāmudrām). Seven diseases cured by Mahāmudrā
[√yā - to go, √bhyas - to practice]
DV compound listing seven ailments destroyed by practice.		 Consumption, skin disease, constipation, splenitis, indigestion, fever and anxiety: [these] diseases end in him who practises Mahāmudrā.
kathiteyaṃ mahāmudrā mahāsiddhikarī nṛṇām |
gopanīyā prayatnena na deyā yasya kasya cit ||63||

De-sandhi:
kathitā iyam mahā-mudrā mahā-siddhi-karī nṛṇām |
gopanīyā prayatnena na deyā yasya kasya cit ||		 kathitā = told (PPP, nom. f.)
iyam = this (nom. f.)
mahā-mudrā = great seal (nom.)
mahā-siddhi-karī = causing great perfection (nom. f.)
nṛṇām = of men (gen. pl.)
gopanīyā = to be guarded (ger. nom. f.)
prayatnena = with effort (inst.)
na = not
deyā = to be given (ger. nom. f.)
yasya kasya cit = to anyone whatsoever (gen.)		 This (iyam) mahāmudrā (mahāmudrā) has been taught (kathitā), which causes great perfection (mahāsiddhikarī) for men (nṛṇām). It is to be guarded (gopanīyā) with effort (prayatnena), not (na) to be given (deyā) to anyone whatsoever (yasya kasya cit). Secrecy of Mahāmudrā teaching
[√kath - to tell, √gup - to guard, √dā - to give]
Gerundives (gopanīyā, deyā) express obligation; esoteric restriction.		 The Mahāmudrā thus taught brings great success (mahāsiddhi°) to men; it should be guarded carefully, not given to all and sundry.
PRAṆAVAJAPAḤ - MEDITATION ON OM
muktāsanam samāruhya samakāyaśirodharaḥ |
nāsāgradṛṣṭirekākī japedomkāramavyayam ||64||

De-sandhi:
muktāsanam samāruhya sama-kāya-śiraḥ-dharaḥ |
nāsāgra-dṛṣṭiḥ ekākī japet omkāram avyayam ||		 muktāsanam = liberated posture (acc.)
samāruhya = having mounted (abs.)
sama-kāya-śiraḥ-dharaḥ = holding body and head straight (nom.)
nāsāgra-dṛṣṭiḥ = gaze on nose-tip (nom.)
ekākī = alone (nom.)
japet = should recite (opt.)
omkāram = Om (acc.)
avyayam = imperishable (acc.)		 Having mounted (samāruhya) the liberated posture (muktāsanam), holding (dharaḥ) body and head (kāyaśiraḥ) straight (sama), with gaze (dṛṣṭiḥ) on the nose-tip (nāsāgra), alone (ekākī) one should recite (japet) the imperishable (avyayam) Om (omkāram). Physical preparation for Om recitation
[√ruh - to mount, √dhṛ - to hold, √jap - to recite]
Muktāsana + nāsāgra-dṛṣṭi for praṇava practice.		 Sitting in the pose of the liberated (muktāsana), holding the body and head straight, the gaze on the tip of the nose, alone, [the yogi] should repeat the imperishable om.
bhūrbhva.ˍsvaṛime lokāścandrasūryāgnidevatāḥ |
yasya mātrāsu tuṣṭhanti tatparam jyotiromiti ||65||

De-sandhi:
bhūḥ bhuvaḥ svaḥ ime lokāḥ candra-sūrya-agni-devatāḥ |
yasya mātrāsu tuṣṭhanti tat param jyotiḥ om iti ||		 bhūḥ = earth (nom.)
bhuvaḥ = atmosphere (nom.)
svaḥ = heaven (nom.)
ime = these (nom. pl.)
lokāḥ = worlds (nom. pl.)
candra-sūrya-agni-devatāḥ = moon-sun-fire deities (nom. pl.)
yasya = whose (gen.)
mātrāsu = in measures (loc. pl.)
tuṣṭhanti = are satisfied (3rd pl.)
tat = that (nom.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
om = Om
iti = thus		 These (ime) worlds (lokāḥ) - Bhūḥ (bhūḥ), Bhuvaḥ (bhuvaḥ), Svaḥ (svaḥ) - and moon-sun-fire deities (candrasūryāgnidevatāḥ), in whose (yasya) measures (mātrāsu) they are satisfied (tuṣṭhanti), that (tat) is the supreme (param) light (jyotiḥ) - Om (om), thus (iti). Three worlds in Om's syllables
[√tuṣ - to be satisfied]
Bhūr-bhuvaḥ-svaḥ correspond to A-U-M.		 In whose letters are found the worlds (the earth, the atmosphere and the heavens) and the gods (the moon, sun and fire), that is the great light, om.
trayaḥ kālāstrayo lokāstrayo devastrayoḥ svaraḥ |
trayo vedāḥ sthitā yatra tatparam jyotiromiti ||66||

De-sandhi:
trayaḥ kālāḥ trayaḥ lokāḥ trayaḥ devāḥ trayaḥ svarāḥ |
trayaḥ vedāḥ sthitāḥ yatra tat param jyotiḥ om iti ||		 trayaḥ = three (nom. pl.)
kālāḥ = times (nom. pl.)
trayaḥ = three (nom. pl.)
lokāḥ = worlds (nom. pl.)
trayaḥ = three (nom. pl.)
devāḥ = gods (nom. pl.)
trayaḥ = three (nom. pl.)
svarāḥ = sounds/tones (nom. pl.)
trayaḥ = three (nom. pl.)
vedāḥ = Vedas (nom. pl.)
sthitāḥ = established (nom. pl.)
yatra = where (rel.)
tat = that (nom.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
om = Om
iti = thus		 Three (trayaḥ) times (kālāḥ), three (trayaḥ) worlds (lokāḥ), three (trayaḥ) gods (devāḥ), three (trayaḥ) sounds (svarāḥ), three (trayaḥ) Vedas (vedāḥ) are established (sthitāḥ) - where (yatra) that (tat) is the supreme (param) light (jyotiḥ) - Om (om), thus (iti). Threefold cosmic structure in Om
[√sthā - to stand]
All trinities contained within praṇava.		 That in which are found the three times, the three worlds, the three gods, the three accents [and] the three Vedas, that is the great light, om.
akāraśca ukāraśca makāro bindusamjñakaḥ |
tridhā mātrāḥ sthitā yatra tatparam jyotiromiti ||67||

De-sandhi:
a-kāraḥ ca u-kāraḥ ca ma-kāraḥ bindu-samjñakaḥ |
tri-dhā mātrāḥ sthitāḥ yatra tat param jyotiḥ om iti ||		 a-kāraḥ = letter A (nom.)
ca = and
u-kāraḥ = letter U (nom.)
ca = and
ma-kāraḥ = letter M (nom.)
bindu-samjñakaḥ = called bindu (nom.)
tri-dhā = in three ways (adv.)
mātrāḥ = measures (nom. pl.)
sthitāḥ = established (nom. pl.)
yatra = where (rel.)
tat = that (nom.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
om = Om
iti = thus		 The letter A (akāraḥ) and (ca) the letter U (ukāraḥ) and (ca) the letter M (makāraḥ) called bindu (bindusamjñakaḥ) - in three ways (tridhā) the measures (mātrāḥ) are established (sthitāḥ) where (yatra) that (tat) is the supreme (param) light (jyotiḥ) - Om (om), thus (iti). A-U-M with bindu structure
[√sthā - to stand, √jñā - to know]
Explicit phonetic breakdown of praṇava.		 That in which the three letters (a, u and ma, which is called bindu) are found, that is the great light, om.
icchā krīyā tathā jñānā brahmī raudrī ca vaiṣṇavī |
tridhā śaktiḥ sthitā yatra tatparam jyotiromiti ||68||

De-sandhi:
icchā krīyā tathā jñānā brahmī raudrī ca vaiṣṇavī |
tri-dhā śaktiḥ sthitā yatra tat param jyotiḥ om iti ||		 icchā = will/desire (nom.)
krīyā = action (nom.)
tathā = and
jñānā = knowledge (nom.)
brahmī = belonging to Brahmā (nom. f.)
raudrī = belonging to Rudra (nom. f.)
ca = and
vaiṣṇavī = belonging to Viṣṇu (nom. f.)
tri-dhā = in three ways (adv.)
śaktiḥ = power (nom.)
sthitā = established (nom. f.)
yatra = where (rel.)
tat = that (nom.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
om = Om
iti = thus		 Will (icchā), action (krīyā), and (tathā) knowledge (jñānā) - Brahmā's (brahmī), Rudra's (raudrī), and (ca) Viṣṇu's (vaiṣṇavī) - in three ways (tridhā) śakti (śaktiḥ) is established (sthitā) where (yatra) that (tat) is the supreme (param) light (jyotiḥ) - Om (om), thus (iti). Three divine powers in Om
[√sthā - to stand]
Icchā-kriyā-jñāna corresponding to A-U-M.		 That in which the three-fold goddess (śakti) – will, action and knowledge, Brāhmī, Raudrī and Vaiṣṇavī – is located, that is the great light, om.
vacasā tajjapedbījaṃ vapuṣā tatsamābhyaset |
manasā tatsmarennityaṃ tatparaṃ jyotiromiti ||69||

De-sandhi:
vacasā tat japet bījam vapuṣā tat samābhyaset |
manasā tat smaret nityam tat param jyotiḥ om iti ||		 vacasā = with speech (inst.)
tat = that (acc.)
japet = should recite (opt.)
bījam = seed (acc.)
vapuṣā = with body (inst.)
tat = that (acc.)
samābhyaset = should practice (opt.)
manasā = with mind (inst.)
tat = that (acc.)
smaret = should remember (opt.)
nityam = constantly (adv.)
tat = that (nom.)
param = supreme (nom.)
jyotiḥ = light (nom.)
om = Om
iti = thus		 With speech (vacasā) one should recite (japet) that (tat) seed (bījam), with body (vapuṣā) should practice (samābhyaset) that (tat), with mind (manasā) should remember (smaret) that (tat) constantly (nityam) - that (tat) supreme (param) light (jyotiḥ) is Om (om), thus (iti). Threefold practice: vāk-kāya-manas
[√jap - to recite, √abhyas - to practice, √smṛ - to remember]
Body-speech-mind integration in Om practice.		 One should repeat it vocally, as a light one should exercise it using one's body (?) [and] one should constantly think of it with one's mind, the great light, om.
śucirvāpyaśucirvāpi yo japetpraṇavaṃ sadā |
na sa lipyeta pāpena padmapatramivāmbhasā ||70||

De-sandhi:
śuciḥ vā api aśuciḥ vā api yaḥ japet praṇavam sadā |
na saḥ lipyeta pāpena padma-patram iva ambhasā ||		 śuciḥ = pure (nom.)
vā = or
api = even
aśuciḥ = impure (nom.)
vā = or
api = even
yaḥ = who (nom.)
japet = should recite (opt.)
praṇavam = praṇava/Om (acc.)
sadā = always (adv.)
na = not
saḥ = he (nom.)
lipyeta = is stained (pass. opt.)
pāpena = by sin (inst.)
padma-patram = lotus leaf (nom.)
iva = like
ambhasā = by water (inst.)		 Pure (śuciḥ) or (vā) even (api) impure (aśuciḥ) or (vā) even (api), whoever (yaḥ) recites (japet) praṇava (praṇavam) always (sadā), he (saḥ) is not (na) stained (lipyeta) by sin (pāpena), like (iva) a lotus leaf (padmapatram) by water (ambhasā). Purifying power of Om recitation
[√jap - to recite, √lip - to smear/stain]
Om practice purifies regardless of initial state.		 Pure or impure, sin does not stick to he who constantly repeats the syllable om, just as water does not stick to a lotus leaf.
PRĀṆĀYĀMAḤ - BREATH CONTROL
cale vāte calaṃ sarvam niścale niścalam tathā |
yogī sthāṇutvamāpnoti tato vāyuṃ nibandhayet ||71||

De-sandhi:
cale vāte calam sarvam niścale niścalam tathā |
yogī sthāṇutvam āpnoti tataḥ vāyum nibandhayet ||		 cale = when moving (loc. abs.)
vāte = breath (loc.)
calam = moving (nom.)
sarvam = all (nom.)
niścale = when still (loc. abs.)
niścalam = still (nom.)
tathā = likewise
yogī = yogi (nom.)
sthāṇutvam = immobility (acc.)
āpnoti = attains (3rd sg.)
tataḥ = therefore
vāyum = breath (acc.)
nibandhayet = should restrain (opt.)		 When breath (vāte) is moving (cale), all (sarvam) is moving (calam); when still (niścale), likewise (tathā) all is still (niścalam). The yogi (yogī) attains (āpnoti) immobility (sthāṇutvam), therefore (tataḥ) one should restrain (nibandhayet) the breath (vāyum). Fundamental principle of prāṇāyāma
[√cal - to move, √āp - to attain, √bandh - to bind]
Mind-breath connection; stillness through breath control.		 When the breath moves, everything moves, and when it is still, everything is still. Thus the yogi becomes as immovable as Śiva by restraining the breath.
yāvadvāyuḥ sthito dehe tāvajjīvitamucyate |
maraṇam tasya niṣkrāntau tato vāyuṃ nirodhayet ||72||

De-sandhi:
yāvat vāyuḥ sthitaḥ dehe tāvat jīvitam ucyate |
maraṇam tasya niṣkrāntau tataḥ vāyum nirodhayet ||		 yāvat = as long as
vāyuḥ = breath (nom.)
sthitaḥ = established (nom.)
dehe = in body (loc.)
tāvat = so long
jīvitam = life (nom.)
ucyate = is called (pass.)
maraṇam = death (nom.)
tasya = its (gen.)
niṣkrāntau = in departure (loc.)
tataḥ = therefore
vāyum = breath (acc.)
nirodhayet = should restrain (opt.)		 As long as (yāvat) breath (vāyuḥ) is established (sthitaḥ) in the body (dehe), so long (tāvat) it is called (ucyate) life (jīvitam). Death (maraṇam) is in its (tasya) departure (niṣkrāntau), therefore (tataḥ) one should restrain (nirodhayet) breath (vāyum). Breath as life principle
[√sthā - to stand, √vac - to speak, √niṣkram - to depart]
Life-breath equation; breath retention for longevity.		 As long as there is breath in the body there is said to be life; death happens when it leaves, so it should be restrained.
yāvadbaddho marūddehe tāvaccittaṃ nirāśrayam |
yāvaddṛṣṭirbhruvormadhye tāvatkālabhayaṃ kutaḥ ||73||

De-sandhi:
yāvat baddhaḥ marut dehe tāvat cittam nirāśrayam |
yāvat dṛṣṭiḥ bhruvoḥ madhye tāvat kāla-bhayam kutaḥ ||		 yāvat = as long as
baddhaḥ = bound (nom.)
marut = wind/breath (nom.)
dehe = in body (loc.)
tāvat = so long
cittam = mind (nom.)
nirāśrayam = without support (nom.)
yāvat = as long as
dṛṣṭiḥ = vision (nom.)
bhruvoḥ = of eyebrows (gen. dual)
madhye = between (loc.)
tāvat = so long
kāla-bhayam = fear of time/death (nom.)
kutaḥ = whence?		 As long as (yāvat) breath (marut) is bound (baddhaḥ) in the body (dehe), so long (tāvat) the mind (cittam) is without support (nirāśrayam). As long as (yāvat) vision (dṛṣṭiḥ) is between (madhye) the eyebrows (bhruvoḥ), whence (kutaḥ) fear of death (kālabhayam)? Breath control and vision focus
[√bandh - to bind, √dṛś - to see]
Bhrūmadhya-dṛṣṭi eliminates death-fear.		 As long as the breath is fixed in the body the mind has no support. As long as the gaze is between the eyes, there is no fear of death.
pūrvakāle'bhavad brahmā prāṇāyāmaparāyaṇaḥ |
yogino munayaścaiva tataḥ prāṇaṃ niyantrayeṭ ||74||

De-sandhi:
pūrva-kāle abhavat brahmā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇaḥ |
yoginaḥ munayaḥ ca eva tataḥ prāṇam niyantrayeṭ ||		 pūrva-kāle = in ancient times (loc.)
abhavat = became/was (3rd sg. aor.)
brahmā = Brahmā (nom.)
prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇaḥ = devoted to breath control (nom.)
yoginaḥ = yogis (nom. pl.)
munayaḥ = sages (nom. pl.)
ca = and
eva = indeed
tataḥ = therefore
prāṇam = breath (acc.)
niyantrayeṭ = should control (opt.)		 In ancient times (pūrvakāle) Brahmā (brahmā) became (abhavat) devoted to breath control (prāṇāyāmaparāyaṇaḥ), and yogis (yoginaḥ) and sages (munayaḥ) as well (caiva). Therefore (tataḥ) one should control (niyantrayeṭ) breath (prāṇam). Ancient authority for prāṇāyāma
[√bhū - to become, √yam - to control]
Divine and sage precedent for breath practice.		 It was out of fear of death that Brahmā devoted himself to breath-control and for that reason yogis and sages should restrain the breath.
ṣaṣtriṃśadaṅgulo haṃsaḥ prayāṇaṃ kurute bahiḥ |
vāmadakṣiṇamārgeṇa cañcalatvaṃ na dṛśyate ||75||

De-sandhi:
ṣat-triṃśat-aṅgulaḥ haṃsaḥ prayāṇam kurute bahiḥ |
vāma-dakṣiṇa-mārgeṇa cañcalatvam na dṛśyate ||		 ṣat-triṃśat = thirty-six (num.)
aṅgulaḥ = finger-widths (nom.)
haṃsaḥ = breath/soul (nom.)
prayāṇam = journey (acc.)
kurute = makes (3rd sg.)
bahiḥ = outside (adv.)
vāma-dakṣiṇa-mārgeṇa = by left-right path (inst.)
cañcalatvam = restlessness (nom.)
na = not
