### Колесо вритти-санскар (vṛtti-saṃskāra-cakra)

Ключевой вклад бхашьи — описание механизма самсары как самоподдерживающегося колеса:

``` вритти → санскара того же типа → новая вритти того же типа → новая санскара → ... ```

Клишта-вритти порождают клишта-санскары, те порождают новые клишта-вритти — и так без конца (aniśam āvartate). Но то же работает и в обратную сторону: аклишта-вритти укрепляют аклишта-санскары, которые порождают ещё больше аклишта-вритти. Это объясняет, почему и дурные привычки, и хорошая практика имеют тенденцию нарастать.

### Два итога для ума

Когда функция ума завершена (avasitādhikāra), остаются два пути:

- Кайвалья: ум стоит ātmakalpena — "подобно атману." Он стал настолько прозрачен, что напоминает пурушу, хотя пурушей так и не становится. Дуализм сохранён. - Пралая: ум возвращается в недифференцированную пракрити до следующего цикла творения. Это не освобождение — лишь космическая пауза.		 [They are:] valid cognition, error, conceptual construction, sleep, and memory.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.7
pratyakṣānumānāgamāḥ pramāṇāni || YS_1.7 ||
De-sandhi:
pratyakṣa-anumāna-āgamāḥ pramāṇāni ||		 pratyakṣa = direct perception, sense-perception (n. stem in cpd.; prati + akṣa — lit. "before the eyes," "in the presence of the senses"; that which is directly apprehended without mediation)
anumāna = inference, reasoning (n. stem in cpd.; anu + √mā — lit. "measuring after/along"; knowledge derived from a previously known relation between a mark and what it signifies)
āgamāḥ = testimony, verbal tradition, scriptural authority (m. nom. pl.; ā + √gam — "that which has come down"; the plural ending carries for the whole dvandva compound)
pramāṇāni = valid means of knowledge (n. nom. pl.; pra + √mā — "measuring forth"; the instruments by which correct knowledge is established)		 Direct perception (pratyakṣa), inference (anumāna), and testimony (āgama) are the valid means of knowledge (pramāṇāni). Meaning: This sūtra defines the first of the five vṛttis listed in 1.6 — pramāṇa (valid cognition). Patañjali accepts three pramāṇas: pratyakṣa (direct perception), anumāna (inference), and āgama (testimony). This is a characteristically Sāṃkhya-Yoga position, intermediate between the Buddhists (who accept only two: pratyakṣa and anumāna) and the Naiyāyikas (who accept four, adding upamāna/analogy). By including āgama, Patañjali grants scriptural and yogic testimony the status of a valid means of knowledge — crucial for a tradition that transmits experiences not accessible to ordinary perception.

Syntax: Pratyakṣānumānāgamāḥ is a dvandva compound (three co-ordinated stems). Pramāṇāni is the predicate: "these three are pramāṇas." The entire sentence defines what was announced in 1.6 as the first member of the fivefold vṛtti classification.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Direct perception, inference, and testimony are the valid means of knowledge.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.7
[1] indriyapraṇālikayā cittasya bāhyavastūparāgāt tadviṣayā sāmānyaviśeṣātmano 'rthasya viśeṣāvadhāraṇapradhānā vṛttiḥ pratyakṣaṃ pramāṇam. indriya-praṇālikayā = through the channel of the senses (f. inst. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: indriya = sense organ; praṇālikā = channel, conduit, pipe — the senses function as conduits between external objects and the mind)
cittasya = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
bāhya-vastu-uparāgāt = from the colouring by an external object (m. abl. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: bāhya = external; vastu = object, real thing; uparāga = colouring, tinging, from upa + √rañj — the mind "takes on the colour" of whatever object the senses present to it)
tad-viṣayā = having that [object] as its domain (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.)
sāmānya-viśeṣa-ātmanaḥ = whose nature is both universal and particular (m. gen. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: sāmānya = universal, the general; viśeṣa = particular, the specific; ātman = nature, self)
arthasya = of the object (m. gen. sg.)
viśeṣa-avadhāraṇa-pradhānā = chiefly the ascertainment of the particular (f. nom. sg., compound: viśeṣa = particular; avadhāraṇa = ascertainment, determination, from ava + √dhṛ; pradhāna = chief, primary)
vṛttiḥ = the modification, the mental act (f. nom. sg.)
pratyakṣam = direct perception (n. nom. sg.)
pramāṇam = valid means of knowledge (n. nom. sg.)		 The vṛtti which arises through the channel of the senses from the mind's being coloured by an external object, which has that object as its domain, and which is chiefly the ascertainment of the particular in an object whose nature is both universal and particular — that is the valid means of knowledge called pratyakṣa. Vyāsa's definition contains a subtle epistemological point. Every object has two aspects: sāmānya (its universal nature — "cow-ness," "tree-ness") and viśeṣa (its particular features — this specific cow, this individual tree). Pratyakṣa, according to Vyāsa, is viśeṣāvadhāraṇapradhānā — it is primarily concerned with the particular. This contrasts with anumāna, which (as we shall see) is sāmānyāvadhāraṇapradhānā — primarily concerned with the universal. The senses deliver the concrete, singular object; inference abstracts to the general rule.

The mechanism is described through uparāga ("colouring"): the external object literally colours the mind through the sensory channel. This is consistent with the Sāṃkhya model where buddhi takes the shape (ākāra) of the perceived object, like a crystal reflecting a nearby flower.		 The vṛtti which arises through the channel of the senses from the mind's being coloured by an external object, which has that object as its domain, and which is chiefly the ascertainment of the particular in an object whose nature is both universal and particular — that is the valid means of knowledge called pratyakṣa.
[2] phalam aviśiṣṭaḥ pauruṣeyaś cittavṛttibodhaḥ. phalam = the fruit, the result (n. nom. sg.)
aviśiṣṭaḥ = indistinguishable, undifferentiated (m. nom. sg.; a + viśiṣṭa = not distinguished — puruṣa's awareness appears identical to the vṛtti, though it is ontologically different)
pauruṣeyaḥ = belonging to puruṣa, of puruṣa (m. nom. sg.; pauruṣeya, taddhita adj. from puruṣa)
citta-vṛtti-bodhaḥ = the awareness of the mind's modification (m. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: citta = mind; vṛtti = modification; bodha = awareness, cognition)		 The fruit [of perception] is puruṣa's awareness of the mind's modification, [which is] indistinguishable [from the modification itself]. This sentence connects back to the sārūpya doctrine of YS 1.4. The "fruit" (phala) of perception is not the vṛtti itself — it is puruṣa's awareness (bodha) of that vṛtti. Puruṣa's awareness is aviśiṣṭa — "indistinguishable" from the vṛtti. This is precisely the mechanism of bondage: puruṣa's awareness appears identical to whatever the mind is doing, so puruṣa seems to be the perceiver, the thinker, the feeler — when in truth puruṣa merely witnesses. The fruit [of perception] is puruṣa's awareness of the mind's modification, [which is] indistinguishable [from the modification itself].
[3] pratisaṃvedī puruṣa ity upariṣṭād upapādayiṣyāmaḥ. pratisaṃvedī = the experiencer, the one who experiences in return (m. nom. sg.; prati + sam + √vid — "one who becomes distinctly aware"; the prati- prefix suggests reciprocal or responsive awareness)
puruṣaḥ = puruṣa (m. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (quotative)
upariṣṭāt = further on, later (indecl.)
upapādayiṣyāmaḥ = we shall establish, we shall demonstrate (1st pl. fut. caus. upa + √pad — lit. "we shall cause to arrive at," i.e. to prove)		 That puruṣa is the experiencer — this we shall establish further on. Vyāsa defers the proof that puruṣa is the ultimate experiencer (pratisaṃvedī) to a later part of the text (likely referring to the discussion around YS 2.20: draṣṭā dṛśimātraḥ). The term pratisaṃvedī is important — puruṣa does not actively perceive; it "responds" to experience, or more precisely, it is that in whose presence experience becomes conscious.

### II. Anumāna (Умозаключение)		 That puruṣa is the experiencer — this we shall establish further on.
[4] anumeyasya tulyajātīyeṣv anuvṛtto bhinnajātīyebhyo vyāvṛttaḥ saṃbandho yas tadviṣayā sāmānyāvadhāraṇapradhānā vṛttir anumānam. anumeyasya = of the inferable, of what is to be inferred (n. gen. sg., FPP anu + √mā — the thing whose existence or nature is being established through inference)
tulya-jātīyeṣu = in things of the same class, in homogeneous instances (m. loc. pl.; tulya = same, equal; jātīya = belonging to a class/type, from jāti)
anuvṛttaḥ = persisting, continuing, being present (m. nom. sg., PPP anu + √vṛt — the mark is found to persist in all instances of the same class)
bhinna-jātīyebhyaḥ = from things of a different class, from heterogeneous instances (m. abl. pl.; bhinna = different, separate; jātīya = belonging to a class)
vyāvṛttaḥ = excluded, absent (m. nom. sg., PPP vi + ā + √vṛt — the mark is absent from all instances of a different class)
saṃbandhaḥ = connection, relation (m. nom. sg.; the invariable concomitance — vyāpti — between the mark and the inferable)
yaḥ = which (m. nom. sg., relative pronoun)
tad-viṣayā = having that [connection] as its domain (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.)
sāmānya-avadhāraṇa-pradhānā = chiefly the ascertainment of the universal (f. nom. sg., compound: sāmānya = universal, the general; avadhāraṇa = ascertainment; pradhāna = chief)
vṛttiḥ = the modification (f. nom. sg.)
anumānam = inference (n. nom. sg.; anu + √mā — "measuring after")		 That connection which persists in things of the same class as the inferable and is excluded from things of a different class — the vṛtti whose domain is that connection and which is chiefly the ascertainment of the universal — that is anumāna. Vyāsa defines inference through two logical operations that correspond to the classical Indian logical pair of anvaya-vyatireka (positive and negative concomitance):

(a) Anvaya (anuvṛtta — "persisting"): the inferential mark persists in all cases belonging to the same class. Wherever there is smoke, there is fire.

(b) Vyatireka (vyāvṛtta — "excluded"): the inferential mark is absent from all cases belonging to a different class. Where there is no fire, there is no smoke.

The key contrast with pratyakṣa: inference is sāmānyāvadhāraṇapradhānā — "chiefly the ascertainment of the universal." Perception grasps the particular; inference grasps the general rule. This is a precise epistemological symmetry.		 That connection which persists in things of the same class as the inferable and is excluded from things of a different class — the vṛtti whose domain is that connection and which is chiefly the ascertainment of the universal — that is anumāna.
[5 (Пример)] yathā deśāntaraprāpter gatimac candratārakaṃ caitravat, vindhyaś cāprāptir agatiḥ. yathā = for example, just as (indecl.)
deśa-antara-prāpteḥ = because of arrival at another place (f. abl. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: deśa = place; antara = other, different; prāpti = arrival, attainment; ablative of cause)
gatimat = having motion, possessing movement (n. nom. sg.; gati = motion; -mat suffix = possessing)
candra-tārakam = the moon and stars (n. nom. sg., dvandva cpd.: candra = moon; tāraka = stars)
caitra-vat = like Caitra (indecl.; Caitra = a conventional name for a person, like "John Doe"; -vat = like, similar to — the standard of comparison)
vindhyaḥ = the Vindhya [mountain range] (m. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
aprāptiḥ = non-arrival, non-attainment (f. nom. sg.; a + prāpti)
agatiḥ = without motion, motionless (m. nom. sg.; a + gati)		 For example: because of arrival at another place, the moon and stars have motion, like Caitra [a man walking]; and the Vindhya [mountain], [because of] non-arrival, is without motion. A classic example of the anvaya-vyatireka method in action:

Anvaya (положительное сопутствие): "Всё, что прибывает в другое место, обладает движением." Мы видим, что луна и звёзды перемещаются по небу — они оказываются в разных точках в разное время. Точно так же человек по имени Чайтра: он ходит и оказывается в другом месте. Из наблюдаемого факта "луна и звёзды перемещаются" мы выводим общее правило: они обладают движением, подобно Чайтре.

Vyatireka (отрицательное сопутствие): "Что не прибывает в другое место, не обладает движением." Гора Виндхья стоит на месте — она никуда не перемещается. Следовательно, она неподвижна. Отсутствие следствия (перемещения) подтверждает отсутствие причины (движения).

Этот пример элегантен: он берёт нечто, что мы не можем наблюдать непосредственно (природу движения небесных тел), и выводит его существование через наблюдаемый признак (перемещение), подтверждаемый как позитивной, так и негативной инстанцией.

### III. Āgama (Свидетельство)		 For example: because of arrival at another place, the moon and stars have motion, like Caitra [a man walking]; and the Vindhya [mountain], [because of] non-arrival, is without motion.
[6] āptena dṛṣṭo 'numito vārthaḥ paratra svabodhasaṃkrāntaye śabdenopadiśyate, śabdāt tadarthaviṣayā vṛttiḥ śrotur āgamaḥ. āptena = by a trustworthy person, by an authority (m. inst. sg.; āpta = trustworthy, reliable, one who has "arrived at" truth, PPP ā + √āp — lit. "one who has attained"; this is the criterial term — the reliability of āgama rests entirely on the trustworthiness of its source)
dṛṣṭaḥ = seen, directly perceived (m. nom. sg., PPP √dṛś)
anumitaḥ = inferred (m. nom. sg., PPP anu + √mā)
vā = or (indecl.)
arthaḥ = object, matter, meaning (m. nom. sg.)
paratra = to another [person], elsewhere (indecl.; para = other; -tra = locative suffix)
sva-bodha-saṃkrāntaye = for the transference of one's own knowledge (f. dat. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: sva = own; bodha = knowledge, understanding; saṃkrānti = transference, transmission, from sam + √kram — "stepping across together")
śabdena = through words, by means of language (m. inst. sg.)
upadiśyate = is communicated, is taught, is pointed out (3rd sg. pres. pass. upa + √diś)
śabdāt = from that word (m. abl. sg.)
tad-artha-viṣayā = having that object/meaning as its content (f. nom. sg., compound)
vṛttiḥ = the modification (f. nom. sg.)
śrotuḥ = of the hearer (m. gen. sg.; śrotṛ, agent noun from √śru)
āgamaḥ = testimony, verbal tradition (m. nom. sg.)		 An object seen or inferred by a trustworthy person is communicated through words for the transference of their own knowledge to another; the vṛtti of the hearer, arising from that word and having that object as its content — that is āgama. Vyāsa's definition of āgama has a precise structure: (a) an āpta — a trustworthy person — has either perceived or inferred something; (b) they communicate it through words (śabda) for the purpose of transferring (saṃkrānti) their knowledge to another; (c) the vṛtti that arises in the hearer from those words, having the communicated object as its content — that is āgama.

Важно: āgama — это не просто "кто-то что-то сказал." Это передача реального знания (dṛṣṭa или anumita) от достоверного источника (āpta) слушателю через слово. Знание āpta является первичным; слово — лишь средство передачи. Вритти возникает у слушателя (śrotuḥ), а не у говорящего. Это означает, что āgama как прамана — это именно акт восприятия слушателем, а не акт высказывания говорящим.		 An object seen or inferred by a trustworthy person is communicated through words for the transference of their own knowledge to another; the vṛtti of the hearer, arising from that word and having that object as its content — that is āgama.
[7] yasyāśraddheyārtho vaktā na dṛṣṭānumitārthaḥ sa āgamaḥ plavate. yasya = whose (m. gen. sg., relative pronoun; refers to āgamaḥ)
aśraddheya-arthaḥ = one whose content is not to be trusted (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: aśraddheya = not to be believed, from a + √śrad + √dhā + FPP; artha = content, meaning)
vaktā = the speaker (m. nom. sg., agent noun √vac)
na = not (indecl.)
dṛṣṭa-anumita-arthaḥ = one whose content has been [directly] seen or inferred (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: dṛṣṭa = seen; anumita = inferred; artha = content — i.e. the speaker has neither personally perceived nor logically inferred what they claim)
saḥ = that (m. nom. sg.)
āgamaḥ = testimony (m. nom. sg.)
plavate = falters, wavers, floats unsteadily (3rd sg. pres. ātm. √plu — lit. "to float, to swim"; metaphorically: to be unstable, unreliable, adrift)		 That āgama whose speaker has untrustworthy content — [i.e.] has neither seen nor inferred the matter — that āgama falters. The image of plavate ("floats") is vivid — unreliable testimony drifts like a boat without anchor. Vyāsa identifies the precise failure mode: the speaker's content is aśraddheya (untrustworthy) because it is na dṛṣṭānumitārtha — the speaker has neither personally perceived nor logically inferred what they claim. Without either perception or inference grounding the speaker's words, the testimony is baseless. It may sound convincing, but it has no epistemological anchor. That āgama whose speaker has untrustworthy content — [i.e.] has neither seen nor inferred the matter — that āgama falters.
[8] mūlavaktari tu dṛṣṭānumitārthe nirviplavaḥ syāt. mūla-vaktari = in/when the original speaker (m. loc. sg., locative absolute; mūla = root, original, primary; vaktṛ = speaker)
tu = but, however (indecl.)
dṛṣṭa-anumita-arthe = having seen or inferred the content (m. loc. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd., agreeing with vaktari in locative absolute: "the original speaker being one who has seen or inferred the content")
nirviplavaḥ = unwavering, firm, without deviation (m. nom. sg.; niḥ + viplava = without disturbance, without wavering; viplava from vi + √plu — same root as plavate, now negated)
syāt = would be (3rd sg. opt. √as)		 But when the original speaker has seen or inferred the content, [that āgama] would be unwavering. Vyāsa completes the criterion with a beautiful lexical symmetry: the unreliable āgama plavate (floats, drifts — from √plu); the reliable āgama is nirviplava (unwavering — niḥ + vi + √plu, the same root negated). The word mūlavaktari ("in the original/root speaker") introduces an important nuance: testimony may pass through many transmitters, but what matters is whether the original source — the root speaker (mūlavaktṛ) — had direct perception or valid inference. If the origin is secure, the tradition stands firm no matter how many intermediaries it passes through. This is clearly relevant to the Vedic and yogic transmission lineages.

## Архитектура: три праманы по Вьясе

### Симметрия праманы

Вьяса выстраивает элегантную симметрию между тремя праманами:

| Прамана | Механизм | Что определяет прежде всего | Ключевой термин | |---------|----------|---------------------------|-----------------| | Pratyakṣa (восприятие) | Ум "окрашивается" (uparāga) внешним объектом через канал чувств (indriyapraṇālikā) | Viśeṣa — частное, конкретное | uparāga (окрашивание) | | Anumāna (умозаключение) | Ум схватывает связь (saṃbandha), которая присутствует в однородных случаях и отсутствует в разнородных | Sāmānya — общее, универсальное | anvaya-vyatireka (наличие-отсутствие) | | Āgama (свидетельство) | Ум слушателя формирует вритти из слов достоверного говорящего | То, что видел или вывел āpta | saṃkrānti (передача знания) |

### Общий плод всех праман

Каков бы ни был тип праманы, phala (плод) один и тот же: aviśiṣṭaḥ pauruṣeyaḥ cittavṛttibodhaḥ — "неотличимое от вритти осознавание пурушей модификации ума." Все три праманы производят вритти в уме; пуруша осознаёт эту вритти; и это осознавание кажется тождественным самой вритти (aviśiṣṭa). Это вновь отсылает к сарупье из YS 1.4.

### Критерий надёжности агамы

Вьяса предлагает чёткий критерий:

- Ненадёжная агама (plavate — "плавает, дрейфует"): говорящий не видел и не вывел содержание своих слов. - Надёжная агама (nirviplava — "непоколебимая"): mūlavaktṛ (корневой, изначальный говорящий) лично воспринял или логически вывел то, о чём говорит.

Красота в словесной игре: plavate (от √plu — "плыть") и nirviplava (niḥ + vi + √plu — "без качки") — тот же корень, отрицание которого превращает дрейф в твёрдость. И практическое значение для йогической традиции: цепочка передачи может быть сколь угодно длинной — если корневой источник надёжен, традиция стоит непоколебимо.		 But when the original speaker has seen or inferred the content, [that āgama] would be unwavering.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.8
viparyayo mithyājñānam atadrūpapratiṣṭham || YS_1.8 ||
De-sandhi:
viparyayaḥ mithyā-jñānam a-tad-rūpa-pratiṣṭham ||		 viparyayaḥ = error, misperception, wrong cognition (m. nom. sg.; vi + pari + √i — lit. "going around wrongly," a reversal, an inversion of the real)
mithyā = falsely, wrongly (indecl.; used as first member of compound: "false-")
jñānam = knowledge, cognition (n. nom. sg.; from √jñā)
a-tad-rūpa-pratiṣṭham = established in a form that is not that [of the real object] (n. nom. sg., compound: a = not; tad = that [object]; rūpa = form; pratiṣṭha = established, grounded — the cognition has a definite form, but that form does not correspond to the actual object)		 Error (viparyayaḥ) is false knowledge (mithyā-jñānam), established in a form that is not that [of the real object] (a-tad-rūpa-pratiṣṭham). Meaning: Viparyaya is the second of the five vṛttis. It is a cognition that has content — it grasps something — but that content does not correspond to reality. The key phrase is atadrūpapratiṣṭham: the knowledge is "established in a form that is not that" — i.e. not the form of the actual object. The classic example is seeing a rope and cognising a snake. The cognition has a definite shape (snake), but that shape does not belong to the object (rope). Viparyaya is thus not the absence of knowledge but a positive misattribution — the mind actively constructs a wrong form and projects it onto reality.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Viparyayaḥ is the subject; mithyājñānam and atadrūpapratiṣṭham are two predicate descriptors that together define it.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Error is false knowledge, grounded in a form that does not belong to the real object.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.8
[1–2] sa kasmān na pramāṇam. yataḥ pramāṇena bādhyate. saḥ = it, that [viparyaya] (m. nom. sg.)
kasmāt = why? (interrogative, abl. of kim)
na = not (indecl.)
pramāṇam = valid knowledge (n. nom. sg.)
yataḥ = because (indecl.)
pramāṇena = by valid knowledge (n. inst. sg.)
bādhyate = is sublated, is contradicted, is overridden (3rd sg. pres. pass. √bādh — a technical epistemological term: when a later, correct cognition reveals the earlier cognition to be false)		 Why is it [viparyaya] not a pramāṇa? Because it is sublated by pramāṇa. Vyāsa raises the natural question: since viparyaya is a form of knowledge (jñāna), why does it not count as valid knowledge? The answer is bādhana — sublation. A correct cognition can override and cancel viparyaya; viparyaya can never override pramāṇa. This asymmetry is what distinguishes truth from error. Why is it [viparyaya] not a pramāṇa? Because it is sublated by pramāṇa.
[3] bhūtārthaviṣayatvāt pramāṇasya. bhūta-artha-viṣayatvāt = because of having a real object as its content (n. abl. sg., compound: bhūta = real, actually existing; artha = object; viṣayatva = the state of having as content; ablative of reason)
pramāṇasya = of pramāṇa (n. gen. sg.)		 Because pramāṇa has a real object as its content. The criterion of truth according to Vyāsa: pramāṇa has bhūtārtha (a really existing object) as its content. Error, by contrast, has atadrūpa (a form that is not that of the real object). Truth is anchored in reality; error is not. Because pramāṇa has a real object as its content.
[4–5] tatra pramāṇena bādhanam apramāṇasya dṛṣṭam. tadyathā dvicandradarśanaṃ sadviṣayeṇaikacandradarśanena bādhyata iti. tatra = in this regard, there (indecl.)
pramāṇena = by valid knowledge (n. inst. sg.)
bādhanam = sublation, overriding (n. nom. sg., from √bādh)
apramāṇasya = of the non-valid [knowledge] (n. gen. sg.)
dṛṣṭam = is observed (n. nom. sg., PPP √dṛś)
tad yathā = for example, namely (indecl.)
dvi-candra-darśanam = the seeing of two moons (n. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: dvi = two; candra = moon; darśana = seeing)
sat-viṣayeṇa = which has a real object (n. inst. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: sat = real, existing; viṣaya = object)
eka-candra-darśanena = by the seeing of one moon (n. inst. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.)
bādhyate = is sublated (3rd sg. pres. pass. √bādh)
iti = thus (quotative)		 In this regard, the sublation of the non-valid [knowledge] by valid knowledge is observed. For example, the seeing of two moons is sublated by the seeing of one moon, which has a real object. Пример с двумя лунами: при определённых нарушениях зрения (или при нажатии на глаз) человек видит две луны вместо одной. Это — viparyaya: познание определённое, уверенное ("я вижу две луны"), но ложное. Когда зрение приходит в норму и человек видит одну луну — это pramāṇa, и оно bādhyate (отменяет, снимает) ложное двойное видение. Ключевой признак истинного знания: оно имеет sadviṣaya ("реальный объект") — одна луна реально существует. Две луны — нет. In this regard, the sublation of the non-valid [knowledge] by valid knowledge is observed. For example, the seeing of two moons is sublated by the seeing of one moon, which has a real object.
[6] seyaṃ pañcaparvā bhavaty avidyā. sā = she, that (f. nom. sg.)
iyam = this (f. nom. sg.)
pañca-parvā = having five segments, five-jointed (f. nom. sg.; pañca = five; parvan = joint, segment, section — the image is of a bamboo stalk with five joints/nodes)
bhavati = is (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)
avidyā = ignorance (f. nom. sg.)		 This [viparyaya] is avidyā, having five segments. Поворотный момент бхашьи. Вьяса отождествляет viparyaya с avidyā (неведением) — и это не просто "заблуждение," а корневая клеша, которая имеет пять "сочленений" (parvan). Образ — как бамбуковый стебель с пятью узлами. Каждый узел — одна из пяти клеш. This [viparyaya] is avidyā, having five segments.
[7] avidyāsmitārāgadveṣābhiniveśāḥ kleśā iti. avidyā = ignorance, non-knowledge (f.; a + vidyā — the root affliction, the failure to see things as they truly are)
asmitā = egoism, I-am-ness (f.; asmi + -tā — the false identification of puruṣa with buddhi, the sense of "I am this")
rāga = attachment, desire (m.; √rañj — "colouring"; the mind coloured by attraction)
dveṣa = aversion, hatred (m.; √dviṣ — "to hate")
abhiniveśāḥ = clinging to life, will to live (m. nom. pl.; abhi + ni + √viś — "entering into completely"; the instinctive dread of death)
kleśāḥ = the afflictions (m. nom. pl.)
iti = thus (quotative)		 Ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life — these are the kleśas. Эти пять клеш — одни из самых важных понятий всей Йогасутры (подробно определяются в YS 2.3–2.9). Вьяса помещает их здесь, в контексте viparyaya, потому что все клеши суть формы неведения — формы "ложного знания, укоренённого в форме, которая не принадлежит реальному объекту." Аsmitā принимает буддхи за пурушу; rāga принимает мимолётное удовольствие за нечто стоящее; dveṣa принимает страдание за нечто присущее объекту; abhiniveśa принимает тело за "я". Ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life — these are the kleśas.
[8] eta eva svasaṃjñābhis tamo moho mahāmohas tāmisro 'ndhatāmisra iti. ete = these (m. nom. pl.)
eva = very, same (indecl.)
sva-saṃjñābhiḥ = by their own names, by their proper designations (f. inst. pl.; sva = own; saṃjñā = name, technical term)
tamaḥ = darkness (n. nom. sg.; = avidyā)
mohaḥ = delusion (m. nom. sg.; = asmitā)
mahā-mohaḥ = great delusion (m. nom. sg.; = rāga)
tāmisraḥ = gloom (m. nom. sg.; = dveṣa)
andha-tāmisraḥ = blind darkness (m. nom. sg.; andha = blind; tāmisra = darkness; = abhiniveśa)
iti = thus (quotative)		 These very [kleśas], by their own [Sāṃkhya] names, are: darkness, delusion, great delusion, gloom, and blind darkness. Вьяса приводит альтернативную номенклатуру из Сāṃкхья-традиции (ср. Sāṃkhyakārikā 48, где перечислены пять форм viparyaya). Соответствия:

| Yogasūtra | Sāṃkhya | Значение | |-----------|---------|----------| | avidyā | tamas | тьма | | asmitā | moha | наваждение | | rāga | mahāmoha | великое наваждение | | dveṣa | tāmisra | мрак | | abhiniveśa | andhatāmisra | слепая тьма |

Каждое Sāṃkhya-название метафорически описывает природу соответствующей клеши: незнание — тьма; отождествление себя с интеллектом — наваждение; привязанность к удовольствию — величайшее наваждение; ненависть — мрак; а цепляние за жизнь — полная слепота во тьме.		 These very [kleśas], by their own [Sāṃkhya] names, are: darkness, delusion, great delusion, gloom, and blind darkness.
[9] ete cittamalaprasaṅgenābhidhāsyante. ete = these (m. nom. pl.)
citta-mala-prasaṅgena = in connection with the impurities of the mind (m. inst. sg., compound: citta = mind; mala = impurity, stain; prasaṅga = connection, occasion, context)
abhidhāsyante = will be discussed, will be stated (3rd pl. fut. pass. abhi + √dhā)		 These will be discussed in connection with the impurities of the mind. Вьяса откладывает полное обсуждение клеш до второй пады (Sādhanapāda, YS 2.3–2.9), где они будут определены одна за другой. Здесь он лишь устанавливает связь: viparyaya = avidyā = пятичленная система клеш. These will be discussed in connection with the impurities of the mind.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.9
śabdajñānānupātī vastuśūnyo vikalpaḥ || YS_1.9 ||
De-sandhi:
śabda-jñāna-anupātī vastu-śūnyaḥ vikalpaḥ ||		 śabda-jñāna-anupātī = following upon verbal knowledge (m. nom. sg., compound: śabda = word, language; jñāna = knowledge, cognition; anupātin = following after, arising in consequence of, from anu + √pat — "falling after")
vastu-śūnyaḥ = devoid of a [corresponding] real thing/object (m. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: vastu = real thing, actual entity; śūnya = empty, devoid of)
vikalpaḥ = conceptual construction, verbal fabrication, imagination (m. nom. sg.; vi + √kḷp — "fashioning variously"; the mind's capacity to construct meaningful content purely from words, without any corresponding reality)		 Vikalpa (vikalpaḥ) is [that vṛtti which] follows upon verbal knowledge (śabda-jñāna-anupātī) and is devoid of a [corresponding] object (vastu-śūnyaḥ). Meaning: Vikalpa is the third of the five vṛttis. It is distinct from both pramāṇa (which has a real object) and viparyaya (which has a wrong object). Vikalpa has no object — it is vastuśūnya, "empty of a thing." It arises purely from the power of words to create meaning. When we say "the horn of a hare" or "the son of a barren woman," we understand something — a vṛtti arises — yet there is nothing in reality corresponding to it. This is vikalpa. It is not error (we do not mistake something for something else); it is pure conceptual fabrication arising from the combinatory power of language.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Two predicates describe vikalpaḥ: (a) śabdajñānānupātī — it follows upon verbal knowledge; (b) vastuśūnyaḥ — it is devoid of a real object. Together they define vikalpa as language-generated content without ontological grounding.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Conceptual construction is the vṛtti that follows verbal knowledge and is devoid of a corresponding object.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.9
[1–2] sa na pramāṇopārohī. na viparyayopārohī ca. saḥ = it, that [vikalpa] (m. nom. sg.)
na = not (indecl.)
pramāṇa-upārohī = riding upon pramāṇa, mounted on pramāṇa, reducible to pramāṇa (m. nom. sg.; pramāṇa + upārohin, from upa + ā + √ruh — "one who mounts upon, one who rides atop")
na = not (indecl.)
viparyaya-upārohī = riding upon viparyaya, reducible to viparyaya (m. nom. sg.)
ca = and, nor (indecl.)		 It [vikalpa] does not ride upon pramāṇa. Nor does it ride upon viparyaya. Вьяса устанавливает категориальную самостоятельность викальпы. Она не является подвидом праманы (ведь у неё нет реального объекта) и не является подвидом випарьяи (ведь она не приписывает объекту ложную форму). Образ upārohin ("всадник") замечателен: викальпа не "сидит верхом" ни на истинном, ни на ложном знании — она категорически отлична от обоих. It [vikalpa] does not ride upon pramāṇa. Nor does it ride upon viparyaya.
[3] vastuśūnyatve 'pi śabdajñānamāhātmyanibandhano vyavahāro dṛśyate. vastu-śūnyatve = in the state of being devoid of a real object (n. loc. sg.; vastu = thing; śūnyatva = emptiness, devoidness; concessive locative: "even when devoid of…")
api = even, although (indecl.)
śabda-jñāna-māhātmya-nibandhanaḥ = grounded in the greatness/power of verbal knowledge (m. nom. sg., compound: śabda = word; jñāna = knowledge; māhātmya = greatness, power, authority; nibandhana = grounded in, dependent upon, from ni + √bandh)
vyavahāraḥ = practical usage, conventional discourse, everyday transacting (m. nom. sg.; vi + ava + √hṛ)
dṛśyate = is observed (3rd sg. pres. pass. √dṛś)		 Even though it is devoid of a corresponding object, practical usage grounded in the power of verbal knowledge is observed. Ключевой тезис: вещи не существует (vastuśūnya), но vyavahāra (практическое использование, повседневное оперирование понятием) — существует. Язык обладает māhātmya — собственной силой, самобытной мощью. Слова способны порождать значимые ментальные содержания даже без онтологического референта. Мы можем осмысленно рассуждать о "рогах зайца" — это не истина и не заблуждение, а вербальная конструкция, которая функционирует в дискурсе. Even though it is devoid of a corresponding object, practical usage grounded in the power of verbal knowledge is observed.
[4–5] tad yathā caitanyaṃ puruṣasya svarūpam iti. yadā citir eva puruṣas tadā kim atra kena vyapadiśyate. tad yathā = for example (indecl.)
caitanyam = consciousness (n. nom. sg.; abstract noun from cetana = conscious)
puruṣasya = of puruṣa (m. gen. sg.)
sva-rūpam = own-nature, own form (n. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (quotative)
yadā = when, if (indecl.)
citiḥ = consciousness, pure awareness (f. nom. sg.)
eva = itself, indeed (indecl., emphatic)
puruṣaḥ = puruṣa (m. nom. sg.)
tadā = then (indecl.)
kim = what? (n. nom. sg., interrogative)
atra = here, in this [statement] (indecl.)
kena = by what? by means of what? (n. inst. sg., interrogative)
vyapadiśyate = is designated, is described (3rd sg. pres. pass. vi + apa + √diś)		 For example: "Consciousness is the own-nature of puruṣa." If consciousness itself IS puruṣa, then what is being designated here by what? Блестящий пример. Утверждение "сознание есть собственная природа пуруши" (caitanyaṃ puruṣasya svarūpam) звучит осмысленно — мы его понимаем, оно порождает вритти. Но если сознание (citi) тождественно пуруше (citir eva puruṣaḥ), то фраза "сознание принадлежит пуруше" предполагает различие между владельцем и свойством, которого в действительности нет. Нельзя сказать "вода мокрая" так, как будто мокрость — это одно, а вода — другое: мокрость и есть вода. Точно так же сознание не "свойство" пуруши — оно и есть пуруша. Высказывание создаёт иллюзию отношения там, где есть тождество. Это чистая викальпа: слова конструируют различие, которого нет в реальности. For example: "Consciousness is the own-nature of puruṣa." If consciousness itself IS puruṣa, then what is being designated here by what?
[6] bhavati ca vyapadeśe vṛttiḥ. bhavati = arises, exists (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)
ca = and (indecl.)
vyapadeśe = in the designation, in the act of referring, in the verbal description (m. loc. sg.; vi + apa + √diś — "pointing out separately")
vṛttiḥ = a modification, a mental event (f. nom. sg.)		 And in the [act of] designation, a vṛtti arises. Даже при отсутствии реального объекта сам акт именования (vyapadeśa) порождает вритти в уме. Назвать — значит помыслить. Слово создаёт ментальный объект. And in the [act of] designation, a vṛtti arises.
[7] yathā caitrasya gaur iti. yathā = like, for example (indecl.)
caitrasya = of Caitra (m. gen. sg.; a conventional proper name)
gauḥ = cow (f. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (quotative)		 Like [the statement] "Caitra's cow." Второй пример, проще первого: "корова Чайтры." Высказывание предполагает отношение владения между Чайтрой и коровой. Но это отношение — не физическая вещь; его нельзя увидеть, потрогать или взвесить. Само "владение" (sva) — это вербальная конструкция, наложенная на реальность. Корова существует, Чайтра существует, но отношение "его корова" — это викальпа: конструкт, обусловленный силой слов. Like [the statement] "Caitra's cow."
[8] tathā pratiṣiddhavastudharmo niṣkriyaḥ puruṣaḥ, tiṣṭhati bāṇaḥ sthāsyati sthita iti. tathā = likewise (indecl.)
pratiṣiddha-vastu-dharmaḥ = one in whom the property of the real thing is negated (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: pratiṣiddha = negated, denied, PPP prati + √sidh; vastu = thing, real entity; dharma = property)
niṣkriyaḥ = actionless, without activity (m. nom. sg.; niḥ + kriyā = devoid of action)
puruṣaḥ = puruṣa (m. nom. sg.)
tiṣṭhati = stands (3rd sg. pres. √sthā)
bāṇaḥ = arrow (m. nom. sg.)
sthāsyati = will stand (3rd sg. fut. √sthā)
sthitaḥ = has stood, stood (m. nom. sg., PPP √sthā)
iti = thus (quotative)		 Likewise: "Puruṣa is actionless" — where the property of the real thing is negated. [Or:] "The arrow stands, will stand, has stood." Третий пример — ещё более тонкий. "Пуруша бездеятелен" (niṣkriyaḥ). Это звучит как описание пуруши, как приписывание ему свойства "бездеятельность." Но на самом деле niṣkriya — это отрицание свойства (деятельности), а не утверждение позитивного свойства. Мы не говорим "у пуруши есть бездеятельность" как какой-то обладаемый атрибут — мы говорим "у пуруши нет деятельности."

Пример со стрелой делает это ещё яснее: "стрела стоит, будет стоять, стояла" (tiṣṭhati, sthāsyati, sthitaḥ). Глагол √sthā ("стоять") здесь описывает не движение, а отсутствие движения. Языковая форма (активный глагол) создаёт впечатление действия — но содержание пусто.		 Likewise: "Puruṣa is actionless" — where the property of the real thing is negated. [Or:] "The arrow stands, will stand, has stood."
[9] gatinivṛttau dhātvarthamātraṃ gamyate. gati-nivṛttau = in the cessation of motion (f. loc. sg.; gati = motion, movement; nivṛtti = cessation, stopping, from ni + √vṛt)
dhātu-artha-mātram = only the bare meaning of the verbal root (n. nom. sg.; dhātu = verbal root; artha = meaning; mātra = mere, only, nothing more than)
gamyate = is understood, is grasped (3rd sg. pres. pass. √gam)		 In the cessation of motion, only the bare meaning of the verbal root is understood. Когда стрела "стоит" — никакого действия не происходит. Мы понимаем лишь "голый" смысл корня √sthā ("пребывание"), а не реальное событие. Это — dhātuarthamātra: чистое вербальное значение без онтологического коррелята. In the cessation of motion, only the bare meaning of the verbal root is understood.
[10] tathānutpattidharmā puruṣa iti, utpattidharmasyābhāvamātram avagamyate na puruṣānvayī dharmaḥ. tathā = likewise (indecl.)
an-utpatti-dharmā = having the property of non-origination (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: an = non; utpatti = origination, arising; dharman = property)
puruṣaḥ = puruṣa (m. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (quotative)
utpatti-dharmasya = of the property of origination (m. gen. sg.)
abhāva-mātram = only the absence (n. nom. sg.; abhāva = absence, non-existence; mātra = mere)
avagamyate = is understood (3rd sg. pres. pass. ava + √gam)
na = not (indecl.)
puruṣa-anvayī = inhering in puruṣa, connected to puruṣa (m. nom. sg.; puruṣa + anvayin = following after, belonging to, from anu + √i)
dharmaḥ = property, attribute (m. nom. sg.)		 Likewise [in the statement] "puruṣa has the property of non-origination" — only the absence of the property of origination is understood, not a positive property that inheres in puruṣa. Самый глубокий пример в серии. "Пуруша не рождается" (anutpattidharmā puruṣaḥ). Грамматически это выглядит как приписывание свойства: пуруша обладает чем-то — свойством "нерождаемости." Но Вьяса разъясняет: на самом деле мы понимаем лишь abhāvamātra — "простое отсутствие" свойства рождаемости. Мы не добавляем пуруше новое позитивное свойство "нерождаемость" — мы лишь убираем свойство "рождаемость." Отрицание не создаёт реального атрибута (na puruṣānvayī dharmaḥ) — оно создаёт vikalpita dharma (сконструированное свойство), существующее только в языке. Likewise [in the statement] "puruṣa has the property of non-origination" — only the absence of the property of origination is understood, not a positive property that inheres in puruṣa.
[11] tasmād vikalpitaḥ sa dharmas tena cāsti vyavahāra iti. tasmāt = therefore (indecl.)
vikalpitaḥ = conceptually constructed, imagined, fabricated (m. nom. sg., PPP vi + √kḷp — the same root as vikalpa, used as its participle: "that which has been vikalpa-ed")
saḥ = that (m. nom. sg.)
dharmaḥ = property, attribute (m. nom. sg.)
tena = by that, by it (m. inst. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
asti = exists (3rd sg. pres. √as)
vyavahāraḥ = practical usage, conventional discourse (m. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (quotative)		 Therefore that property is conceptually constructed, and by it practical usage exists. Заключение: свойство vikalpita (сконструировано), но vyavahāra (практическое применение) от этого не страдает. Мы можем осмысленно говорить "пуруша не рождается," "пуруша бездеятелен," "сознание — природа пуруши" — и эти высказывания функционируют в философском дискурсе. Они порождают вритти, они передают смысл, они полезны. Но их содержание vastuśūnya — пусто с точки зрения реальности. Это и есть викальпа: язык, создающий значение без онтологического основания.

## Архитектура: три типа познания и пустота языка

### Тройная классификация по наличию/отсутствию объекта

| Vṛtti | Объект | Соответствие реальности | |-------|--------|------------------------| | Pramāṇa (1.7) | Реальный объект (bhūtārtha) | Форма познания соответствует объекту | | Viparyaya (1.8) | Реальный объект, но ложно постигнутый | Форма познания НЕ соответствует объекту (atadrūpa) | | Vikalpa (1.9) | Объект отсутствует (vastuśūnya) | Нечему соответствовать — чистая вербальная конструкция |

### Четыре примера викальпы у Вьясы

Вьяса выстраивает градацию от простого к сложному:

1. "Сознание — природа пуруши" — язык создаёт видимость отношения "свойство/владелец" между тем, что на самом деле тождественно. Citi = puruṣa, но слова конструируют различие.

2. "Корова Чайтры" — отношение владения. Корова реальна, Чайтра реален, но отношение "его" — вербальная конструкция.

3. "Стрела стоит" — активный глагол описывает отсутствие действия. Грамматическая форма подсказывает событие, но содержание — пустота (gatinivṛtti).

4. "Пуруша не рождается" — грамматическая форма подсказывает позитивное свойство ("нерождаемость"), но реально понимается лишь abhāvamātra (простое отсутствие рождаемости). Это не свойство, присущее пуруше (na puruṣānvayī dharmaḥ), а чистый конструкт (vikalpita dharmaḥ).

### Практическое значение для йогина

Осознание того, что огромная часть нашего мышления — это викальпа (вербальные конструкции без реального референта), имеет прямое отношение к практике. Бóльшая часть ментального шума — это не восприятие и не заблуждение, а безостановочное языковое конструирование: внутренний монолог, который строит отношения, свойства и события там, где их нет. Распознать это — первый шаг к ниродхе.		 Therefore that property is conceptually constructed, and by it practical usage exists.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.10
abhāvapratyayālambanā vṛttir nidrā || YS_1.10 ||
De-sandhi:
abhāva-pratyaya-ālambanā vṛttiḥ nidrā ||		 abhāva-pratyaya-ālambanā = having as its support a cognition of absence (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: abhāva = absence, non-existence, from a + √bhū; pratyaya = cognition, mental content; ālambana = support, substrate, that which is held on to, from ā + √lamb)
vṛttiḥ = fluctuation, modification, mental activity (f. nom. sg., from √vṛt)
nidrā = sleep (f. nom. sg., from ni + √drā — "that which draws inward")		 Sleep (nidrā) is a fluctuation (vṛttiḥ) whose support (ālambanā) is a cognition of absence (abhāva-pratyaya). Meaning: Sleep is classified as the fourth of the five vṛttis. The key insight is that nidrā is not the mere absence of mental activity — it is a positive vṛtti, a specific kind of cognition. Its defining characteristic is that it takes abhāva (absence) as its content. During sleep, the mind is active, but its object is absence itself — the blankness, the non-apprehension of any specific content. This is why sleep leaves a trace (saṃskāra): upon waking, one can say "I slept well" or "I slept badly."

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Nidrā is the subject; vṛttiḥ is the predicate; abhāvapratyayālambanā is a bahuvrīhi compound qualifying vṛttiḥ — "a vṛtti which has as its support (ālambanā) a pratyaya of abhāva."

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Sleep is a mental fluctuation that rests upon a cognition of absence.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.10
[1] sā ca saṃprabodhe pratyavamarśāt pratyayaviśeṣaḥ. sā = that [sleep] (f. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
saṃprabodhe = upon full awakening, at the time of waking up (m. loc. sg.; sam + pra + √budh — "complete awakening")
pratyavamarśāt = from the retrospective awareness, from reflective recognition (m. abl. sg.; prati + ava + √mṛś — lit. "touching back," reflecting on past experience)
pratyaya-viśeṣaḥ = a specific cognition, a particular kind of mental content (m. nom. sg.; pratyaya = cognition; viśeṣa = specific, distinct)		 And that [sleep] is a specific cognition — [this is known] from the retrospective awareness upon full awakening. Вьяса начинает с доказательства того, что сон — это вритти. Откуда мы знаем? Из pratyavamarśa — ретроспективного осознания при пробуждении. Если бы во сне не было никакого ментального содержания, откуда бы появились воспоминания о качестве сна? Термин pratyavamarśa (от prati + ava + √mṛś, "обратное касание") означает рефлексивное обращение к только что пережитому опыту. And that [sleep] is a specific cognition — [this is known] from the retrospective awareness upon full awakening.
[2] katham, sukham aham asvāpsam. prasannaṃ me manaḥ. prajñāṃ me viśāradīkaroti. katham = how? (indecl.)
sukham = happily, pleasantly (adv./n. acc. sg.)
aham = I (1st sg. nom.)
asvāpsam = I slept (1st sg. aorist √svap — a rare aorist form: a-svāp-s-am)
prasannam = clear, calm, serene (n. nom. sg., PPP pra + √sad — lit. "settled down")
me = my (1st sg. gen.)
manaḥ = mind (n. nom. sg.)
prajñām = understanding, wisdom (f. acc. sg.)
me = my (1st sg. gen.)
viśāradīkaroti = makes sharp, makes skilled (3rd sg. pres.; viśāradī + √kṛ, denominative-like construction: viśārada = expert, skilled, clear + karoti = makes)		 How [do we know]? [One says:] "I slept happily. My mind is clear. It makes my understanding sharp." Первый из трёх "протоколов пробуждения" — сон с преобладанием саттвы. Человек просыпается и говорит: "я хорошо спал" (sukham asvāpsam). Форма asvāpsam — это аорист от √svap, довольно редкий. Результат саттвического сна: ум ясен (prasanna), понимание обострено (viśāradīkaroti). Это прямое доказательство того, что во сне было определённое качество ментального содержания, хотя объект отсутствовал. How [do we know]? [One says:] "I slept happily. My mind is clear. It makes my understanding sharp."
[3] duḥkham aham asvāpsam. styānaṃ me mano bhramaty anavasthitam. duḥkham = badly, painfully (adv./n. acc. sg.)
aham = I (1st sg. nom.)
asvāpsam = I slept (1st sg. aor. √svap)
styānam = sluggish, torpid, dull (n. nom. sg., PPP √styai — "to stiffen, become rigid")
me = my (1st sg. gen.)
manaḥ = mind (n. nom. sg.)
bhramati = wanders, roams (3rd sg. pres. √bhram)
anavasthitam = unsettled, unstable (n. nom. sg.; an + ava + √sthā + ita — "not standing firm")		 "I slept badly. My mind is sluggish, it wanders, it is unsettled." Второй протокол — сон с преобладанием раджаса. Пробуждение тяжёлое: ум вялый (styāna), блуждает (bhramati), не может сосредоточиться (anavasthita). Опять-таки, эти ощущения свидетельствуют о том, что во время сна имело место определённое ментальное состояние. "I slept badly. My mind is sluggish, it wanders, it is unsettled."
[4] gāḍhaṃ mūḍho 'ham asvāpsam. gurūṇi me gātrāṇi. klāntaṃ me cittam. alasaṃ muṣitam iva tiṣṭhatīti. gāḍham = deeply (adv.; PPP √gāh — "to plunge, immerse deeply")
mūḍhaḥ = dull, stupefied (m. nom. sg., PPP √muh — "to be confused, bewildered")
aham = I (1st sg. nom.)
asvāpsam = I slept (1st sg. aor. √svap)
gurūṇi = heavy (n. nom. pl.)
me = my (1st sg. gen.)
gātrāṇi = limbs (n. nom. pl.)
klāntam = exhausted, fatigued (n. nom. sg., PPP √klam — "to become weary")
me = my (1st sg. gen.)
cittam = mind (n. nom. sg.)
alasam = lazy, sluggish (n. nom. sg.; a + lasa, from √las — "to be languid")
muṣitam = robbed, stolen (n. nom. sg., PPP √muṣ — "to steal, to rob")
iva = as if (indecl.)
tiṣṭhati = remains, stands (3rd sg. pres. √sthā)
iti = thus, end-quote (indecl.)		 "I slept deeply and dully. My limbs are heavy. My mind is exhausted. It remains as if lazy and robbed [of energy]." Третий протокол — сон с преобладанием тамаса. Самый тяжёлый: глубокий (gāḍha), тупой (mūḍha) сон. После него тело тяжёлое (gurūṇi gātrāṇi), ум изнурён (klānta), и всё словно ограблено (muṣita) — энергия украдена. Слово muṣitam iva замечательно: ум стоит (tiṣṭhati), но как будто обворован — он на месте, но опустошён.

Три протокола соответствуют трём гунам: саттва (ясный сон), раджас (беспокойный сон), тамас (тупой сон). Это показывает, что нидра, как и все вритти, окрашена гунами.		 "I slept deeply and dully. My limbs are heavy. My mind is exhausted. It remains as if lazy and robbed [of energy]."
[5] sa khalv ayaṃ prabuddhasya pratyavamarśo na syād asati pratyayānubhave. saḥ = that (m. nom. sg.)
khalu = certainly, indeed (indecl., emphatic particle)
ayam = this (m. nom. sg.)
prabuddhasya = of the awakened person (m. gen. sg., PPP pra + √budh)
pratyavamarśaḥ = retrospective awareness, reflective recognition (m. nom. sg.; prati + ava + √mṛś)
na = not (indecl.)
syāt = would be, would exist (3rd sg. opt. √as)
asati = in the absence of, when there is no (m. loc. sg., PPA √as — locative absolute: "there not being")
pratyaya-anubhave = experience of a cognition (m. loc. sg.; pratyaya = cognition; anubhava = experience, from anu + √bhū)		 Certainly, this retrospective awareness of the awakened person would not exist in the absence of the experience of a cognition [during sleep]. Это — ядро аргумента. Логика проста: если бы во сне не было никакого ментального содержания (pratyaya), то при пробуждении не было бы и ретроспективного осознания (pratyavamarśa). Но мы имеем такое осознание ("я хорошо спал" и т.д.) — значит, во сне было содержание. Форма asati pratyayānubhave — это locative absolute: "при отсутствии опыта познания." Certainly, this retrospective awareness of the awakened person would not exist in the absence of the experience of a cognition [during sleep].
[6] tadāśritāḥ smṛtayaś ca tadviṣayā na syuḥ. tad-āśritāḥ = depending on that, resting on that (m. nom. pl.; tad = that; āśrita = sheltered, dependent, PPP ā + √śri)
smṛtayaḥ = memories (f. nom. pl.)
ca = and (indecl.)
tad-viṣayāḥ = having that as their object (m. nom. pl.; tad = that; viṣaya = object, domain)
na = not (indecl.)
syuḥ = would exist (3rd pl. opt. √as)		 And the memories that depend on that [experience] and have that as their object would not exist [either]. Второй уровень аргумента: помимо непосредственного pratyavamarśa при пробуждении, есть ещё и смрити (воспоминания) о качестве сна, которые сохраняются позже. Эти воспоминания āśrita (зависят от) и viṣaya (направлены на) тот опыт, который был во сне. Нет опыта — нет воспоминаний. Но воспоминания есть — значит, опыт был. And the memories that depend on that [experience] and have that as their object would not exist [either].
[7] tasmāt pratyayaviśeṣo nidrā. tasmāt = therefore (indecl., abl. sg. of tad — used as logical connective)
pratyaya-viśeṣaḥ = a specific cognition (m. nom. sg.)
nidrā = sleep (f. nom. sg.)		 Therefore sleep is a specific cognition. Заключение аргумента: tasmāt подводит итог. Нидра — не "ничто," не простое отсутствие сознания. Это pratyayaviśeṣa — специфический вид познания, чьим содержанием является именно abhāva (отсутствие), как сказано в сутре. Therefore sleep is a specific cognition.
[8] sā ca samādhāv itarapratyayavan niroddhavyeti. sā = that [sleep] (f. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
samādhau = in samādhi (m. loc. sg.)
itara-pratyaya-vat = like other cognitions, just as [one suppresses] the remaining cognitions (indecl.; itara = other, remaining; pratyaya = cognition; -vat = like, in the manner of)
niroddhavyā = must be suppressed, is to be restrained (f. nom. sg., FPP ni + √rudh — gerundive: "that which ought to be blocked")
iti = thus (indecl.)		 And that [sleep], like other cognitions, must be suppressed in samādhi. Практический вывод, завершающий комментарий к 1.10. Нидра — это вритти, и, как всякая вритти, она подлежит ниродхе (niroddhavyā — герундив от ni + √rudh, "должна быть подавлена"). В самадхи не остаётся ни восприятия, ни заблуждения, ни языковой конструкции, ни воспоминания — и ни сна тоже. Суффикс -vat подчёркивает: сон должен быть устранён точно так же, как и все остальные виды вритти. Никакого особого статуса у нидры нет. And that [sleep], like other cognitions, must be suppressed in samādhi.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.11
anubhūtaviṣayāsaṃpramoṣaḥ smṛtiḥ || YS_1.11 ||
De-sandhi:
anubhūta-viṣaya-asaṃpramoṣaḥ smṛtiḥ ||		 anubhūta-viṣaya-asaṃpramoṣaḥ = non-theft of an experienced object (m. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: anubhūta = experienced, PPP anu + √bhū; viṣaya = object, domain; a-saṃ-pramoṣa = non-stealing-away, non-loss, from a + sam + pra + √muṣ — the same √muṣ "to steal" that appeared in 1.10's muṣitam)
smṛtiḥ = memory (f. nom. sg., from √smṛ — "to remember")		 Memory (smṛtiḥ) is the non-theft (asaṃpramoṣaḥ) of an experienced object (anubhūta-viṣaya). Meaning: Memory is the fifth and final vṛtti. Its definition is strikingly via negativa: memory is not described as "re-cognition" or "recall" but as asaṃpramoṣa — "non-theft," the mind's holding-on so that the experienced content is not stolen away. The root √muṣ ("to steal") connects this sūtra to 1.10, where tamasic sleep leaves the mind muṣitam iva ("as if robbed"). Sleep robs; memory refuses to be robbed.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. The entire compound anubhūtaviṣayāsaṃpramoṣaḥ is a single predicate descriptor defining smṛtiḥ. The long compound is a tatpuruṣa: "the non-theft (asaṃpramoṣa) of experienced (anubhūta) objects (viṣaya)."

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Memory is the retention of an experienced object — the mind's refusal to let the object slip away.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.11
[1] kiṃ pratyayasya cittaṃ smaraty āhosvid viṣayasyeti. kim = whether? (interrog. particle)
pratyayasya = of the cognition, of the mental content (m. gen. sg.)
cittam = mind (n. nom. sg.)
smarati = remembers (3rd sg. pres. √smṛ)
āhosvit = or rather? or perhaps? (indecl., disjunctive particle — a classic interrogative particle of Sanskrit philosophical prose, composed of āho + svit)
viṣayasya = of the object (m. gen. sg.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Does the mind remember the cognition or [does it remember] the object? Вьяса начинает с фундаментального вопроса эпистемологии: что именно помнит ум — pratyaya (познавательный акт) или viṣaya (внешний объект)? Это вопрос о содержании памяти: воспроизводит ли смрити субъективный опыт познания или объективную вещь? Частица āhosvit — изящный маркер классического философского диалога. Does the mind remember the cognition or [does it remember] the object?
[2] grāhyoparaktaḥ pratyayo grāhyagrahaṇobhayākāranirbhāsas tajjātīyakaṃ saṃskāram ārabhate. grāhya-uparaktaḥ = coloured by the graspable, tinged by the object (m. nom. sg.; grāhya = to-be-grasped, FPP √grah — the object; uparakta = coloured, tinged, PPP upa + √rañj)
pratyayaḥ = cognition (m. nom. sg.)
grāhya-grahaṇa-ubhaya-ākāra-nirbhāsaḥ = shining forth in the dual form of both graspable and grasping (m. nom. sg., compound: grāhya = the object; grahaṇa = the act of grasping, the cognising faculty; ubhaya = both; ākāra = form, aspect; nirbhāsa = shining forth, manifesting, from nir + √bhās)
tad-jātīyakam = of that same kind, homogeneous with that (n. acc. sg.; tad = that; jātīyaka = of the type, belonging to the class, from jāti + suffix -īyaka)
saṃskāram = latent impression, subliminal trace (m. acc. sg.; sam + √kṛ — "that which is thoroughly made/prepared")
ārabhate = initiates, begins, produces (3rd sg. pres. ā + √rabh)		 The cognition, coloured by the graspable [object], shining forth in the dual form of both the graspable and the grasping, initiates a latent impression of the same kind. Ответ Вьясы — и то, и другое. Каждый pratyaya двуформен (ubhayākāra): он содержит в себе и форму объекта (grāhya — "то, что схватывается"), и форму самого процесса познания (grahaṇa — "схватывание"). Познание окрашено объектом (uparakta — от upa + √rañj, "подкрашено"). И этот двойной оттиск порождает tajjātīyaka saṃskāra — латентный след того же типа, содержащий обе формы.

Это важнейшая эпистемологическая модель: познание всегда несёт в себе и объектную, и субъектную сторону. И самскара сохраняет обе.		 The cognition, coloured by the graspable [object], shining forth in the dual form of both the graspable and the grasping, initiates a latent impression of the same kind.
[3] sa saṃskāraḥ svavyañjakāñjanas tadākārām eva grāhyagrahaṇobhayātmikāṃ smṛtiṃ janayati. saḥ = that (m. nom. sg.)
saṃskāraḥ = latent impression (m. nom. sg.)
sva-vyañjaka-añjanaḥ = anointed with its own manifester (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: sva = own; vyañjaka = manifester, that which reveals, from vi + √añj; añjana = ointment, anointing — a beautiful metaphor: the saṃskāra is "smeared" with its own capacity to manifest)
tad-ākārām = having precisely that form (f. acc. sg.; tad = that; ākāra = form)
eva = precisely, just (indecl., emphatic)
grāhya-grahaṇa-ubhaya-ātmikām = consisting of both the graspable and the grasping (f. acc. sg.; ātmikā = consisting of, having the nature of)
smṛtim = memory (f. acc. sg.)
janayati = generates, produces (3rd sg. pres. caus. √jan)		 That latent impression, anointed with its own manifesting [agent], generates memory that has precisely that form — consisting of both the graspable and the grasping. Цепочка: pratyaya → saṃskāra → smṛti. Самскара "помазана" (añjana — буквально "мазь") своим собственным проявляющим агентом (svavyañjaka). Когда условия созревают, самскара порождает (janayati, каузатив от √jan) смрити — воспоминание, которое воспроизводит ту же самую двойную форму (grāhya + grahaṇa). Память, таким образом, — это не бледная копия, а точное воспроизведение двойной структуры исходного познания. That latent impression, anointed with its own manifesting [agent], generates memory that has precisely that form — consisting of both the graspable and the grasping.
[4] tatra grahaṇākārapūrvā buddhiḥ. grāhyākārapūrvā smṛtiḥ. tatra = in this regard, here (indecl.)
grahaṇa-ākāra-pūrvā = having the form of grasping as primary (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: grahaṇa = grasping, the cognitive act; ākāra = form; pūrva = primary, preceding)
buddhiḥ = cognition, intellect (f. nom. sg.)
grāhya-ākāra-pūrvā = having the form of the graspable as primary (f. nom. sg.; grāhya = object)
smṛtiḥ = memory (f. nom. sg.)		 In this regard, cognition has the form of grasping as primary. Memory has the form of the graspable as primary. Тончайшее различие. И буддхи (прямое познание), и смрити (память) содержат оба аспекта — объектный и субъектный. Но акцент различается: в прямом познании на первом плане (pūrva) стоит grahaṇa — сам акт схватывания ("я вижу"); в памяти на первом плане — grāhya — объект ("то, что я видел"). При прямом восприятии мы "в процессе"; при воспоминании мы "перед картиной." In this regard, cognition has the form of grasping as primary. Memory has the form of the graspable as primary.
[5] sā ca dvayī — bhāvitasmartavyā cābhāvitasmartavyā ca. sā = that [memory] (f. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
dvayī = twofold (f. nom. sg.)
bhāvita-smartavyā = [memory] in which the object to be remembered has been cultivated (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: bhāvita = cultivated, produced, practised, PPP caus. √bhū; smartavya = that which is to be remembered, FPP √smṛ)
ca = and (indecl.)
abhāvita-smartavyā = [memory] in which the object to be remembered has not been cultivated (f. nom. sg.; a-bhāvita = uncultivated)
ca = and (indecl.)		 And that [memory] is twofold — [one] in which the object of remembrance has been cultivated, and [one] in which it has not been cultivated. Двоичная классификация памяти. Bhāvitasmartavyā: объект воспоминания был "обработан" (bhāvita — каузатив PPP от √bhū, "приведён в бытие," "культивирован") — то есть сознательно осмыслен, прожит, "переварен." Abhāvitasmartavyā: объект не был сознательно обработан — он всплывает "сырым." And that [memory] is twofold — [one] in which the object of remembrance has been cultivated, and [one] in which it has not been cultivated.
[6] svapne bhāvitasmartavyā. jāgratsamaye tv abhāvitasmartavyeti. svapne = in dream (m. loc. sg., √svap)
bhāvita-smartavyā = [memory] with cultivated object of remembrance (f. nom. sg.)
jāgrat-samaye = at the time of waking, in the waking state (m. loc. sg.; jāgrat = waking, PPA √jāgṛ; samaya = time, occasion)
tu = however, but (indecl.)
abhāvita-smartavyā = [memory] with uncultivated object of remembrance (f. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 In dream [there is memory] in which the object of remembrance has been cultivated. In the waking state, however, [there is memory] in which it has not been cultivated. Парадоксальное распределение. Во сне (svapna) память — bhāvitasmartavyā: содержание сновидения целиком "произведено" умом, сконструировано из самскар, полностью "переработано" — это чистый продукт внутренней обработки. При бодрствовании (jāgrat) память — abhāvitasmartavyā: объекты всплывают непроизвольно, без сознательной "культивации," — мы просто вспоминаем что-то без намеренного воспроизведения. In dream [there is memory] in which the object of remembrance has been cultivated. In the waking state, however, [there is memory] in which it has not been cultivated.
[7] sarvāḥ smṛtayaḥ pramāṇaviparyayavikalpanidrāsmṛtīnām anubhavāt prabhavanti. sarvāḥ = all (f. nom. pl.)
smṛtayaḥ = memories (f. nom. pl.)
pramāṇa-viparyaya-vikalpa-nidrā-smṛtīnām = of valid cognition, error, verbal construction, sleep, and memory (f. gen. pl., dvandva cpd. — listing all five vṛttis)
anubhavāt = from experience (m. abl. sg.; anu + √bhū)
prabhavanti = arise, originate (3rd pl. pres. pra + √bhū)		 All memories arise from the experience of the [five kinds]: valid cognition, error, verbal construction, sleep, and memory [itself]. Замыкание круга: смрити может возникнуть из любой из пяти вритти — включая саму смрити (память о памяти). Это делает систему рекурсивной: можно помнить восприятие, помнить заблуждение, помнить слова, помнить сон — и помнить воспоминание. Все пять вритти перечислены в точном порядке сутр 1.6 (pramāṇa, viparyaya, vikalpa, nidrā, smṛti). All memories arise from the experience of the [five kinds]: valid cognition, error, verbal construction, sleep, and memory [itself].
[8] sarvāś caitā vṛttayaḥ sukhaduḥkhamohātmikāḥ. sarvāḥ = all (f. nom. pl.)
ca = and (indecl.)
etāḥ = these (f. nom. pl.)
vṛttayaḥ = fluctuations (f. nom. pl.)
sukha-duḥkha-moha-ātmikāḥ = having the nature of pleasure, pain, and delusion (f. nom. pl., bahuvrīhi cpd.: sukha = pleasure; duḥkha = pain; moha = delusion; ātmikā = having the nature of)		 And all these fluctuations have the nature of pleasure, pain, and delusion. Начинается грандиозное заключение ко всему блоку 1.5–1.11. Все пять вритти — какими бы они ни были — окрашены тремя качествами: sukha (удовольствие = саттва), duḥkha (страдание = раджас), moha (заблуждение = тамас). Это прямая связь с тремя гунами. And all these fluctuations have the nature of pleasure, pain, and delusion.
[9] sukhaduḥkhamohāś ca kleśeṣu vyākhyeyāḥ. sukha-duḥkha-mohāḥ = pleasure, pain, and delusion (m. nom. pl., dvandva cpd.)
ca = and (indecl.)
kleśeṣu = in [the section on] the afflictions (m. loc. pl.; kleśa = affliction, from √kliś)
vyākhyeyāḥ = are to be explained, will be explained (m. nom. pl., FPP vi + ā + √khyā — "that which is to be expounded")		 And pleasure, pain, and delusion are to be explained in [the section on] the afflictions. Перекрёстная ссылка на Sādhana-pāda (YS 2.3 и далее), где будет дано полное объяснение пяти клеш. Форма vyākhyeyāḥ — это герундив (FPP) от vi + ā + √khyā, "то, что подлежит разъяснению." Вьяса обещает вернуться к теме. And pleasure, pain, and delusion are to be explained in [the section on] the afflictions.
[10] sukhānuśayī rāgaḥ. duḥkhānuśayī dveṣaḥ. mohaḥ punar avidyeti. sukha-anuśayī = clinging to pleasure, having pleasure as its latent tendency (m. nom. sg.; sukha = pleasure; anuśayin = lying alongside, clinging to, from anu + √śī — "sleeping along with," a subliminal tendency that persists)
rāgaḥ = attachment, passion, desire (m. nom. sg., from √rañj — "to be coloured")
duḥkha-anuśayī = clinging to pain (m. nom. sg.)
dveṣaḥ = aversion, hatred (m. nom. sg., from √dviṣ — "to hate")
mohaḥ = delusion (m. nom. sg., from √muh)
punaḥ = in turn, furthermore, again (indecl.)
avidyā = ignorance (f. nom. sg.; a + vidyā, from a + √vid)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Attachment is that which clings to pleasure. Aversion is that which clings to pain. And delusion, in turn, is ignorance. Краткая формула трёх корневых клеш. Rāga — не просто "желание," а anuśayin — "то, что лежит рядом" (anu + √śī), подспудная склонность, которая "спит" в самскарах и пробуждается при контакте с удовольствием. Dveṣa — то же самое, но в отношении боли. Moha = avidyā — фундаментальное незнание, корень всех клеш.

Три гуны — три переживания — три клеши: саттва → сукха → рāга; раджас → дуḥкха → двеша; тамас → моха → авидья.		 Attachment is that which clings to pleasure. Aversion is that which clings to pain. And delusion, in turn, is ignorance.
[11] etāḥ sarvā vṛttayo niroddhavyāḥ. etāḥ = these (f. nom. pl.)
sarvāḥ = all (f. nom. pl.)
vṛttayaḥ = fluctuations (f. nom. pl.)
niroddhavyāḥ = must be suppressed (f. nom. pl., FPP ni + √rudh)		 All these fluctuations must be suppressed. Повторение того же герундива niroddhavyā, что и в конце комментария к 1.10. Все вритти — сукховые, дуḥкховые, моховые — все без исключения подлежат ниродхе. Это окончательное подтверждение определения йоги из 1.2: yogaś cittavṛttinirodhaḥ. All these fluctuations must be suppressed.
[12] āsāṃ nirodhe saṃprajñāto vā samādhir bhavaty asaṃprajñāto veti. āsām = of these (f. gen. pl.)
nirodhe = in the suppression (m. loc. sg.; ni + √rudh)
saṃprajñātaḥ = saṃprajñāta, "with-knowledge" (m. nom. sg.; sam + pra + √jñā — "fully known, distinctly cognised")
vā = or (indecl.)
samādhiḥ = samādhi, meditative absorption (m. nom. sg.)
bhavati = arises, exists (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)
asaṃprajñātaḥ = asaṃprajñāta, "without-knowledge" (m. nom. sg.; a + sam + pra + √jñā)
vā = or (indecl.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 In the suppression of these [fluctuations], there arises either saṃprajñāta samādhi or asaṃprajñāta [samādhi]. Величественное завершение всего блока 1.5–1.11. Вьяса замыкает кольцо: в комментарии к 1.1 он впервые упомянул два вида самадхи, в 1.2 определил йогу как ниродху, в 1.5–1.11 описал все пять вритти — и теперь возвращается к исходной точке. Подавление (nirodha) вритти даёт либо сампраджнята-самадхи (с объектом, с различающим знанием), либо асампраджнята-самадхи (без объекта, за пределами различения). Первый блок Самадхи-пады завершён: мы знаем, что такое йога, что такое вритти, и куда ведёт их ниродха.

## Архитектура: от пяти вритти к двум самадхи

### Пять вритти — итоговая таблица (YS 1.5–1.11)

| № | Vṛtti | Сутра | Определение | Объект | |---|-------|-------|-------------|--------| | 1 | Pramāṇa | 1.7 | Верное познание (pratyakṣa, anumāna, āgama) | Реальный объект, постигнутый верно | | 2 | Viparyaya | 1.8 | Ложное знание (mithyājñāna) | Реальный объект, постигнутый ложно | | 3 | Vikalpa | 1.9 | Вербальная конструкция (śabdajñānānupātī) | Объект отсутствует (vastuśūnya) | | 4 | Nidrā | 1.10 | Познание отсутствия (abhāvapratyaya) | Отсутствие как таковое | | 5 | Smṛti | 1.11 | Неутрата пережитого (asaṃpramoṣa) | Прошлый объект любой из 5 вритти |

### Тройная окраска всех вритти

| Гуна | Переживание | Клеша | Пример (сон) | |------|-------------|-------|--------------| | Sattva | Sukha | Rāga | "Хорошо спал, ум ясен" | | Rajas | Duḥkha | Dveṣa | "Плохо спал, ум блуждает" | | Tamas | Moha | Avidyā | "Тупо спал, тело тяжёлое" |

### Кольцевая структура блока 1.1–1.11

``` YS 1.1 — atha: начало учения YS 1.2 — определение: yogaś cittavṛttinirodhaḥ YS 1.3 — результат ниродхи: draṣṭuḥ svarūpe YS 1.4 — иначе: sārūpyam itaratra YS 1.5 — vṛttayaḥ pañcatayyaḥ (пять вритти) YS 1.6 — kliṣṭākliṣṭāḥ (мучительные/немучительные) YS 1.7 — pramāṇa ─────┐ YS 1.8 — viparyaya ────┤ YS 1.9 — vikalpa ──────┤ 5 vṛttayaḥ YS 1.10 — nidrā ────────┤ YS 1.11 — smṛti ────────┘ │ └──→ nirodha ──→ saṃprajñāta / asaṃprajñāta samādhi (возврат к 1.1–1.2) ```

### Эпистемология памяти (1.11)

``` pratyaya (познание) │ ├── grāhya-ākāra (форма объекта) │ ↓ [вторичен в buddhiḥ, первичен в smṛtiḥ] │ └── grahaṇa-ākāra (форма познания) ↓ [первичен в buddhiḥ, вторичен в smṛtiḥ]

pratyaya ──→ saṃskāra ──→ smṛti (двойной) (тот же тип) (двойная: grāhya + grahaṇa) ```

### Практическое значение для йогина

Блок 1.5–1.11 говорит йогину: весь твой ментальный мир — это пять типов вритти, и все они окрашены тремя гунами (сукха, дуḥкха, моха). Даже сон — это вритти, даже память — это вритти. Нет ни одного состояния ума, которое было бы "нейтральным" или "безопасным" — каждое оставляет самскару, каждое поддерживает колесо вритти-самскара-чакры.

Но есть и хорошая новость: все эти вритти поддаются ниродхе. И результат ниродхи — не пустота, а самадхи: либо сампраджнята (с различающим знанием), либо асампраджнята (за пределами различения). С этого места Патанджали перейдёт к описанию средств достижения ниродхи (abhyāsa и vairāgya, YS 1.12–1.16).		 In the suppression of these [fluctuations], there arises either saṃprajñāta samādhi or asaṃprajñāta [samādhi].
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.12
athāsāṃ nirodhe ka upāya iti —
De-sandhi:
atha āsām nirodhe kaḥ upāyaḥ iti —		 atha = now, next (indecl. — transitional particle, marking a new topic)
āsām = of these (f. gen. pl., referring to the five vṛttis of 1.5–1.11)
nirodhe = in the suppression, for the suppression (m. loc. sg.; ni + √rudh)
kaḥ = what? (m. nom. sg., interrog. pron.)
upāyaḥ = means, method, expedient (m. nom. sg.; upa + √i — "approach, way")
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Now, what is the means for the suppression of these [fluctuations]? Переходная фраза Вьясы, открывающая второй блок Самадхи-пады. Блок 1.5–1.11 описал что такое вритти; теперь вопрос — как их остановить. Слово upāya ("средство, подход") задаёт практический тон всему, что последует. Now, what is the means for the suppression of these [fluctuations]?
Sūtra YS 1.12
abhyāsavairāgyābhyāṃ tannirodhaḥ || YS_1.12 ||
De-sandhi:
abhyāsa-vairāgyābhyām tat-nirodhaḥ ||		 abhyāsa-vairāgyābhyām = by practice and dispassion (n. inst. du., dvandva cpd.: abhyāsa = practice, repeated application, from abhi + √as — "throwing oneself at [something] repeatedly"; vairāgya = dispassion, freedom from attachment, from vi + rāga + -ya — "the state of being free from colouring")
tat-nirodhaḥ = the suppression of that (m. nom. sg.; tat = that, referring to cittavṛtti; nirodha = suppression, from ni + √rudh)		 The suppression of that (tat-nirodhaḥ) [is achieved] by practice (abhyāsa) and dispassion (vairāgya) [taken together] (-ābhyām, inst. du.). Meaning: The answer to Vyāsa's question is elegantly dual: two instruments, one result. Abhyāsa is the positive effort — the active cultivation of stillness. Vairāgya is the negative clearing — the withdrawal from objects. Together, they form the complete method. The instrumental dual (-ābhyām) emphasises that both are needed simultaneously, like two wings of a bird.

Syntax: A nominal sentence with instrumental of means. The dual number (-ābhyām) is syntactically significant: it marks abhyāsa and vairāgya as a pair operating jointly, not as alternatives.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 The suppression of those fluctuations is achieved by practice and dispassion together.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.12
[1] cittanadī nāmobhayatovāhinī vahati kalyāṇāya vahati pāpāya ca. citta-nadī = river of the mind (f. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: citta = mind; nadī = river, from √nad — "to roar, to flow")
nāma = indeed, namely (indecl., emphatic particle — here softening the metaphor: "so to speak")
ubhayataḥ-vāhinī = flowing in both directions (f. nom. sg.; ubhayataḥ = from both sides, in both directions; vāhinī = flowing, carrying, from √vah — "to carry, to flow")
vahati = flows, carries (3rd sg. pres. √vah)
kalyāṇāya = towards the good, for well-being (n. dat. sg.; kalyāṇa = auspicious, good, beautiful)
vahati = flows (3rd sg. pres. √vah — repeated for rhetorical symmetry)
pāpāya = towards evil, for harm (n. dat. sg.; pāpa = evil, sin)
ca = and (indecl.)		 The river of the mind, flowing in both directions, flows towards the good and flows towards evil. Одна из самых знаменитых метафор Вьясы — cittanadī, "река ума." Ум подобен реке, которая может течь в двух направлениях: к благу (kalyāṇa) или к злу (pāpa). Важно: это одна река с двумя возможными руслами, а не две реки. Частица nāma смягчает метафору: "ум — это, так сказать, река." Двойной vahati создаёт ритмический параллелизм. The river of the mind, flowing in both directions, flows towards the good and flows towards evil.
[2] yā tu kaivalyaprāgbhārā vivekaviṣayanimnā sā kalyāṇavahā. yā = which (f. nom. sg., rel. pron.)
tu = but, indeed (indecl.)
kaivalya-prāgbhārā = leaning forward towards liberation (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: kaivalya = absolute freedom, isolation of puruṣa; prāgbhāra = leaning forward, inclined towards, from prāk + bhāra — "having its weight/load forward")
viveka-viṣaya-nimnā = sloping downhill towards the object of discrimination (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: viveka = discrimination, discernment, from vi + √vic; viṣaya = object, domain; nimnā = sloping, inclined downward — the river naturally gravitates downhill)
sā = that [current] (f. nom. sg.)
kalyāṇa-vahā = carrying the good, flowing towards well-being (f. nom. sg.; kalyāṇa = good; vahā = carrying, from √vah)		 That [current] which leans forward towards liberation, which slopes downhill towards the object of discrimination — that carries the good. Первое русло: kaivalyaprāgbhārā — река "наклоняется вперёд" (prāgbhāra, от prāk + bhāra, "тяжесть вперёд") в сторону кайвальи (абсолютного освобождения). Её русло "склоняется вниз" (nimnā) к объекту различения (vivekaviṣaya) — то есть вода сама течёт туда, по наклону, без усилий. Когда ум настроен на различение, движение к освобождению становится естественным, как течение реки под уклон. That [current] which leans forward towards liberation, which slopes downhill towards the object of discrimination — that carries the good.
[3] saṃsāraprāgbhārāvivekaviṣayaniṃnā pāpavahā. saṃsāra-prāgbhārā = leaning forward towards the cycle of rebirth (f. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: saṃsāra = the cycle, from sam + √sṛ; prāgbhāra = leaning forward)
a-viveka-viṣaya-nimnā = sloping towards the object of non-discrimination (f. nom. sg.; a-viveka = non-discrimination, absence of discernment)
pāpa-vahā = carrying evil (f. nom. sg.)		 [That current which] leans forward towards saṃsāra, which slopes downhill towards the object of non-discrimination — that carries evil. Зеркальная структура: второе русло той же реки. Здесь наклон (prāgbhāra) — в сторону сансары, а склон (nimnā) ведёт к a-viveka (отсутствию различения). Обратите внимание на точную симметрию: kaivalya ↔ saṃsāra; viveka ↔ aviveka; kalyāṇavahā ↔ pāpavahā. Одна река, два русла, два направления. [That current which] leans forward towards saṃsāra, which slopes downhill towards the object of non-discrimination — that carries evil.
[4] tatra vairāgyeṇa viṣayasrotaḥ khilīkriyate. tatra = of those [two], in this regard (indecl.)
vairāgyeṇa = by dispassion, by non-attachment (n. inst. sg.)
viṣaya-srotaḥ = the stream [flowing] towards objects (n. nom. sg.; viṣaya = object; srotas = stream, current, from √sru — "to flow")
khilī-kriyate = is made fallow, is blocked up, is dammed (3rd sg. pres. pass.; khilī + √kṛ — a denominative: khila = fallow land, waste ground; khilīkṛ = "to make into wasteland," i.e. to block, to render barren)		 Of those [two currents], by dispassion the stream [flowing] towards objects is dammed up. Функция вайрагьи: перекрыть русло, текущее к объектам. Глагол khilīkriyate замечателен: khila — это "целина, заброшенное поле." Сделать русло khilī — значит превратить его в пересохшее, заброшенное русло, по которому вода уже не течёт. Вайрагья не уничтожает реку — она перекрывает одно из двух русел. Of those [two currents], by dispassion the stream [flowing] towards objects is dammed up.
[5] vivekadarśanābhyāsena vivekasrota udghāṭyata ity ubhayādhīnaś cittavṛttinirodhaḥ. viveka-darśana-abhyāsena = by the practice of the vision of discrimination (m. inst. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: viveka = discrimination; darśana = vision, seeing; abhyāsa = practice)
viveka-srotaḥ = the stream of discrimination (n. nom. sg.)
udghāṭyate = is opened up, is unblocked (3rd sg. pres. pass. ud + √ghaṭ — "to open, to unlock")
iti = thus (indecl.)
ubhaya-adhīnaḥ = dependent on both (m. nom. sg.; ubhaya = both; adhīna = dependent, from adhi + √i)
citta-vṛtti-nirodhaḥ = suppression of mental fluctuations (m. nom. sg.)		 By the practice of the vision of discrimination, the stream of discrimination is opened up. Thus the suppression of mental fluctuations depends on both. Функция абхьясы: открыть русло различения. Глагол udghāṭyate (ud + √ghaṭ, "отпирать, распахивать") — точная противоположность khilīkriyate. Одно русло перекрывается, другое распахивается. И заключение: ниродха ubhayādhīna — "зависит от обоих." Нельзя только перекрыть поток к объектам (без практики различение не откроется); нельзя только практиковать различение (без вайрагьи объекты продолжат тянуть). Нужны оба — одновременно. By the practice of the vision of discrimination, the stream of discrimination is opened up. Thus the suppression of mental fluctuations depends on both.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.13
tatra sthitau yatno 'bhyāsaḥ || YS_1.13 ||
De-sandhi:
tatra sthitau yatnaḥ abhyāsaḥ ||		 tatra = of those two, in that [pair of abhyāsa and vairāgya] (indecl.)
sthitau = towards stability, in steadiness (f. loc. sg.; sthiti = steadiness, standing firm, from √sthā — the locative here expresses direction/purpose: "effort directed at stability")
yatnaḥ = effort, exertion (m. nom. sg., from √yat — "to strive")
abhyāsaḥ = practice (m. nom. sg.; abhi + √as — "throwing at [repeatedly]")		 Of those two, practice (abhyāsaḥ) is the effort (yatnaḥ) towards stability (sthitau). Meaning: Abhyāsa is defined with striking simplicity: effort (yatna) aimed at steadiness (sthiti). The sthiti here refers back to the praśāntavāhitā (peaceful flow) that Vyāsa will describe — the mind's capacity to rest in a state of non-fluctuation. Practice is not any random effort but effort specifically oriented towards making the mind stable.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Tatra refers back to the pair (abhyāsa + vairāgya) from 1.12; sthitau is a locative of direction/purpose.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Of those two, practice is the effort directed towards steadiness.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.13
[1] cittasyāvṛttikasya praśāntavāhitā sthitiḥ. cittasya = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
