a-vṛttikasya = that is without fluctuations, free from vṛttis (n. gen. sg.; a = without; vṛttika = having fluctuations, from vṛtti + -ka)
praśānta-vāhitā = peaceful flow, tranquil streaming (f. nom. sg.; praśānta = calmed, quieted, PPP pra + √śam; vāhitā = flow, streaming, state of flowing, from √vah + -itā — abstract noun: "the state of flowing peacefully")
sthitiḥ = steadiness, stability (f. nom. sg.)		 Steadiness is the peaceful flow of the mind that is free from fluctuations. Вьяса раскрывает, что именно означает sthiti из сутры. Это не окаменелость, не мёртвая неподвижность — это praśāntavāhitā, "мирное течение." Ум течёт (vāhitā, от √vah), но течёт спокойно (praśānta), без вритти. Это тот же образ реки: стабильность — это не остановка воды, а её ровное, невозмущённое течение. Steadiness is the peaceful flow of the mind that is free from fluctuations.
[2] tadarthaḥ prayatno vīryam utsāhaḥ. tad-arthaḥ = having that as its aim, directed at that (m. nom. sg.; tad = that [sthiti]; artha = aim, purpose)
pra-yatnaḥ = effort, exertion (m. nom. sg.; pra + √yat — intensified form of yatna)
vīryam = energy, vigour, heroic strength (n. nom. sg., from √vīr)
utsāhaḥ = enthusiasm, zeal, perseverance (m. nom. sg.; ud + √sah — "bearing up," the capacity to endure and push forward)		 The effort directed at that [steadiness] is energy, enthusiasm. Тройной синоним: prayatna (усилие), vīrya (мощь, героическая энергия), utsāha (воодушевление, рвение). Это не три разные вещи — это три грани одного: практика требует не только дисциплины (prayatna), но и силы (vīrya) и внутреннего огня (utsāha). Корень ud + √sah в utsāha буквально означает "нести вверх" — способность выдержать и продолжить. The effort directed at that [steadiness] is energy, enthusiasm.
[3] tatsaṃpipādayiṣayā tatsādhanānuṣṭhānam abhyāsaḥ. tat-saṃpipādayiṣayā = by the desire to accomplish that (f. inst. sg.; tat = that; saṃpipādayiṣā = desire to bring about, desiderative abstract noun from sam + pra + √pad in causative desiderative: sam-pi-pāday-iṣā — "the wish to make [that] succeed")
tat-sādhana-anuṣṭhānam = the performance of the means for that (n. nom. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: tat = that; sādhana = means, instrument, from √sādh; anuṣṭhāna = performance, practice, carrying out, from anu + √sthā)
abhyāsaḥ = practice (m. nom. sg.)		 Practice is the performance of the means for that [steadiness], driven by the desire to accomplish that. Форма saṃpipādayiṣā заслуживает особого внимания: это абстрактное существительное от каузативного дезидератива корня √pad. Дезидератив выражает желание (pipādayiṣa = "желающий привести к успеху"), а суффикс -ā превращает его в абстракцию ("желание привести к успеху"). Абхьяса, таким образом, — это не механическое повторение, а целенаправленная практика, движимая осознанным стремлением к результату (saṃpipādayiṣā) и воплощённая в реальном выполнении (anuṣṭhāna) конкретных средств (sādhana). Practice is the performance of the means for that [steadiness], driven by the desire to accomplish that.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.14
sa tu dīrghakālanairantaryasatkārāsevito dṛḍhabhūmiḥ || YS_1.14 ||
De-sandhi:
saḥ tu dīrgha-kāla-nairantarya-satkāra-āsevitaḥ dṛḍha-bhūmiḥ ||		 saḥ = that [practice, abhyāsa] (m. nom. sg.)
tu = but, however (indecl. — marking a qualification or condition)
dīrgha-kāla-nairantarya-satkāra-āsevitaḥ = cultivated with long time, without interruption, and with devotion (m. nom. sg., compound: dīrgha = long; kāla = time; nairantarya = uninterruptedness, continuity, from nir + antara + -ya — "without gaps"; satkāra = devotion, respect, earnestness, from sat + √kṛ — "doing with truth/goodness"; āsevita = cultivated, practised, PPP ā + √sev — "served, attended to")
dṛḍha-bhūmiḥ = firmly grounded, having firm ground (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: dṛḍha = firm, solid, PPP √dṛh; bhūmi = ground, stage, level)		 But that [practice] (saḥ), when cultivated (āsevitaḥ) for a long time (dīrgha-kāla), without interruption (nairantarya), and with devotion (satkāra), becomes firmly grounded (dṛḍha-bhūmiḥ). Meaning: The essential qualifier: practice alone is not enough — it must be sustained (dīrghakāla), unbroken (nairantarya), and performed with genuine commitment (satkāra). Only then does it become dṛḍhabhūmi — "firm-grounded," established beyond the reach of regression. The triple condition — time, continuity, devotion — forms one of yoga's most practical teachings.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. The massive compound dīrghakālanairantaryasatkārāsevitaḥ functions as a conditional predicate: "when [it is] cultivated with long-time-uninterruptedness-devotion." Tu adds a note of caution: "but [only when] ..."

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 But that practice becomes firmly grounded when it is cultivated for a long time, without interruption, and with devotion.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.14
[1] dīrghakālāsevito nirantarāsevitaḥ satkārāsevitaḥ. dīrgha-kāla-āsevitaḥ = cultivated for a long time (m. nom. sg.; dīrgha = long; kāla = time; āsevita = cultivated, PPP ā + √sev)
nirantara-āsevitaḥ = cultivated without interruption (m. nom. sg.; nir-antara = without interval, continuous)
satkāra-āsevitaḥ = cultivated with devotion (m. nom. sg.; satkāra = respect, earnest care)		 Cultivated for a long time, cultivated without interruption, cultivated with devotion. Вьяса разбивает длинное сложное слово сутры на три отдельных определения, показывая, что это три самостоятельных условия, а не одно. Каждое повторяет āsevita — "практикуемый, культивируемый" — подчёркивая: все три условия относятся именно к качеству практики. Cultivated for a long time, cultivated without interruption, cultivated with devotion.
[2] tapasā brahmacaryeṇa vidyayā śraddhayā ca saṃpāditaḥ satkāravān dṛḍhabhūmir bhavati. tapasā = through austerity, by ascetic practice (n. inst. sg.; tapas, from √tap — "to burn, to heat")
brahmacaryeṇa = through celibacy, by the conduct of brahman (n. inst. sg.; brahmacarya = "walking in brahman," chaste and disciplined living)
vidyayā = through knowledge, by learning (f. inst. sg.; vidyā, from √vid)
śraddhayā = through faith, by trust (f. inst. sg.; śraddhā, from śrat + √dhā — "placing the heart")
ca = and (indecl.)
saṃpāditaḥ = accomplished, brought about (m. nom. sg., PPP sam + pra + √pad — caus.: "made to succeed")
satkāra-vān = possessing devotion, endowed with earnest care (m. nom. sg.; satkāra + -vat — possessive suffix)
dṛḍha-bhūmiḥ = firmly grounded (m. nom. sg.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)		 Accomplished through austerity, celibacy, knowledge, and faith — that which has devotion becomes firmly grounded. Вьяса раскрывает содержание сат-кары через четыре опоры: tapas (аскеза, жар — от √tap, "гореть"), brahmacarya (целомудрие, дисциплина жизни), vidyā (знание), śraddhā (вера — буквально "то, во что помещают сердце," от śrat + √dhā). Когда практика подкреплена этими четырьмя — она становится satkāravat (обладающей подлинной серьёзностью) и dṛḍhabhūmi (укоренённой). Accomplished through austerity, celibacy, knowledge, and faith — that which has devotion becomes firmly grounded.
[3] vyutthānasaṃskāreṇa drāg ity evānabhibhūtaviṣaya ity arthaḥ. vyutthāna-saṃskāreṇa = by the latent impression of emergence (m. inst. sg.; vyutthāna = emergence, rising up, the return from samādhi to ordinary consciousness, from vi + ud + √sthā; saṃskāra = latent impression)
drāk = quickly, instantly (indecl.)
iti = thus (indecl.)
eva = just, merely (indecl.)
an-abhibhūta-viṣayaḥ = one whose domain is not overwhelmed, not overpowered in its scope (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: an = not; abhibhūta = overwhelmed, overpowered, PPP abhi + √bhū; viṣaya = domain, scope)
iti = thus (indecl.)
arthaḥ = meaning (m. nom. sg.)		 The meaning is: [it becomes] one whose domain is not quickly overwhelmed by the latent impressions of emergence [from samādhi]. Практическое объяснение того, что значит dṛḍhabhūmi в реальности: практика укоренена настолько, что vyutthānasaṃskāra (латентные следы "всплывания" из самадхи обратно в обычное сознание) не могут её быстро (drāk) разрушить. Слово drāk важно: Вьяса не говорит, что вьюттхана-самскары исчезают полностью — он говорит, что они не могут мгновенно подавить практику. Укоренённая практика сопротивляется обратному откату. The meaning is: [it becomes] one whose domain is not quickly overwhelmed by the latent impressions of emergence [from samādhi].
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.15
dṛṣṭānuśravikaviṣayavitṛṣṇasya vaśīkārasaṃjñā vairāgyam || YS_1.15 ||
De-sandhi:
dṛṣṭa-ānuśravika-viṣaya-vitṛṣṇasya vaśīkāra-saṃjñā vairāgyam ||		 dṛṣṭa-ānuśravika-viṣaya-vitṛṣṇasya = of one free from thirst for seen and revealed objects (m. gen. sg., compound: dṛṣṭa = seen, directly perceived, PPP √dṛś; ānuśravika = handed down by hearing, scriptural, relating to what is heard [in the Vedas], from anu + √śru + -ika; viṣaya = object; vi-tṛṣṇa = free from thirst, from vi + tṛṣṇā, from √tṛṣ — "to thirst")
vaśīkāra-saṃjñā = the consciousness of mastery, the awareness that is mastery (f. nom. sg.; vaśīkāra = subjugation, mastery, bringing under control, from vaśa + √kṛ — "making [something] obedient"; saṃjñā = consciousness, awareness, name, from sam + √jñā)
vairāgyam = dispassion (n. nom. sg.)		 Dispassion (vairāgyam) is the consciousness of mastery (vaśīkāra-saṃjñā) of one who is free from thirst (vitṛṣṇasya) for objects seen (dṛṣṭa) and heard-of [in scripture] (ānuśravika). Meaning: Vairāgya is defined with two crucial dimensions. First, it covers both worldly objects (dṛṣṭa — women, food, power) and scriptural promises (ānuśravika — heaven, divine bodies, absorption in prakṛti). Second, it is not mere suppression but vaśīkārasaṃjñā — a positive state of conscious mastery. The mind does not merely restrain itself from objects; it knows that it is free, it recognises its own sovereignty. This self-aware freedom is vairāgya.

Syntax: The genitive vitṛṣṇasya is an abstract genitive describing the person in whom this state arises. Vaśīkārasaṃjñā is the predicate nominative equated with vairāgyam.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Dispassion is the conscious mastery that arises in one who has ceased to thirst for objects both seen and described in scripture.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.15
[1 (a single extended definition)] striyo 'nnapānam aiśvaryam iti dṛṣṭaviṣaye vitṛṣṇasya svargavaidehyaprakṛtilayatvaprāptāv ānuśravikaviṣaye vitṛṣṇasya divyādivyaviṣayasaṃprayoge 'pi cittasya viṣayadoṣadarśinaḥ prasaṃkhyānabalād anābhogātmikā heyopādeyaśūnyā vaśīkārasaṃjñā vairāgyam. striyaḥ = women (f. nom. pl.)
anna-pānam = food and drink (n. nom. sg., dvandva cpd.)
aiśvaryam = power, lordship, sovereignty (n. nom. sg., from īśvara + -ya)
iti = thus, namely (indecl.)
dṛṣṭa-viṣaye = in regard to the seen object, in the domain of the directly perceived (m. loc. sg.)
vitṛṣṇasya = of one free from thirst (m. gen. sg.)
svarga-vaideha-prakṛti-layatva-prāptau = in the attainment of heaven, the bodiless state, and absorption into prakṛti (f. loc. sg., tatpuruṣa cpd.: svarga = heaven; vaideha = bodiless state, existence without a body, from vi + deha; prakṛti-layatva = the state of absorption into prakṛti, from prakṛti + laya + -tva; prāpti = attainment, from pra + √āp)
ānuśravika-viṣaye = in regard to the revealed object (m. loc. sg.)
vitṛṣṇasya = of one free from thirst (m. gen. sg.)
divya-adivya-viṣaya-saṃprayoge = in [the moment of] contact with divine and non-divine objects (m. loc. sg.; divya = divine; adivya = non-divine; saṃprayoga = contact, union, from sam + pra + √yuj)
api = even (indecl.)
cittasya = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
viṣaya-doṣa-darśinaḥ = that sees the fault in objects (m. gen. sg., bahuvrīhi: viṣaya = object; doṣa = fault, defect; darśin = seeing, from √dṛś + -in)
prasaṃkhyāna-balāt = by the strength of discriminative meditation (n. abl. sg.; prasaṃkhyāna = discerning meditation, clear enumeration, from pra + sam + √khyā; bala = strength, force)
an-ābhoga-ātmikā = without inclination, having as its nature the absence of engagement (f. nom. sg.; an = without; ābhoga = inclination, engagement, full extension, from ā + √bhuj; ātmikā = having the nature of)
heya-upādeya-śūnyā = devoid of [the categories] "to be rejected" and "to be acquired" (f. nom. sg.; heya = to-be-rejected, FPP √hā; upādeya = to-be-acquired, FPP upa + ā + √dā; śūnya = empty, devoid of)
vaśīkāra-saṃjñā = the consciousness of mastery (f. nom. sg.)
vairāgyam = dispassion (n. nom. sg.)		 For one who is free from thirst regarding the seen objects — namely women, food-and-drink, and power — and free from thirst regarding the revealed objects — namely the attainment of heaven, of the bodiless state, and of absorption into prakṛti — for one whose mind, even when in contact with objects divine and non-divine, sees the fault in objects — by the strength of discriminative meditation, the consciousness of mastery that is without inclination, devoid of [the categories] "to be rejected" and "to be acquired" — that is dispassion. Это одно огромное предложение — вся бхашья к 1.15 — и оно заслуживает подробного разбора.

Два уровня отречения: 1. Dṛṣṭaviṣaya — "видимые объекты": женщины, еда, власть (strī, annapāna, aiśvarya). Это мирские наслаждения. 2. Ānuśravikaviṣaya — "объекты, известные из писания": небеса (svarga), бестелесное существование (vaideha), растворение в пракрити (prakṛtilaya). Это духовные наслаждения, обещанные Ведами.

Йогин должен быть свободен от жажды (vitṛṣṇa) к обоим уровням.

Механизм вайрагьи: Даже при контакте (saṃprayoga) с объектами — божественными (divya) и небожественными (adivya) — ум видит изъян в объектах (viṣayadoṣadarśin). Это не слепое подавление, а видение (darśin): ум распознаёт дефект, присущий всем объектам.

Источник силы: Prasaṃkhyāna-bala — сила различающей медитации. Prasaṃkhyāna (от pra + sam + √khyā, "ясное перечисление") — это систематическое различение реального от нереального.

Результат: - Anābhogātmikā — без наклонности, без тяги. Ум не тянется ни к чему. - Heyopādeyaśūnyā — пуст от категорий "отвергнуть" и "принять." Йогин не думает: "это надо отбросить, а это взять." Он за пределами самого деления.

Это высшая форма вайрагьи: не "я не хочу этого" (что ещё предполагает отношение к объекту), а полное отсутствие самих категорий желания и отвержения.

## Архитектура: два крыла йоги

### Метафора реки (YS 1.12)

``` cittanadī (река ума) │ ┌───────────┴───────────┐ ▼ ▼ kalyāṇavahā pāpavahā (поток блага) (поток зла) │ │ kaivalya-prāgbhārā saṃsāra-prāgbhārā (наклон к кайвалье) (наклон к сансаре) │ │ viveka-viṣaya-nimnā aviveka-viṣaya-nimnā (склон к различению) (склон к неразличению) │ │ ▼ ▼ ABHYĀSA открывает VAIRĀGYA перекрывает (udghāṭyate) (khilīkriyate) ```

### Три условия укоренения практики (YS 1.14)

| Условие | Санскрит | Значение | Средства (по Вьясе) | |---------|----------|----------|---------------------| | Длительность | dīrgha-kāla | Долгое время | — | | Непрерывность | nairantarya | Без перерывов | — | | Серьёзность | satkāra | С подлинной преданностью | tapas, brahmacarya, vidyā, śraddhā |

### Два уровня вайрагьи (YS 1.15)

| Уровень | Объекты | Примеры | |---------|---------|---------| | Dṛṣṭa (видимое) | Мирские наслаждения | Женщины, еда и питьё, власть | | Ānuśravika (из писаний) | Духовные обещания | Небеса, бестелесность, растворение в пракрити |

### Формула вайрагьи

``` viṣayadoṣadarśin → видеть изъян в объектах + prasaṃkhyānabala → сила различающей медитации = anābhogātmikā → без наклонности + heyopādeyaśūnyā → за пределами "отвергнуть/принять" = vaśīkārasaṃjñā → осознание своей свободы = VAIRĀGYA ```

### Связь блоков: от диагноза к лечению

Блок 1.5–1.11 был диагнозом: пять вритти, все окрашены гунами, все производят клеши. Блок 1.12–1.15 — это первая часть лечения: два инструмента (абхьяса и вайрагья), условия их укоренения, и определение высшей формы отречения. Следующий шаг — YS 1.16: паравайрагья (высшее отречение), выходящее за пределы даже гун.		 For one who is free from thirst regarding the seen objects — namely women, food-and-drink, and power — and free from thirst regarding the revealed objects — namely the attainment of heaven, of the bodiless state, and of absorption into prakṛti — for one whose mind, even when in contact with objects divine and non-divine, sees the fault in objects — by the strength of discriminative meditation, the consciousness of mastery that is without inclination, devoid of [the categories] "to be rejected" and "to be acquired" — that is dispassion.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.16
tat paraṃ puruṣakhyāter guṇavaitṛṣṇyam || YS_1.16 ||
De-sandhi:
tat param puruṣa-khyāteḥ guṇa-vaitṛṣṇyam ||		 tat = that (n. nom. sg., referring to vairāgya from 1.15)
param = supreme, higher, ultimate (n. nom. sg.; para = beyond, supreme)
puruṣa-khyāteḥ = from the discernment of puruṣa, from the vision of the Self (f. abl. sg.; puruṣa = the Self, pure consciousness; khyāti = discernment, vision, clear seeing, from √khyā — "to be known, to shine forth." The ablative expresses the source/cause)
guṇa-vaitṛṣṇyam = freedom from thirst for the guṇas (n. nom. sg.; guṇa = the three constituents of prakṛti: sattva, rajas, tamas; vaitṛṣṇya = state of being free from thirst, from vi + tṛṣṇā + -ya — abstract noun: "the condition of non-thirsting")		 That (tat) supreme [dispassion] (param) is the freedom from thirst for the guṇas (guṇa-vaitṛṣṇyam), [arising] from the discernment of puruṣa (puruṣa-khyāteḥ). Meaning: This sūtra elevates vairāgya to its ultimate form. The lower vairāgya (1.15) was freedom from thirst for specific objects — seen and scriptural. The supreme vairāgya (param) is freedom from the guṇas themselves — the very fabric of prakṛti. Since every object, every experience, every state of mind is a product of the guṇas, freedom from the guṇas means freedom from the entire matrix of manifestation. The cause of this supreme dispassion is not willpower but puruṣakhyāti — the clear vision of what puruṣa truly is.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Tat param = "that [vairāgya], the supreme one"; guṇavaitṛṣṇyam is the predicate; puruṣakhyāteḥ is an ablative of cause/source.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 The supreme form of dispassion is freedom from thirst for the guṇas themselves, born from the discernment of puruṣa.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.16
[1] dṛṣṭānuśravikaviṣayadoṣadarśī viraktaḥ puruṣadarśanābhyāsāt tacchuddhipravivekāpyāyitabuddhir guṇebhyo vyaktāvyaktadharmakebhyo virakta iti. dṛṣṭa-ānuśravika-viṣaya-doṣa-darśī = one who sees the fault in seen and revealed objects (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd. — reprising the language of 1.15: doṣadarśin = seeing the defect)
viraktaḥ = dispassionate, detached (m. nom. sg., PPP vi + √rañj — "de-coloured," freed from the tinge of attachment)
puruṣa-darśana-abhyāsāt = from the practice of the vision of puruṣa (m. abl. sg.; puruṣa = the Self; darśana = vision, seeing; abhyāsa = practice — ablative of cause)
tat-śuddhi-praviveka-āpyāyita-buddhiḥ = one whose intellect is nourished by the discrimination of the purity of that [puruṣa] (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi cpd.: tat = that [puruṣa]; śuddhi = purity; praviveka = keen discrimination, thorough discernment, from pra + vi + √vic; āpyāyita = nourished, fattened, made to swell, PPP caus. ā + √pyai — "caused to fill up"; buddhi = intellect)
guṇebhyaḥ = from/towards the guṇas (m. dat./abl. pl.)
vyakta-avyakta-dharmakebhyaḥ = which have the character of both manifest and unmanifest (m. dat./abl. pl., bahuvrīhi: vyakta = manifest, evolved products; avyakta = unmanifest, prakṛti in its undifferentiated state; dharmaka = having the character of, from dharma + -ka)
viraktaḥ = dispassionate (m. nom. sg. — repeated, now at the higher level)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 The one who is dispassionate, having seen the fault in the objects both seen and revealed — through the practice of the vision of puruṣa, his intellect nourished by the discrimination of the purity of that [puruṣa], becomes dispassionate towards the guṇas, which have the character of both the manifest and the unmanifest. Двухступенчатая структура вайрагьи. Первая ступень (dṛṣṭānuśravikaviṣayadoṣadarśī viraktaḥ) — отречение от конкретных объектов, описанное в 1.15. Вторая ступень — от гун как таковых, включая и vyakta (всё проявленное: 23 таттвы от махата до элементов) и avyakta (непроявленное — мулапракрити). Источник этого отречения — puruṣadarśanābhyāsa (практика видения пуруши), которая āpyāyita (питает, наполняет) буддхи различением чистоты пуруши (tacchuddhipraviveka). Глагол √pyai ("набухать, наполняться") создаёт образ интеллекта, который буквально "набухает" от знания пуруши, как растение, напитанное водой. The one who is dispassionate, having seen the fault in the objects both seen and revealed — through the practice of the vision of puruṣa, his intellect nourished by the discrimination of the purity of that [puruṣa], becomes dispassionate towards the guṇas, which have the character of both the manifest and the unmanifest.
[2] tad dvayaṃ vairāgyam. tat = that (n. nom. sg.)
dvayam = twofold, a pair (n. nom. sg.)
vairāgyam = dispassion (n. nom. sg.)		 That twofold [state] is dispassion. Вьяса подводит итог: вайрагья двойственна (dvaya). Низшая (1.15): vaśīkārasaṃjñā — осознанное владение собой при контакте с объектами. Высшая (1.16): guṇavaitṛṣṇya — свобода от гун как таковых. That twofold [state] is dispassion.
[3] tatra yad uttaraṃ taj jñānaprasādamātram. tatra = of those [two] (indecl.)
yat = which (n. nom. sg., rel. pron.)
uttaram = the higher, the latter, the superior (n. nom. sg.; uttara = upper, later)
tat = that (n. nom. sg.)
jñāna-prasāda-mātram = nothing but the clarity/serenity of knowledge (n. nom. sg.; jñāna = knowledge; prasāda = clarity, serenity, grace, from pra + √sad — "that which has settled clear," like clear water; mātra = mere, nothing but)		 Of those [two], that which is the higher one — that is nothing but the clarity of knowledge. Высшая вайрагья — это jñānaprasādamātra: "лишь ясность знания." Слово prasāda (от pra + √sad) — это не просто "ясность." Его первичное значение — "осаждение," как мутная вода, которая стала прозрачной: все примеси осели, и остался чистый, ясный покой. Суффикс -mātra ("лишь," "только") подчёркивает: высшая вайрагья — это исключительно знание, и ничего больше. Никакого волевого усилия, никакого подавления — просто ясное видение. Of those [two], that which is the higher one — that is nothing but the clarity of knowledge.
[4] yasyodaye sati yogī pratyuditakhyātir evaṃ manyate — prāptaṃ prāpaṇīyaṃ, kṣīṇāḥ kṣetavyāḥ kleśāḥ, chinnaḥ śliṣṭaparvā bhavasaṃkramaḥ, yasyāvicchedāj janitvā mriyate mṛtvā ca jāyata iti. yasya = of which, when that (n. gen. sg., temporal gen.: "of which [knowledge]")
udaye = in the arising, upon the dawn (m. loc. sg.; udaya = rising, from ud + √i)
sati = being, when there is (m. loc. sg., PPA √as — locative absolute with udaye)
yogī = the yogin (m. nom. sg.)
pratyudita-khyātiḥ = one in whom discernment has dawned, whose knowledge has arisen (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi: pratyudita = dawned, arisen, from prati + ud + √i; khyāti = discernment, vision)
evam = thus (indecl.)
manyate = thinks, considers (3rd sg. pres. ā. √man)
prāptam = attained, obtained (n. nom. sg., PPP pra + √āp)
prāpaṇīyam = what was to be attained, the to-be-obtained (n. nom. sg., FPP pra + √āp — gerundive: "that which ought to be reached")
kṣīṇāḥ = destroyed, worn away (m. nom. pl., PPP √kṣi — "to diminish, to perish")
kṣetavyāḥ = that were to be destroyed (m. nom. pl., FPP √kṣi — gerundive: "those which ought to be destroyed")
kleśāḥ = afflictions (m. nom. pl.)
chinnaḥ = cut, severed (m. nom. sg., PPP √chid)
śliṣṭa-parvā = whose links cling together (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi: śliṣṭa = clinging, adhering, PPP √śliṣ; parvan = joint, link, node — the joints of a chain)
bhava-saṃkramaḥ = the passage of existence, the transit through states of being (m. nom. sg.; bhava = existence, becoming; saṃkrama = passage, crossing, from sam + √kram)
yasya = of which (m. gen. sg.)
avicchedāt = from the uninterruptedness, through the unbroken continuity (m. abl. sg.; a + viccheda = non-cutting, unbroken, from vi + √chid + a)
janitvā = having been born (absolutive, √jan)
mriyate = dies (3rd sg. pres. √mṛ)
mṛtvā = having died (absolutive, √mṛ)
ca = and (indecl.)
jāyate = is born (3rd sg. pres. pass. √jan)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 When that [knowledge] has arisen, the yogin, in whom discernment has dawned, thinks thus: "What was to be attained has been attained. The afflictions that were to be destroyed have been destroyed. The passage of existence, whose links cling together, has been cut — [that passage] through whose unbroken [continuity] one, having been born, dies, and having died, is born [again]." Это кульминационный пассаж — "внутренний монолог" йогина, достигшего паравайрагьи. Три утверждения образуют триаду завершённости:

1. Prāptaṃ prāpaṇīyam — "достигнуто то, что подлежало достижению." PPP + FPP от одного корня √āp: свершившееся соединяется с долженствующим.

2. Kṣīṇāḥ kṣetavyāḥ kleśāḥ — "уничтожены клеши, подлежавшие уничтожению." Опять PPP + FPP от одного корня √kṣi.

3. Chinnaḥ śliṣṭaparvā bhavasaṃkramaḥ — "рассечена цепь бытия, чьи звенья слипались." Образ śliṣṭaparvan ("слипшиеся сочленения") изображает сансару как цепь, звенья которой прилипают друг к другу (√śliṣ = "липнуть, цепляться"), так что aviccheda ("нерассечённость") поддерживает бесконечный цикл: родился — умер — родился.

Грамматическая элегантность: janitvā mriyate, mṛtvā ca jāyate — две абсолютивные конструкции с противоположными корнями √jan (рождаться) и √mṛ (умирать), создающие образ безостановочного маятника.		 When that [knowledge] has arisen, the yogin, in whom discernment has dawned, thinks thus: "What was to be attained has been attained. The afflictions that were to be destroyed have been destroyed. The passage of existence, whose links cling together, has been cut — [that passage] through whose unbroken [continuity] one, having been born, dies, and having died, is born [again]."
[5] jñānasyaiva parā kāṣṭhā vairāgyam. jñānasya = of knowledge (n. gen. sg.)
eva = itself, indeed (indecl., emphatic)
parā = ultimate, supreme (f. nom. sg.)
kāṣṭhā = frontier, boundary, extreme limit, pinnacle (f. nom. sg. — lit. "a piece of wood used as a boundary marker," hence: the uttermost limit)
vairāgyam = dispassion (n. nom. sg.)		 Dispassion is the ultimate frontier of knowledge itself. Поразительное утверждение: вайрагья — это не противоположность знания и не его дополнение, а его kāṣṭhā — "предел," "крайняя точка," "граничный столб." Знание, достигшее своего максимума, автоматически становится отречением. Это не два разных достижения — это одно: полнота знания и есть совершенное отречение. Dispassion is the ultimate frontier of knowledge itself.
[6] etasyaiva hi nāntarīyakaṃ kaivalyam iti. etasya = of this (n. gen. sg., referring to vairāgya/jñāna)
eva = alone, indeed (indecl.)
hi = for, because (indecl., causal particle)
na antarīyakam = inseparable, that which has nothing in between (n. nom. sg.; na + antarīyaka = without interval, without intermediary — from antara + -īyaka — lit. "there is nothing between this and that")
kaivalyam = liberation, absolute isolation (n. nom. sg.; from kevala = alone, sole + -ya)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 For liberation is the inseparable [consequence] of this [dispassion/knowledge] alone. Заключительное слово: nāntarīyaka — "без промежутка." Между высшей вайрагьей и кайвальей нет ни единого шага, ни единого зазора. Это не причина и следствие через время — это одно и то же, без интервала (antara). Термин nāntarīyaka — технический: "то, что неотделимо от предыдущего," как огонь неотделим от жара. For liberation is the inseparable [consequence] of this [dispassion/knowledge] alone.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.17
athopāyadvayena niruddhacittavṛtteḥ katham ucyate saṃprajñātaḥ samādhir iti —
De-sandhi:
atha upāya-dvayena niruddha-citta-vṛtteḥ katham ucyate saṃprajñātaḥ samādhiḥ iti —		 atha = now (indecl.)
upāya-dvayena = by the pair of means (n. inst. sg.; upāya = means; dvaya = pair — referring to abhyāsa and vairāgya)
niruddha-citta-vṛtteḥ = of the one whose mental fluctuations have been suppressed (m. gen. sg., bahuvrīhi: niruddha = suppressed, PPP ni + √rudh; citta = mind; vṛtti = fluctuation)
katham = how? (indecl.)
ucyate = is described, is said (3rd sg. pres. pass. √vac)
saṃprajñātaḥ = saṃprajñāta, with-full-knowledge (m. nom. sg.)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Now, for the one whose mental fluctuations have been suppressed by the pair of means [abhyāsa and vairāgya] — how is saṃprajñāta samādhi described? Переходная фраза: если вритти уже ниродхированы (niruddhacittavṛtteḥ), как может быть самадхи "с познанием" (saṃprajñāta)? Ведь если всё подавлено, что там ещё познавать? Этот вопрос задаёт тон для 1.17: сампраджнята-самадхи — это не полное отсутствие содержания, а трансформированное содержание, поэтапно утончающееся. Now, for the one whose mental fluctuations have been suppressed by the pair of means [abhyāsa and vairāgya] — how is saṃprajñāta samādhi described?
Sūtra YS 1.17
vitarkavicārānandāsmitārūpānugamāt saṃprajñātaḥ || YS_1.17 ||
De-sandhi:
vitarka-vicāra-ānanda-asmitā-rūpa-anugamāt saṃprajñātaḥ ||		 vitarka-vicāra-ānanda-asmitā-rūpa-anugamāt = through the accompaniment of the forms: deliberation, reflection, bliss, and I-am-ness (m. abl. sg., compound: vitarka = deliberation, gross cognitive engagement, from vi + √tark — "reasoning about"; vicāra = reflection, subtle inquiry, from vi + √car — "moving through, examining"; ānanda = bliss, joy; asmitā = I-am-ness, the pure sense of being, from asmi + -tā — "the state of 'I am'"; rūpa = form, aspect; anugama = accompaniment, following after, from anu + √gam — "going along with." The ablative expresses cause/means)
saṃprajñātaḥ = with full knowledge, distinctly cognised (m. nom. sg.; sam + pra + √jñā — "thoroughly and distinctly known")		 [Samādhi] with full knowledge (saṃprajñātaḥ) [arises] through the accompaniment (anugamāt) of [these] forms (rūpa): deliberation (vitarka), reflection (vicāra), bliss (ānanda), and the sense of I-am (asmitā). Meaning: Saṃprajñāta samādhi is defined by four successive "accompaniments" (anugama) — four forms (rūpa) of residual mental content that progressively thin out. Vitarka is the grossest (engagement with a concrete object); vicāra is subtler (engagement with a subtle element); ānanda is subtler still (pure bliss, the sattva of buddhi shining); asmitā is the subtlest (only the bare sense of "I exist" remains). Each stage drops the preceding element.

Syntax: The entire compound functions as a single ablative of cause. Rūpa is the hinge word: "through the accompaniment (anugama) of the forms (rūpa) [called] vitarka, vicāra, ānanda, asmitā."

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Saṃprajñāta samādhi arises through the accompaniment of deliberation, reflection, bliss, and I-am-ness.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.17
[1] vitarkaś cittasyālambane sthūla ābhogaḥ. vitarkaḥ = deliberation (m. nom. sg.; vi + √tark)
cittasya = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
ālambane = upon the object, with regard to the support (n. loc. sg.; ālambana = support, substrate, from ā + √lamb)
sthūlaḥ = gross, coarse (m. nom. sg.)
ābhogaḥ = engagement, bending towards, full extension of attention (m. nom. sg.; ā + √bhuj — "bending round," curving of the mind towards its object, full embrace of the object)		 Deliberation is the mind's gross engagement with the object. Витарка — это sthūla ābhoga: "грубое объятие" ума со своим объектом. Слово ābhoga (от ā + √bhuj, "огибать, обнимать") передаёт полный контакт — ум полностью "облекает" объект, как рука обхватывает предмет. Sthūla ("грубый") указывает, что этот контакт — самый плотный, самый насыщенный содержанием. Deliberation is the mind's gross engagement with the object.
[2] sūkṣmo vicāraḥ. sūkṣmaḥ = subtle, fine (m. nom. sg.)
vicāraḥ = reflection, subtle examination (m. nom. sg.; vi + √car — "moving through")		 Reflection is [engagement that is] subtle. Вичара — то же самое "объятие," но тонкое (sūkṣma). Ум движется (√car) через объект не на уровне грубых форм (имя, форма, свойства), а на уровне тонких принципов (танматры, тонкие элементы). Минимальное определение: два слова. Вьяса контрастирует их со sthūla ābhoga — тем самым vicāra = sūkṣma ābhoga. Reflection is [engagement that is] subtle.
[3] ānando hlādaḥ. ānandaḥ = bliss, joy (m. nom. sg.)
hlādaḥ = delight, gladness, refreshment (m. nom. sg., from √hlād — "to be glad, to refresh")		 Bliss is delight. Ещё более краткое определение: ананда = hlāda ("радость, освежение"). На этой ступени нет ни грубого, ни тонкого объекта. Осталась лишь чистая радость — саттва буддхи, освобождённая от раджаса и тамаса, светится сама по себе. Bliss is delight.
[4] ekātmikā saṃvid asmitā. eka-ātmikā = unitary, having a single nature (f. nom. sg.; eka = one; ātmikā = having the nature of)
saṃvit = awareness, consciousness (f. nom. sg., from sam + √vid — "thorough knowing")
asmitā = I-am-ness (f. nom. sg.; asmi = "I am" + -tā — abstract suffix: "the state of 'I am'")		 I-am-ness is unitary awareness. Тончайшая ступень: ekātmikā saṃvit — "единичное сознание." Уже нет ни объекта, ни радости — осталось лишь голое "я есмь" (asmitā). Это не эго (ahaṃkāra) — это чистое ощущение бытия, единое (eka), неразделённое, без содержания. Последнее, что стоит между сампраджнятой и асампраджнятой — одна эта точка saṃvit. I-am-ness is unitary awareness.
[5] tatra prathamaś catuṣṭayānugataḥ samādhiḥ savitarkaḥ. tatra = of those, in that [series] (indecl.)
prathamaḥ = first (m. nom. sg.)
catuṣṭaya-anugataḥ = accompanied by the quartet, followed by [all] four (m. nom. sg.; catuṣṭaya = tetrad, set of four; anugata = followed by, accompanied, PPP anu + √gam)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
sa-vitarkaḥ = with deliberation (m. nom. sg.; sa = with; vitarka)		 Of those [four stages], the first samādhi, accompanied by the quartet, is savitarka. Первая ступень — savitarka — содержит все четыре компонента: витарку + вичару + ананду + асмиту. Все четыре присутствуют одновременно. Это самый "полный" самадхи — с максимумом содержания. Of those [four stages], the first samādhi, accompanied by the quartet, is savitarka.
[6] dvitīyo vitarkavikalaḥ savicāraḥ. dvitīyaḥ = second (m. nom. sg.)
vitarka-vikalaḥ = devoid of deliberation (m. nom. sg.; vitarka = deliberation; vikala = devoid of, lacking, deficient — from vi + kalā, "missing a part")
sa-vicāraḥ = with reflection (m. nom. sg.)		 The second, devoid of deliberation, is savicāra. Вторая ступень: витарка отпала (vikala — "ущербный, лишённый части"), осталась вичара + ананда + асмита. Ум больше не "обнимает" грубый объект — он работает лишь на тонком уровне. The second, devoid of deliberation, is savicāra.
[7] tṛtīyo vicāravikalaḥ sānandaḥ. tṛtīyaḥ = third (m. nom. sg.)
vicāra-vikalaḥ = devoid of reflection (m. nom. sg.)
sa-ānandaḥ = with bliss (m. nom. sg.)		 The third, devoid of reflection, is sānanda. Третья ступень: вичара тоже отпала, осталась ананда + асмита. Ни грубого, ни тонкого объекта — только блаженство и "я есмь." The third, devoid of reflection, is sānanda.
[8] caturthas tadvikalo 'smitāmātra iti. caturthaḥ = fourth (m. nom. sg.)
tat-vikalaḥ = devoid of that [bliss] (m. nom. sg.; tat = that; vikala = devoid)
asmitā-mātraḥ = nothing but I-am-ness (m. nom. sg.; asmitā + mātra = mere, only)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 The fourth, devoid of that [bliss], is nothing but I-am-ness. Четвёртая ступень: даже ананда отпала. Осталось asmitāmātra — "лишь асмита," чистое "я есмь" без какого-либо содержания, объекта или даже радости. Это последний порог перед асампраджнятой. The fourth, devoid of that [bliss], is nothing but I-am-ness.
[9] sarva ete sālambanāḥ samādhayaḥ. sarve = all (m. nom. pl.)
ete = these (m. nom. pl.)
sa-ālambanāḥ = with support, having an object (m. nom. pl.; sa = with; ālambana = support, object)
samādhayaḥ = samādhis (m. nom. pl.)		 All these samādhis have a support. Заключительная ремарка: все четыре ступени — sālambana ("с опорой"). У каждой есть хоть какой-то объект, хоть какое-то содержание. Даже четвёртая (asmitāmātra) имеет опору — само ощущение "я есмь." Асампраджнята-самадхи (1.18) будет nirālambana — без опоры вообще. Это подготовка к следующей сутре.

## Архитектура: от двойной вайрагьи к четырём самадхи

### Две вайрагьи

| | Apara-vairāgya (1.15) | Para-vairāgya (1.16) | |---|---|---| | Объект отречения | Конкретные viṣaya (видимые + из писаний) | Guṇas как таковые (vyakta + avyakta) | | Механизм | viṣayadoṣadarśin (видение изъяна) | puruṣakhyāti (видение пуруши) | | Результат | vaśīkārasaṃjñā (осознание владения) | jñānaprasādamātra (ясность знания) | | Характер | Активное владение | Безусильное знание |

### Четыре ступени сампраджнята-самадхи (1.17)

``` Ступень 1: SAVITARKA vitarka + vicāra + ānanda + asmitā ← все четыре │ ▼ (vitarka отпадает) Ступень 2: SAVICĀRA vicāra + ānanda + asmitā ← три │ ▼ (vicāra отпадает) Ступень 3: SĀNANDA ānanda + asmitā ← два │ ▼ (ānanda отпадает) Ступень 4: SĀSMITĀ asmitā-mātra ← одно │ ▼ (asmitā отпадает) ? ASAṂPRAJÑĀTA (1.18) ← ноль (nirālambana) ```

### Терминология четырёх факторов

| Фактор | Определение Вьясы | Уровень объекта | Русское приближение | |--------|-------------------|-----------------|---------------------| | Vitarka | sthūla ābhoga (грубое объятие) | Грубые объекты (sthūla) | Содержательное размышление | | Vicāra | sūkṣma [ābhoga] (тонкое объятие) | Тонкие объекты (sūkṣma) | Утончённое исследование | | Ānanda | hlāda (радость) | Нет объекта; саттва буддхи | Блаженство чистой саттвы | | Asmitā | ekātmikā saṃvit (единичное сознание) | Нет объекта; чистое "я есмь" | Голое ощущение бытия |

### Три утверждения йогина (кульминация паравайрагьи)

Вьяса вкладывает в уста йогина, достигшего паравайрагьи, три утверждения — каждое построено по формуле PPP + FPP от одного корня:

| Утверждение | PPP | FPP | Корень | Значение | |-------------|-----|-----|--------|----------| | prāptaṃ prāpaṇīyam | prāpta | prāpaṇīya | √āp | "Достигнуто то, что надлежало достичь" | | kṣīṇāḥ kṣetavyāḥ kleśāḥ | kṣīṇa | kṣetavya | √kṣi | "Уничтожены клеши, подлежавшие уничтожению" | | chinnaḥ bhavasaṃkramaḥ | chinna | — | √chid | "Рассечена цепь бытия" |

Это тройное "свершилось" — момент, когда между йогином и кайвальей нет промежутка (nāntarīyaka).

### Общая карта блока 1.12–1.17

``` 1.12 abhyāsa + vairāgya = два крыла 1.13 abhyāsa = yatna → sthiti (praśāntavāhitā) 1.14 условия: dīrgha-kāla + nairantarya + satkāra → dṛḍhabhūmi 1.15 apara-vairāgya = vaśīkārasaṃjñā (объекты dṛṣṭa + ānuśravika) 1.16 para-vairāgya = guṇavaitṛṣṇya ← puruṣakhyāti → jñānaprasādamātra → nāntarīyakaṃ kaivalyam 1.17 saṃprajñāta samādhi: 4 ступени нисходящего содержания savitarka → savicāra → sānanda → sāsmitā все sālambana (с опорой) ```

Следующая сутра (1.18) определит асампраджнята-самадхи — то, что лежит за пределами всех четырёх ступеней: самадхи без опоры, без содержания, без "я есмь."		 All these samādhis have a support.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.18
athāsaṃprajñātaḥ samādhiḥ kimupāyaḥ kiṃsvabhāvo veti —
De-sandhi:
atha asaṃprajñātaḥ samādhiḥ kim-upāyaḥ kim-svabhāvaḥ vā iti —		 atha = now (indecl.)
asaṃprajñātaḥ = without full knowledge (m. nom. sg.; a + sam + pra + √jñā)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
kim-upāyaḥ = what is [its] means? (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi: kim = what; upāya = means)
kim-svabhāvaḥ = what is [its] nature? (m. nom. sg.; kim = what; svabhāva = own-nature)
vā = or, and (indecl.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Now, what is the means and what is the nature of asaṃprajñāta samādhi? Два вопроса: (1) upāya — каким средством достигается асампраджнята? (2) svabhāva — какова его внутренняя природа? Ответы: средство — паравайрагья; природа — saṃskāraśeṣa (лишь остаток самскар). Now, what is the means and what is the nature of asaṃprajñāta samādhi?
Sūtra YS 1.18
virāmapratyayābhyāsapūrvaḥ saṃskāraśeṣo 'nyaḥ || YS_1.18 ||
De-sandhi:
virāma-pratyaya-abhyāsa-pūrvaḥ saṃskāra-śeṣaḥ anyaḥ ||		 virāma-pratyaya-abhyāsa-pūrvaḥ = preceded by the practice of the cognition of cessation (m. nom. sg., compound: virāma = cessation, stopping, from vi + √ram — "to cease, to rest"; pratyaya = cognition, mental content; abhyāsa = practice; pūrva = preceded by, having as [its] prior [cause])
saṃskāra-śeṣaḥ = having only a residue of latent impressions (m. nom. sg., bahuvrīhi: saṃskāra = latent impression; śeṣa = remainder, residue, from √śiṣ — "to leave over")
anyaḥ = the other (m. nom. sg. — referring to asaṃprajñāta, as distinct from saṃprajñāta of 1.17)		 The other [samādhi] (anyaḥ) is preceded by the practice of the cognition of cessation (virāma-pratyaya-abhyāsa-pūrvaḥ) and has only a residue of latent impressions (saṃskāra-śeṣaḥ). Meaning: Asaṃprajñāta samādhi is defined by three features: (1) it is anya — "the other," fundamentally different from saṃprajñāta; (2) it is preceded (pūrva) by the practice of virāmapratyaya — the "cognition of cessation," i.e. the mind practises stopping itself; (3) in this state, only saṃskāraśeṣa remains — no active content, no object, no bliss, no I-am-ness, only the residual imprints from past experience. This is the seedless (nirbīja) samādhi — or rather, the state approaching it, where seeds still exist but are dormant.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Anyaḥ is the subject (referring to asaṃprajñāta samādhi). Two bahuvrīhi compounds describe it: virāmapratyayābhyāsapūrvaḥ (its cause) and saṃskāraśeṣaḥ (its nature).

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 The other samādhi is preceded by the practice of the cognition of cessation, and in it only latent impressions remain.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.18
[1] sarvavṛttipratyastamaye saṃskāraśeṣo nirodhaś cittasya samādhir asaṃprajñātaḥ. sarva-vṛtti-pratyastamaye = when all fluctuations have set, upon the setting of all vṛttis (m. loc. sg., locative absolute: sarva = all; vṛtti = fluctuation; pratyastamaya = setting, going down, from prati + asta + √i — as the sun sets: prati-asta-maya = "the going towards setting")
saṃskāra-śeṣaḥ = having only a residue of latent impressions (m. nom. sg.)
nirodhaḥ = suppression, cessation (m. nom. sg.)
cittasya = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
asaṃprajñātaḥ = without full knowledge (m. nom. sg.)		 When all fluctuations have set [like the sun], the suppression of the mind that has only a residue of latent impressions — that is asaṃprajñāta samādhi. Замечательный образ: pratyastamaya — "заход," как солнце садится за горизонт. Все вритти "заходят" (prati + asta + √i), как светила на закате. Что остаётся после захода? Темнота? Нет — saṃskāraśeṣa: латентные следы, "зёрна," которые ещё существуют, но не проявлены. Ум не уничтожен — он лишён всякого активного содержания, но его "почва" (самскары) остаётся. When all fluctuations have set [like the sun], the suppression of the mind that has only a residue of latent impressions — that is asaṃprajñāta samādhi.
[2] tasya paraṃ vairāgyam upāyaḥ. tasya = for that (m. gen. sg.)
param = supreme (n. nom. sg.)
vairāgyam = dispassion (n. nom. sg.)
upāyaḥ = means (m. nom. sg.)		 The means for that [asaṃprajñāta samādhi] is the supreme dispassion. Ответ на первый вопрос Вьясы (kim upāyaḥ): средство — paraṃ vairāgyam, высшая вайрагья из 1.16 (guṇavaitṛṣṇya). Абхьяса тут бессильна — потому что любая практика предполагает объект, а асампраджнята по определению безобъектна. The means for that [asaṃprajñāta samādhi] is the supreme dispassion.
[3] sālambano hy abhyāsas tatsādhanāya na kalpata iti virāmapratyayo nirvastuka ālambanīkriyate. sa-ālambanaḥ = having a support, with an object (m. nom. sg.)
hi = for, because (indecl.)
abhyāsaḥ = practice (m. nom. sg.)
tat-sādhanāya = as a means for that (n. dat. sg.; tat = that; sādhana = means)
na = not (indecl.)
kalpate = is suitable, is fit for (3rd sg. pres. ā. √kḷp — "to be in order, to serve as")
iti = therefore (indecl.)
virāma-pratyayaḥ = the cognition of cessation (m. nom. sg.)
nir-vastukaḥ = objectless, without a thing (m. nom. sg.; niḥ + vastu + -ka)
ālambanī-kriyate = is made into a support (3rd sg. pres. pass.; ālambanī + √kṛ — denominative: "to make into an ālambana")		 For practice that has a support is not suitable as a means for that [asaṃprajñāta]. Therefore the objectless cognition of cessation is made into the support. Парадокс: как практиковать отсутствие объекта? Решение: virāmapratyaya — "познание прекращения" — само становится ālambanī-kriyate (превращается в опору). Это познание, чьё содержание — остановка. Оно nirvastuka (безвещное), и всё же функционирует как квази-опора. Это мост между sālambana (самадхи с опорой) и nirālambana (самадхи без опоры): вы "опираетесь" на само прекращение опоры. For practice that has a support is not suitable as a means for that [asaṃprajñāta]. Therefore the objectless cognition of cessation is made into the support.
[4] sa cārthaśūnyaḥ. saḥ = that (m. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
artha-śūnyaḥ = void of content, empty of meaning/object (m. nom. sg.; artha = object, content; śūnya = empty)		 And that [cognition of cessation] is void of content. Arthaśūnya — "пустой от содержания." Ср. vastuśūnya из определения викальпы (1.9). Но если викальпа пуста, потому что слова не обеспечены реальностью, то вирама-пратьяя пуста намеренно: это сознательное вхождение в пустоту. And that [cognition of cessation] is void of content.
[5] tadabhyāsapūrvakaṃ hi cittaṃ nirālambanam abhāvaprāptam iva bhavatīty eṣa nirbījaḥ samādhir asaṃprajñātaḥ. tat-abhyāsa-pūrvakam = preceded by the practice of that (n. nom. sg.; tat = that [virāmapratyaya]; abhyāsa = practice; pūrvaka = preceded by, having as prior [cause])
hi = for, indeed (indecl.)
cittam = the mind (n. nom. sg.)
nir-ālambanam = without support, objectless (n. nom. sg.)
abhāva-prāptam = having attained non-existence (n. nom. sg.; abhāva = non-existence; prāpta = attained, PPP pra + √āp)
iva = as if, as though (indecl.)
bhavati = becomes (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)
iti = thus (indecl.)
eṣaḥ = this (m. nom. sg.)
nir-bījaḥ = seedless, without seed (m. nom. sg.; niḥ + bīja)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
asaṃprajñātaḥ = without full knowledge (m. nom. sg.)		 For the mind, preceded by the practice of that [cognition of cessation], becomes as if it had attained non-existence — without support. This is the seedless, asaṃprajñāta samādhi. Кульминация: ум становится nirālambana (без опоры), abhāvaprāptam iva — "как будто достигший небытия." Важнейшее слово — iva ("как будто"): ум не уничтожается — он "как будто" перестаёт существовать. Самскары ещё есть (saṃskāraśeṣa), но активной деятельности нет. И наконец — первое появление термина nirbīja ("безсеменный"): самадхи, в котором семена будущих вритти больше не прорастают. Это и есть высшая цель Самадхи-пады. For the mind, preceded by the practice of that [cognition of cessation], becomes as if it had attained non-existence — without support. This is the seedless, asaṃprajñāta samādhi.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.19
sa khalv ayaṃ dvividhaḥ — upāyapratyayo bhavapratyayaś ca. tatropāyapratyayo yogināṃ bhavati —
De-sandhi:
saḥ khalu ayam dvi-vidhaḥ — upāya-pratyayaḥ bhava-pratyayaḥ ca. tatra upāya-pratyayaḥ yoginām bhavati —		 saḥ khalu ayam = that, certainly, this (m. nom. sg. + emphatic + m. nom. sg.)
dvi-vidhaḥ = of two kinds (m. nom. sg.)
upāya-pratyayaḥ = caused by means, arising from [deliberate] method (m. nom. sg.; upāya = means; pratyaya = here: cause, basis)
bhava-pratyayaḥ = caused by existence, arising from [the momentum of] being (m. nom. sg.; bhava = existence, becoming)
ca = and (indecl.)
tatra = of those (indecl.)
yoginām = of yogins, for yogins (m. gen. pl.)
bhavati = belongs, arises (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)		 That [asaṃprajñāta samādhi] is certainly of two kinds — caused by means [of practice] and caused by [the momentum of] existence. Of those, the one caused by means belongs to yogins — Критическое разветвление: существуют два пути к состоянию, напоминающему асампраджняту. Один — upāyapratyaya (через сознательную практику) — принадлежит йогинам. Другой — bhavapratyaya (через инерцию бытия) — принадлежит видехам и пракритилаям. Второй путь — ловушка, и 1.19 предупреждает о нём. That [asaṃprajñāta samādhi] is certainly of two kinds — caused by means [of practice] and caused by [the momentum of] existence. Of those, the one caused by means belongs to yogins —
Sūtra YS 1.19
bhavapratyayo videhaprakṛtilayānām || YS_1.19 ||
De-sandhi:
bhava-pratyayaḥ videha-prakṛti-layānām ||		 bhava-pratyayaḥ = caused by existence, arising from the momentum of being (m. nom. sg.; bhava = existence, becoming, from √bhū; pratyaya = cause, basis)
videha-prakṛti-layānām = of the bodiless ones and those absorbed in prakṛti (m. gen. pl., dvandva cpd.: videha = bodiless, without body, from vi + deha; prakṛti-laya = absorbed in prakṛti, dissolved into primordial nature, from prakṛti + laya, from √lī — "to dissolve, to merge")		 [The samādhi that is] caused by [the momentum of] existence (bhava-pratyayaḥ) belongs to the bodiless ones and those absorbed in prakṛti (videha-prakṛti-layānām). Meaning: This is a warning sūtra. Some beings attain states that resemble asaṃprajñāta — the videhas (bodiless gods) and prakṛtilayas (those merged in undifferentiated prakṛti) — but their state is bhavapratyaya: caused by the momentum of existence, not by genuine liberative knowledge. They will return to saṃsāra when their merit is exhausted. Their "samādhi" is a cosmic timeout, not liberation.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Bhavapratyayaḥ is the predicate; videhaprakṛtilayānām is a genitive of possession/belonging.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 The samādhi caused by the momentum of existence belongs to the bodiless beings and those merged in prakṛti.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.19
[1] videhānāṃ devānāṃ bhavapratyayaḥ. videhānām = of the bodiless ones (m. gen. pl.)
devānām = of the gods (m. gen. pl. — apposition: the videhas are gods)
bhava-pratyayaḥ = caused by existence (m. nom. sg.)		 For the bodiless gods, [the cause is] the momentum of existence. Вьяса уточняет: videha = deva (боги). Это боги, обитающие без физического тела, в тонких мирах. Их состояние "безобъектности" — не плод различения, а побочный эффект божественного бытия. For the bodiless gods, [the cause is] the momentum of existence.
[2] te hi svasaṃskāramātropayogena cittena kaivalyapadam ivānubhavantaḥ svasaṃskāravipākaṃ tathājātīyakam ativāhayanti. te = they (m. nom. pl.)
hi = for, indeed (indecl.)
sva-saṃskāra-mātra-upayogena = by means of [functioning] only through their own latent impressions (m. inst. sg.; sva = own; saṃskāra = latent impression; mātra = only; upayoga = use, employment, from upa + √yuj)
cittena = through the mind (n. inst. sg.)
kaivalya-padam = the state of liberation (n. acc. sg.; kaivalya = absolute freedom; pada = state, place, step)
iva = as if, as though (indecl.)
anubhavantaḥ = experiencing (m. nom. pl., PPA anu + √bhū)
sva-saṃskāra-vipākam = the ripening of their own latent impressions (m. acc. sg.; vipāka = ripening, maturation, from vi + √pac — "cooking through")
tathā-jātīyakam = of that same kind, of the same type (n. acc. sg.; tathā = thus, of that kind; jātīyaka = belonging to the type)
ativāhayanti = they pass through, they spend [time] (3rd pl. pres. caus. ati + √vah — "to carry across/beyond")		 For they, experiencing something like the state of liberation through the mind that functions only by means of its own latent impressions, pass through the ripening of their own latent impressions of that same kind. Ключевое слово — iva ("как будто"). Видехи как будто переживают кайвалью (kaivalyapadam iva), но это лишь vipāka (созревание) их собственных самскар. Их ум функционирует минимально (svasaṃskāramātropayogena — "используя лишь свои собственные самскары"), создавая иллюзию освобождения. Но самскары созревают (vipāka — от vi + √pac, "варятся"), а не уничтожаются. Когда "варка" закончится — они вернутся. For they, experiencing something like the state of liberation through the mind that functions only by means of its own latent impressions, pass through the ripening of their own latent impressions of that same kind.
[3] tathā prakṛtilayāḥ sādhikāre cetasi prakṛtilīne kaivalyapadam ivānubhavanti, yāvan na punar āvartate 'dhikāravaśāc cittam iti. tathā = likewise (indecl.)
prakṛti-layāḥ = those absorbed in prakṛti (m. nom. pl.)
sa-adhikāre = still possessing its authority/capacity (n. loc. sg., locative absolute; sa = with; adhikāra = authority, entitlement, competence, karmic right — the mind retains its "karmic credentials")
cetasi = when the mind (n. loc. sg., locative absolute)
prakṛti-līne = has dissolved into prakṛti (n. loc. sg., PPP lī + na; prakṛti = primordial nature)
kaivalya-padam = the state of liberation (n. acc. sg.)
iva = as if (indecl.)
anubhavanti = they experience (3rd pl. pres. anu + √bhū)
yāvat = until, so long as (indecl.)
na = not (indecl.)
punaḥ = again (indecl.)
āvartate = returns, revolves back (3rd sg. pres. ā + √vṛt)
adhikāra-vaśāt = driven by the force of [remaining] authority (m. abl. sg.; adhikāra = authority, right; vaśa = force, power, control)
cittam = the mind (n. nom. sg.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Likewise those absorbed in prakṛti, when their mind — still possessing its potential authority — has dissolved into prakṛti, experience something like the state of liberation, until the mind returns again, driven by the force of [its remaining] authority. Пракритилайи растворяют ум в пракрити — но их ум sādhikāra: он сохраняет свой "мандат" (adhikāra), своё кармическое право на существование. Ум līna (растворён), но не naṣṭa (уничтожен). Слово yāvat ("до тех пор пока") — приговор: это состояние временно. Когда кармический заряд (adhikāravaśāt) вновь набирает силу, ум āvartate (возвращается, "оборачивается обратно" — от ā + √vṛt). Saṃsāra буквально означает "вращение," и ум снова вращается. Likewise those absorbed in prakṛti, when their mind — still possessing its potential authority — has dissolved into prakṛti, experience something like the state of liberation, until the mind returns again, driven by the force of [its remaining] authority.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.20
śraddhāvīryasmṛtisamādhiprajñāpūrvaka itareṣām || YS_1.20 ||
De-sandhi:
śraddhā-vīrya-smṛti-samādhi-prajñā-pūrvakaḥ itareṣām ||		 śraddhā-vīrya-smṛti-samādhi-prajñā-pūrvakaḥ = preceded by faith, energy, memory, samādhi, and wisdom (m. nom. sg., compound: śraddhā = faith, trust, from śrat + √dhā; vīrya = energy, heroic vigour, from √vīr; smṛti = memory, mindfulness, from √smṛ; samādhi = meditative absorption; prajñā = wisdom, discerning knowledge, from pra + √jñā; pūrvaka = preceded by, having as [its] prior [steps])
itareṣām = for the others (m. gen. pl.; itara = other, the rest — "the rest" as opposed to the videhas and prakṛtilayas of 1.19)		 For the others (itareṣām), [that asaṃprajñāta samādhi is] preceded by (pūrvakaḥ) faith (śraddhā), energy (vīrya), memory (smṛti), samādhi (samādhi), and wisdom (prajñā). Meaning: After the warning of 1.19, Patañjali describes the genuine path to asaṃprajñāta samādhi — the path of the yogins (itareṣām = "for the others," i.e. those who are not videhas or prakṛtilayas). The path is a chain of five: śraddhā → vīrya → smṛti → samādhi → prajñā. Each link produces the next in sequence, forming a causal cascade.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. The entire compound śraddhā...pūrvakaḥ is the predicate; itareṣām is a genitive of belonging. The order within the compound is significant — it represents a sequential causation.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 For the others — the yogins — that samādhi is preceded by faith, energy, memory, concentration, and wisdom.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.20
[1] upāyapratyayo yogināṃ bhavati. upāya-pratyayaḥ = caused by means (m. nom. sg.)
yoginām = for yogins (m. gen. pl.)
bhavati = arises, belongs (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)		 For yogins, [the cause] is the means [of practice]. Краткое противопоставление 1.19: там — bhavapratyaya (инерция бытия), здесь — upāyapratyaya (сознательное средство). For yogins, [the cause] is the means [of practice].
[2] śraddhā cetasaḥ saṃprasādaḥ. śraddhā = faith (f. nom. sg.)
cetasaḥ = of the mind (n. gen. sg.)
saṃprasādaḥ = serenity, clarity, deep calmness (m. nom. sg.; sam + pra + √sad — "thorough settling," the deepest form of prasāda)		 Faith is the serenity of the mind. Шраддха определяется не как слепая вера, а как saṃprasāda — "глубокая ясность ума." Тот же корень √sad (оседать), что и prasāda из 1.16 (jñānaprasādamātra), но с усиленным приставочным рядом: sam + pra. Это тихая, глубокая уверенность, подобная прозрачной воде, в которой нет мути сомнения. Faith is the serenity of the mind.
[3] sā hi jananīva kalyāṇī yoginaṃ pāti. sā = she [śraddhā] (f. nom. sg.)
hi = for, indeed (indecl.)
jananī = mother (f. nom. sg., from √jan)
iva = like (indecl.)
kalyāṇī = auspicious, good, virtuous (f. nom. sg.)
yoginam = the yogin (m. acc. sg.)
pāti = protects (3rd sg. pres. √pā — "to guard, to protect")		 For she, like a good mother, protects the yogin. Одна из самых нежных метафор в Бхашье: шраддха — как добрая мать (jananī kalyāṇī), которая защищает (pāti, от √pā) йогина. Не учит, не направляет — именно защищает. Как мать оберегает ребёнка, пока он растёт, так вера оберегает практикующего, пока он ещё уязвим. Это первое звено цепи — и без него цепь не начнётся. For she, like a good mother, protects the yogin.
[4] tasya hi śraddadhānasya vivekārthino vīryam upajāyate. tasya = of that one, for that one (m. gen. sg.)
hi = for (indecl.)
śraddadhānasya = of the one who has faith, of the believing one (m. gen. sg., PPA śrat + √dhā — "placing [the heart]," middle voice: śraddadhāna = "one who is placing trust")
viveka-arthinaḥ = of the one who seeks discrimination (m. gen. sg.; viveka = discrimination; arthin = seeker, desirous of, from artha + -in)
vīryam = energy, vigour (n. nom. sg.)
upajāyate = arises, is born (3rd sg. pres. upa + √jan — "arises near")		 For in that one who has faith, who seeks discrimination — energy arises. Второе звено: вера порождает энергию (vīrya). Но с важным уточнением: энергия возникает в том, кто śraddadhāna (верящем) и vivekārthin (ищущем различения). Не слепая вера, а вера, направленная на различение — вот что порождает героическую силу. For in that one who has faith, who seeks discrimination — energy arises.
[5] samupajātavīryasya smṛtir upatiṣṭhate. sam-upajāta-vīryasya = of the one in whom energy has fully arisen (m. gen. sg., bahuvrīhi: sam + upa + jāta = fully arisen, PPP sam + upa + √jan; vīrya = energy)
smṛtiḥ = memory, mindfulness (f. nom. sg.)
upatiṣṭhate = presents itself, stands ready, comes to serve (3rd sg. pres. ā. upa + √sthā — "stands near, presents itself in readiness")		 For one in whom energy has fully arisen, memory presents itself. Третье звено: энергия → память. Upatiṣṭhate (от upa + √sthā) — замечательный глагол: "становится рядом, предстаёт." Память не вызывается силой — она сама приходит (upatiṣṭhate) к тому, в ком есть энергия. Как слуга, который встаёт рядом с хозяином, готовый к службе. For one in whom energy has fully arisen, memory presents itself.
[6] smṛtyupasthāne ca cittam anākulaṃ samādhīyate. smṛti-upasthāne = when memory has presented itself (n. loc. sg., locative absolute; smṛti = memory; upasthāna = presenting itself, being at hand, from upa + √sthā)
ca = and (indecl.)
cittam = the mind (n. nom. sg.)
an-ākulam = unperturbed, unconfused, clear (n. nom. sg.; an = not; ākula = confused, agitated)
samādhīyate = becomes concentrated, enters samādhi (3rd sg. pres. pass. denominative from samādhi + √ya — "becomes samādhi-ified," a remarkable denominative passive: the mind literally "samādhi-fies itself")		 And when memory has presented itself, the mind, unperturbed, becomes concentrated. Четвёртое звено: память → самадхи. Глагол samādhīyate — деноминатив от samādhi: ум буквально "самадхифицируется." Условие: ум должен быть anākula ("неволнуемый"). Память даёт уму устойчивость, и в этой устойчивости ум сам собой входит в состояние собранности. And when memory has presented itself, the mind, unperturbed, becomes concentrated.
[7] samāhitacittasya prajñāviveka upāvartate. samāhita-cittasya = of one whose mind is concentrated (m. gen. sg., bahuvrīhi: samāhita = concentrated, composed, PPP sam + ā + √dhā; citta = mind)
prajñā-vivekaḥ = discriminative wisdom (m. nom. sg.; prajñā = wisdom; viveka = discrimination)
upāvartate = comes round, turns towards, presents itself (3rd sg. pres. ā. upa + ā + √vṛt — "turns itself towards [the yogin]")		 For one whose mind is concentrated, discriminative wisdom comes round. Пятое звено: самадхи → праджня. Upāvartate (upa + ā + √vṛt) — "поворачивается к": мудрость сама поворачивается к йогину с сосредоточенным умом. Как и в случае со смрити (upatiṣṭhate), высшие состояния не берутся силой — они приходят сами, когда условия созрели. For one whose mind is concentrated, discriminative wisdom comes round.
[8] yena yathārthaṃ vastu jānāti. yena = by which (n. inst. sg., rel. pron.)
yathā-artham = as it truly is, in accordance with reality (adv.; yathā = as; artha = reality, meaning)
vastu = reality, the real thing (n. acc. sg.)
jānāti = knows (3rd sg. pres. √jñā)		 By which [wisdom] one knows reality as it truly is. Результат праджни: yathārthaṃ vastu jānāti — "знает вещь как она есть." Не как она кажется, не как слова её описывают — а yathārtha ("в соответствии с реальностью"). By which [wisdom] one knows reality as it truly is.
[9] tadabhyāsāt tattadviṣayāc ca vairāgyād asaṃprajñātaḥ samādhir bhavati. tat-abhyāsāt = from the practice of that [prajñāviveka] (m. abl. sg.)
tat-tat-viṣayāt = towards each respective object [of each stage] (m. abl. sg.; tat-tat = each respective, "this and that")
ca = and (indecl.)
vairāgyāt = from dispassion (n. abl. sg.)
asaṃprajñātaḥ = asaṃprajñāta (m. nom. sg.)
samādhiḥ = samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
bhavati = arises (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)		 From the practice of that [discriminative wisdom] and from dispassion towards each of those [stages of saṃprajñāta] — asaṃprajñāta samādhi arises. Замыкание: цепь из пяти звеньев порождает праджню, которая через abhyāsa (практику) и vairāgya (отречение от каждого предшествующего уровня: tattadviṣayāt — "от каждого соответствующего объекта") приводит к асампраджнята-самадхи. Заметьте, что abhyāsa и vairāgya из 1.12 снова появляются здесь — два крыла работают до самого конца. From the practice of that [discriminative wisdom] and from dispassion towards each of those [stages of saṃprajñāta] — asaṃprajñāta samādhi arises.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.21
te khalu nava yogino mṛdumadhyādhimātropāyā bhavanti. tadyathā — mṛdūpāyo madhyopāyo 'dhimātropāya iti. tatra mṛdūpāyas trividhaḥ — mṛdusaṃvego madhyasaṃvegas tīvrasaṃvega iti. tathā madhyopāyas tathādhimātropāya iti. tatrādhimātropāyānām —
De-sandhi:
te khalu nava yoginaḥ mṛdu-madhya-adhimātra-upāyāḥ bhavanti. tat yathā — mṛdu-upāyaḥ madhya-upāyaḥ adhimātra-upāyaḥ iti. tatra mṛdu-upāyaḥ tri-vidhaḥ — mṛdu-saṃvegaḥ madhya-saṃvegaḥ tīvra-saṃvegaḥ iti. tathā madhya-upāyaḥ tathā adhimātra-upāyaḥ iti. tatra adhimātra-upāyānām —		 te = these (m. nom. pl.)
khalu = indeed (indecl.)
nava = nine (indecl.)
yoginaḥ = yogins (m. nom. pl.)
mṛdu-madhya-adhimātra-upāyāḥ = [classified by] mild, medium, and intense means (m. nom. pl., compound: mṛdu = mild, soft; madhya = medium, middle; adhimātra = intense, exceeding the measure, from adhi + mātrā; upāya = means)
saṃvegaḥ = urgency, vehemence, impetus (m. nom. sg.; sam + √vij — "to be shaken, agitated" — the inner force of spiritual urgency)
tīvra = intense, sharp, acute (from √tīv)
tri-vidhaḥ = threefold (m. nom. sg.)		 These yogins are indeed nine, [classified by] mild, medium, and intense means. Namely: one of mild means, one of medium means, one of intense means. Of those, the one of mild means is threefold: [having] mild urgency, medium urgency, and intense urgency. Likewise the one of medium means; likewise the one of intense means. Of those, for those of intense means — Вьяса строит матрицу 3×3 = 9 типов йогинов. Два параметра: upāya (качество средств: мягкие, средние, интенсивные) и saṃvega (степень внутренней настоятельности). Saṃvega (от sam + √vij, "встряхивание") — это внутреннее рвение, духовная неотложность, "трясучка" стремления к освобождению. These yogins are indeed nine, [classified by] mild, medium, and intense means. Namely: one of mild means, one of medium means, one of intense means. Of those, the one of mild means is threefold: [having] mild urgency, medium urgency, and intense urgency. Likewise the one of medium means; likewise the one of intense means. Of those, for those of intense means —
Sūtra YS 1.21
tīvrasaṃvegānām āsannaḥ || YS_1.21 ||
De-sandhi:
tīvra-saṃvegānām āsannaḥ ||		 tīvra-saṃvegānām = of those with intense urgency (m. gen. pl.; tīvra = intense, sharp; saṃvega = urgency, impetus)
āsannaḥ = near, close at hand (m. nom. sg.; ā + √sad — PPP: "sat down near," i.e. nearby, imminent)		 For those of intense urgency (tīvra-saṃvegānām), [samādhi is] near (āsannaḥ). Meaning: A brief, encouraging sūtra: the intensity of spiritual urgency (saṃvega) determines how quickly samādhi arrives. Āsanna literally means "having sat down nearby" — samādhi is already sitting next to the yogin of intense urgency, waiting to be noticed.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. The genitive tīvrasaṃvegānām is a possessive genitive; the implied subject is samādhi (or its attainment).

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).

### Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.21

> samādhilābhaḥ samādhiphalaṃ ca bhavatīti.

Translation: Both the attainment of samādhi and the fruit of samādhi arise [for them].

Word-by-word:

- samādhi-lābhaḥ = attainment of samādhi (m. nom. sg.; lābha = attainment, gain, from √labh) - samādhi-phalam = the fruit of samādhi (n. nom. sg.; phala = fruit, result) - ca = and (indecl.) - bhavati = arises (3rd sg. pres. √bhū) - iti = thus (indecl.)

Note: Тонкое различие: не только lābha (достижение самадхи как состояния), но и phala (плод самадхи — т.е. кайвалья). Для тех, у кого самвега максимален, оба результата близки.		 For those whose spiritual urgency is intense, samādhi is near.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.22
mṛdumadhyādhimātratvāt tato 'pi viśeṣaḥ || YS_1.22 ||
De-sandhi:
mṛdu-madhya-adhimātratvāt tataḥ api viśeṣaḥ ||		 mṛdu-madhya-adhimātratvāt = because of the [quality of being] mild, medium, and intense (n. abl. sg.; mṛdu = mild; madhya = medium; adhimātra = intense; -tva = abstract suffix: "the state of being mild-medium-intense" — the ablative expresses cause)
tataḥ = from that, among those (indecl.)
api = even, also (indecl.)
viśeṣaḥ = distinction, differentiation (m. nom. sg.; vi + √śiṣ — "that which remains apart, is distinguished")		 Because of the [further division into] mild, medium, and intense (mṛdu-madhya-adhimātratvāt), there is a further distinction (viśeṣaḥ) even among those (tataḥ api). Meaning: The 3×3 matrix becomes a 3×3×3 model — or at least, within the "intense" category of 1.21, there are further subdivisions. Even among the tīvrasaṃvega yogins, some are mṛdu-tīvra (mildly intense), some madhya-tīvra (moderately intense), and some adhimātra-tīvra (supremely intense). For the last, samādhi is āsannatama — nearest of all.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. The ablative mṛdumadhyādhimātratvāt gives the reason for the viśeṣa.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Even among those of intense urgency, there is a further distinction according to whether that intensity is mild, medium, or supreme.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.22
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Sūtra YS 1.23
īśvarapraṇidhānād vā || YS_1.23 ||
De-sandhi:
īśvara-praṇidhānāt vā ||		 īśvara-praṇidhānāt = through devotion to Īśvara, from the placing [of oneself] before the Lord (n. abl. sg.; īśvara = the Lord, the supreme puruṣa, from √īś — "to rule, to be master"; praṇidhāna = placing before, devotion, profound meditation upon, from pra + ni + √dhā — "setting down before," complete surrender — the ablative expresses means/cause)
vā = or, alternatively (indecl. — offering this as an alternative path alongside abhyāsa-vairāgya and the five-link chain)		 Or (vā), [samādhi is attained] through devotion to Īśvara (īśvara-praṇidhānāt). Meaning: The particle vā ("or") is crucial: Patañjali offers īśvarapraṇidhāna as an alternative route to samādhi, alongside the gradual path of abhyāsa-vairāgya described in 1.12–1.22. This is not a mandatory theistic requirement but an option — a shortcut for those with the capacity for complete self-surrender. Praṇidhāna (pra + ni + √dhā) means "placing down before" — the total laying-down of oneself at the feet of the Lord.

Syntax: A minimal sentence. The ablative praṇidhānāt expresses cause/means; the implied verb is "samādhi arises/is attained." Vā connects this sūtra to the preceding discussion as an alternative.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Or, through wholehearted devotion to Īśvara.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.23
[1] praṇidhānād bhaktiviśeṣād āvarjita īśvaras tam anugṛhṇāty abhidhyānamātreṇa. praṇidhānāt = through/by praṇidhāna (n. abl. sg.)
bhakti-viśeṣāt = by a special form of devotion (m. abl. sg.; bhakti = devotion, from √bhaj — "to share, to serve, to worship"; viśeṣa = special, particular)
āvarjitaḥ = inclined, drawn towards, won over (m. nom. sg., PPP ā + √vṛj — "turned towards, bent towards" — as a tree inclines towards the one who waters it)
īśvaraḥ = Īśvara, the Lord (m. nom. sg.)
tam = that [yogin] (m. acc. sg.)
anugṛhṇāti = bestows grace upon, favours (3rd sg. pres. anu + √grah — "grasps towards, takes up [in protection]")
abhidhyāna-mātreṇa = by mere intent meditation (n. inst. sg.; abhidhyāna = intent meditation, focused contemplation, from abhi + √dhyai — "meditating upon"; mātra = mere, only)		 Inclined by praṇidhāna — a special form of devotion — Īśvara bestows grace upon that [yogin] by mere intent meditation [upon him]. Вьяса определяет пранидхану как bhaktiviśeṣa — "особый вид бхакти," не обычное поклонение, а нечто высшее. Ишвара āvarjita (наклонён, привлечён) этой преданностью — замечательный образ: Бог, склоняющийся к преданному, как дерево наклоняется. И его ответ — anugraha ("благодать," буквально anu + √grah = "схватывание вслед," т.е. подхватывание, поддержка). Механизм: abhidhyānamātra — "лишь глубокая медитация." Ишвара отвечает не на ритуалы или жертвы, а на чистое медитативное сосредоточение. Inclined by praṇidhāna — a special form of devotion — Īśvara bestows grace upon that [yogin] by mere intent meditation [upon him].
[2] tadabhidhyānamātrād api yogina āsannatamaḥ samādhilābhaḥ samādhiphalaṃ ca bhavatīti. tat-abhidhyāna-mātrāt = from merely that intent meditation (n. abl. sg.)
api = also, too (indecl.)
yoginaḥ = for the yogin (m. gen. sg.)
āsannatamaḥ = nearest (m. nom. sg., superlative of āsanna — reprising the language of 1.22)
samādhi-lābhaḥ = attainment of samādhi (m. nom. sg.)
samādhi-phalam = the fruit of samādhi (n. nom. sg.)
ca = and (indecl.)
bhavati = becomes, arises (3rd sg. pres. √bhū)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 From merely that intent meditation too, for the yogin, the attainment of samādhi and the fruit of samādhi become nearest. Та же формула āsannatama из 1.22, но теперь без матрицы 9 типов: ишварапранидхана даёт āsannatama ("ближайший") результат сама по себе. Это подтверждает, что путь бхакти — не ниже пути различения, а параллелен ему и столь же эффективен. From merely that intent meditation too, for the yogin, the attainment of samādhi and the fruit of samādhi become nearest.
Sanskrit Text Word-by-Word Analysis Literal Translation Notes Clean Translation
Vyāsa's Introductory Line to YS 1.24
atha pradhānapuruṣavyatiriktaḥ ko 'yam īśvaro nāmeti —
De-sandhi:
atha pradhāna-puruṣa-vyatiriktaḥ kaḥ ayam īśvaraḥ nāma iti —		 atha = now (indecl.)
pradhāna-puruṣa-vyatiriktaḥ = distinct from pradhāna and [ordinary] puruṣa (m. nom. sg.; pradhāna = the chief [principle], i.e. prakṛti; puruṣa = the Self; vyatirikta = distinct from, separate, PPP vi + ati + √ric — "left over beyond, exceeding")
kaḥ = who? (m. nom. sg.)
ayam = this (m. nom. sg.)
īśvaraḥ = Īśvara (m. nom. sg.)
nāma = called, by name (indecl.)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 Now, who is this one called Īśvara, who is distinct from pradhāna [= prakṛti] and [ordinary] puruṣa? Вьяса ставит онтологический вопрос: если вся реальность — это пракрити (pradhāna) и пуруши, то кто такой Ишвара? Он vyatirikta ("обособлен") от обоих — не часть пракрити и не обычный пуруша. Это подготавливает определение Ишвары как puruṣaviśeṣa ("особый пуруша") в сутре 1.24. Now, who is this one called Īśvara, who is distinct from pradhāna [= prakṛti] and [ordinary] puruṣa?
Sūtra YS 1.24
kleśakarmavipākāśayair aparāmṛṣṭaḥ puruṣaviśeṣa īśvaraḥ || YS_1.24 ||
De-sandhi:
kleśa-karma-vipāka-āśayaiḥ aparāmṛṣṭaḥ puruṣa-viśeṣaḥ īśvaraḥ ||		 kleśa-karma-vipāka-āśayaiḥ = by afflictions, karma, [karmic] ripening, and latent deposits (m. inst. pl., dvandva cpd.: kleśa = affliction, from √kliś; karma = action, deed; vipāka = ripening, maturation, from vi + √pac; āśaya = latent deposit, repository, resting place, from ā + √śī — "lying in," the subliminal storehouse)
aparāmṛṣṭaḥ = untouched, not grasped by (m. nom. sg.; a + parā + √mṛś — "not touched from all sides," not seized — the same root √mṛś as in pratyavamarśa from 1.10, but here negated and intensified with parā)
puruṣa-viśeṣaḥ = a special puruṣa, a unique Self (m. nom. sg.; puruṣa = Self; viśeṣa = special, particular, distinct)
īśvaraḥ = Īśvara, the Lord (m. nom. sg.)		 Īśvara (īśvaraḥ) is a special puruṣa (puruṣa-viśeṣaḥ), untouched (aparāmṛṣṭaḥ) by the afflictions (kleśa), karma (karma), the ripening [of karma] (vipāka), and the latent deposits (āśaya). Meaning: Īśvara is defined not by positive attributes (omnipotence, creation) but apophatically — by what does not touch him. He is aparāmṛṣṭa ("untouched") by the four-link chain of saṃsāric bondage: kleśa (afflictions) → karma (actions driven by affliction) → vipāka (karmic ripening) → āśaya (latent deposits that restart the cycle). The term puruṣaviśeṣa is carefully chosen: Īśvara is a puruṣa — not a different ontological category — but a special one, one who has never been in bondage.

Syntax: A nominal sentence. Īśvaraḥ and puruṣaviśeṣaḥ are in apposition (both subject/predicate). Aparāmṛṣṭaḥ is the defining participle, with the instrumental kleśa...āśayaiḥ expressing the agents that fail to touch him.

Metre: Prose (sūtra form).		 Īśvara is a special puruṣa, untouched by afflictions, karma, karmic ripening, and latent deposits.
Vyāsabhāṣya on YS 1.24
[1] avidyādayaḥ kleśāḥ. avidyā-ādayaḥ = ignorance and so forth, beginning with ignorance (m. nom. pl.; avidyā = ignorance; ādayaḥ = and the others, beginning with, from ādi = beginning)
kleśāḥ = afflictions (m. nom. pl.)		 The afflictions are ignorance and the rest. Краткая отсылка к пяти клешам: avidyā, asmitā, rāga, dveṣa, abhiniveśa (подробнее в YS 2.3). Первый элемент четырёхзвенной цепи. The afflictions are ignorance and the rest.
[2] kuśalākuśalāni karmāṇi. kuśala-akuśalāni = meritorious and demeritorious (n. nom. pl., dvandva: kuśala = skilful, meritorious; akuśala = unskilful, demeritorious)
karmāṇi = actions, karmas (n. nom. pl.)		 Karmas are [both] meritorious and demeritorious. Второй элемент: действия обоих видов. Важно: даже kuśala (благие) кармы — часть цепи, ведущей к связанности. Karmas are [both] meritorious and demeritorious.
[3] tatphalaṃ vipākaḥ. tat-phalam = the fruit of that (n. nom. sg.; tat = that [karma]; phala = fruit)
vipākaḥ = ripening, maturation (m. nom. sg.; vi + √pac — "cooking through")		 The ripening is the fruit of that [karma]. Третий элемент: кармический плод, созревание действий. The ripening is the fruit of that [karma].
[4] tadanuguṇā vāsanā āśayāḥ. tat-anuguṇāḥ = conforming to that, following the quality of that (m. nom. pl.; tat = that; anuguṇa = following the quality of, corresponding to, from anu + guṇa)
vāsanāḥ = subliminal tendencies, perfuming imprints (f. nom. pl.; from √vas — "to dwell, to perfume" — the residual "fragrance" of past experience that lingers in the mind)
āśayāḥ = latent deposits, repositories (m. nom. pl.; ā + √śī — "resting places," the deep-seated storehouses)		 The latent deposits are the subliminal tendencies that conform to that [karma]. Четвёртый элемент: vāsanā (от √vas, "обитать, придавать аромат") = āśaya (от ā + √śī, "хранилище"). Это самый глубокий слой — подсознательные склонности, которые "пахнут" прошлым опытом и подталкивают к новым клешам. Замыкание круга: āśaya → kleśa → karma → vipāka → āśaya. The latent deposits are the subliminal tendencies that conform to that [karma].
[5] te ca manasi vartamānāḥ puruṣe vyapadiśyante, sa hi tatphalasya bhokteti. te = these (m. nom. pl.)
ca = and (indecl.)
manasi = in the mind (n. loc. sg.)
vartamānāḥ = existing, being present (m. nom. pl., PPA √vṛt)
puruṣe = in/to puruṣa (m. loc. sg.)
vyapadiśyante = are figuratively designated, are attributed by way of indication (3rd pl. pres. pass. vi + apa + √diś — "to point out indirectly, to designate figuratively")
saḥ = he (m. nom. sg.)
hi = for (indecl.)
tat-phalasya = of their fruit (n. gen. sg.)
bhoktā = the experiencer, the enjoyer (m. nom. sg., agent noun from √bhuj)
iti = thus (indecl.)		 And these, though existing in the mind, are figuratively attributed to puruṣa — for he is the experiencer of their fruit. Важнейшая эпистемологическая оговорка. Клеши, карма и прочее vartamāna (присутствуют) в manas (уме), а не в пуруше. Но они vyapadiśyante (фигурально приписываются) пуруше, поскольку пуруша — bhoktā ("вкушатель") их плодов. Глагол vyapadiśyante (vi + apa + √diś) — технический термин: "непрямое указание," "перенос обозначения." Это не реальное обладание, а условная атрибуция. And these, though existing in the mind, are figuratively attributed to puruṣa — for he is the experiencer of their fruit.
[6] yathā jayaḥ parājayo vā yoddhṛṣu vartamānaḥ svāmini vyapadiśyate. yathā = just as (indecl.)
jayaḥ = victory (m. nom. sg., from √ji)
parājayaḥ = defeat (m. nom. sg.; parā + √ji — "being conquered")
vā = or (indecl.)
yoddhṛṣu = among the warriors (m. loc. pl.; yoddhṛ = warrior, fighter, agent noun from √yudh)
vartamānaḥ = existing (m. nom. sg., PPA √vṛt)
svāmini = in/to the lord, the king (m. loc. sg.; svāmin = owner, master, lord)
vyapadiśyate = is figuratively attributed (3rd sg. pres. pass.)		 Just as victory or defeat, though existing among the warriors, is figuratively attributed to the king. Блестящая аналогия. Победа или поражение происходят среди воинов (yoddhṛṣu), но приписываются царю (svāmin). Царь не сражается лично — но мы говорим "царь победил" или "царь проиграл." Точно так же клеши "сражаются" в уме, но мы говорим "пуруша страдает." Это vyapadeśa — фигуральное обозначение, перенос. Just as victory or defeat, though existing among the warriors, is figuratively attributed to the king.
[7] yo hy anena bhogenāparāmṛṣṭaḥ sa puruṣaviśeṣa īśvaraḥ. yaḥ = who (m. nom. sg., rel. pron.)
hi = indeed (indecl.)
anena = by this (m. inst. sg.)
bhogena = by experience, by enjoyment/suffering (m. inst. sg.; bhoga = experience, from √bhuj)
aparāmṛṣṭaḥ = untouched (m. nom. sg.)
saḥ = he (m. nom. sg.)
puruṣa-viśeṣaḥ = the special puruṣa (m. nom. sg.)
īśvaraḥ = Īśvara (m. nom. sg.)		 He who is untouched by this experience — he is the special puruṣa, Īśvara.
